papers.title: FePySR: A Neural Feature Extraction Framework for Efficient and Scalable Symbolic Regression
papers.summary: A fundamental challenge in symbolic regression (SR) is efficiently recovering complex mathematical expressions from observational data. Although this problem is NP-hard, many expressions of practical interest decompose naturally into combinations of nonlinear feature modules, concentrating structural complexity into a small number of reusable components. Here, we introduce FePySR, a two-stage framework that reduces the SR search space by extracting valid features prior to equation search. FePySR first employs a heterogeneous neural network to constrain observational data to a set of candidate expressions, then performs structural optimization within this refined expression space using PySR. Across five standard benchmarks, FePySR outperforms state-of-the-art methods by achieving higher equation recovery rates. On a set of 75 highly complex synthesized equations, FePySR recovers 36 equations, while producing substantially smaller mean squared errors on the remaining unrecovered cases, with reduced computation time compared to PySR. FePySR's first stage also maintains consistent performance under varying numbers of selected top features and increasing levels of noise in the observational data. Applied to ordinary differential equations governing biological systems, FePySR successfully identifies governing equations in 24 out of 100 tests where PySR recovers none. Taken together, FePySR is a generalizable framework that can enhance the SR solvers, enabling the efficient and reliable recovery of symbolic expressions across scientific domains.

ai.tldr: 提出FePySR框架，通过两阶段方法（神经网络特征提取+结构优化）提升符号回归的效率和可扩展性，在多个基准上优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 符号回归（SR）从观测数据恢复复杂数学表达式是NP难问题，但许多实际表达式可分解为可重用非线性特征模块，现有方法搜索空间大、效率低。
ai.method: 第一阶段使用异构神经网络将观测数据约束到候选表达式集，第二阶段利用PySR在精简表达式空间中进行结构优化。
ai.result: 在五个标准基准上方程恢复率更高；在75个复杂合成方程中恢复36个，未恢复案例均方误差更小且计算时间更短；在生物系统常微分方程测试中恢复24个（PySR为0）。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从数据中高效、可靠地发现符号表达式，适用于科学发现和模型解释。

idea_spark.idea: 将异构神经网络与现有SR求解器结合，先缩小搜索空间再精细优化。
idea_spark.risk: 第一阶段特征提取可能遗漏有效表达式，对噪声敏感度需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用问题结构（可分解性）设计混合方法，平衡搜索效率与准确性。

en.tldr: FePySR is a two-stage framework that uses neural feature extraction to reduce the search space for symbolic regression, achieving higher recovery rates and lower errors across benchmarks.
en.motivation: Symbolic regression (SR) is NP-hard, but many practical expressions decompose into reusable nonlinear feature modules; existing methods suffer from large search spaces and inefficiency.
en.method: First stage: a heterogeneous neural network constrains observational data to a set of candidate expressions. Second stage: structural optimization within this refined space using PySR.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art on five benchmarks; recovers 36 of 75 complex synthetic equations with smaller MSE and less computation; recovers 24 of 100 biological ODE tests where PySR recovers none.
en.help_to_user: Enables efficient and reliable discovery of symbolic expressions from data, aiding scientific modeling and interpretability.

idea_spark.idea: Combine heterogeneous neural networks with existing SR solvers to first narrow the search space then refine.
idea_spark.risk: The feature extraction stage may miss valid expressions; sensitivity to noise needs further investigation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage problem structure (decomposability) to design hybrid methods balancing search efficiency and accuracy.

papers.title: UFO: A Domain-Unification-Free Operator Framework for Generalized Operator Learning
papers.summary: Neural operators have become an effective framework for learning mappings between function spaces, yet most existing architectures realize operators within a single representational domain, such as physical, spectral, or latent space. In this work, we introduce UFO (Domain-Unification-Free Operator), a cross-domain neural operator framework that realizes operators through adaptive, jointly conditioned interactions among representations defined on distinct domains. UFO enables discretization decoupling: the input function can be observed at resolutions or locations different from those used during training, while the solution can be queried at arbitrary output resolutions. Across four complementary benchmarks covering discontinuous inputs, irregular sampling with spectral mismatch, nonlinear dynamics, and stochastic high-frequency fields, UFO delivers accurate, robust, and physically coherent predictions under distribution shifts. These results establish cross-domain, phase-modulated realization as a powerful framework for discretization-decoupled neural operator learning.

ai.tldr: 提出UFO框架，无需统一域即可实现跨域神经算子学习，支持离散化解耦。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子局限于单一表示域（物理、谱或隐空间），难以处理跨域分布偏移和离散化差异。
ai.method: 通过自适应联合条件交互，在不同域表示间实现算子学习，无需域统一。
ai.result: 在四个基准测试（不连续输入、不规则采样、非线性动力学、随机高频场）中，UFO在分布偏移下保持准确、鲁棒且物理一致的预测。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户学习如何设计跨域、离散化解耦的神经算子，适用于多分辨率观测和任意查询。

idea_spark.idea: 将不同域表示视为独立模态，通过注意力或门控机制动态融合，实现自适应算子学习。
idea_spark.risk: 跨域交互可能增加计算复杂度，且相位调制对高频噪声敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多模态学习启发，将函数空间映射视为多视图表示融合问题。

en.tldr: UFO is a cross-domain neural operator framework that eliminates the need for domain unification and enables discretization decoupling.
en.motivation: Existing neural operators are confined to a single representational domain, limiting their ability to handle cross-domain distribution shifts and discretization mismatches.
en.method: UFO realizes operators via adaptive, jointly conditioned interactions among representations defined on distinct domains, without unifying them.
en.result: Across four benchmarks (discontinuous inputs, irregular sampling, nonlinear dynamics, stochastic high-frequency fields), UFO delivers accurate, robust, and physically coherent predictions under distribution shifts.
en.help_to_user: Helps users learn to design cross-domain, discretization-decoupled neural operators suitable for multi-resolution observations and arbitrary queries.

idea_spark.idea: Treat representations from different domains as independent modalities and fuse them adaptively via attention or gating mechanisms for operator learning.
idea_spark.risk: Cross-domain interactions may increase computational cost, and phase modulation could be sensitive to high-frequency noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multimodal learning, viewing function space mapping as a multi-view representation fusion problem.

papers.title: U-HNO: A U-shaped Hybrid Neural Operator with Sparse-Point Adaptive Routing for Non-stationary PDE Dynamics
papers.summary: Solutions to many partial differential equations (PDEs) display coexisting smooth global transport and localized sharp features within a single trajectory: shock fronts, thin interfaces, and concentrated high-frequency content sit on top of slowly varying backgrounds. This poses a challenge for neural operators: Fourier-based architectures mix nonlocal interactions efficiently but tend to under-resolve localized non-smooth features, whereas spatially local architectures recover fine detail at the cost of long-range propagation and rollout stability. Existing hybrid operators paper over this tension with a fixed, spatially uniform fusion that forces the same trade-off everywhere.   We propose U-HNO, a U-shaped hybrid neural operator whose central design is Sparse-Point Adaptive Routing (SPAR): at every spatial location, a per-pixel hard mask selects whether the global Fourier branch or the local multi-scale Gaussian branch should dominate, and the sparsity ratio is a function of the local contrast of the routing signal, so smooth and shock-aligned regions receive different mixtures of global and local computation. SPAR is embedded in a hierarchical encoder-bottleneck-decoder backbone with skip connections so that the dual branches and the gate operate at every resolution. Training combines pointwise supervision with a finite-difference H^1 gradient term and a band-wise spectral consistency regularizer.   Across benchmarks spanning 1D Burgers, Kuramoto-Sivashinsky, KdV, 2D advection, Allen-Cahn, Navier-Stokes, Darcy flow, and 3D transonic compressible Navier-Stokes from PDEBench, U-HNO achieves state-of-the-art rollout accuracy on the majority of tasks in both relative L^2 and H^1 metrics, with the largest gains on problems dominated by sharp localized features. Ablations show that removing any single component substantially degrades rollout error.

ai.tldr: 提出U-HNO，一种U形混合神经算子，通过稀疏点自适应路由（SPAR）在全局傅里叶分支和局部多尺度高斯分支间动态选择，有效处理非平稳PDE中的光滑与局部尖锐特征共存问题。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子在处理同时包含光滑全局传输和局部尖锐特征（如激波、薄界面）的PDE解时，傅里叶架构难以解析局部非光滑特征，而局部架构则牺牲了长程传播和滚动稳定性。现有混合算子采用固定均匀融合，无法适应不同区域的需求。
ai.method: 设计U形编码器-瓶颈-解码器骨干网络，嵌入稀疏点自适应路由（SPAR）：每个空间位置通过逐像素硬掩码选择全局傅里叶分支或局部多尺度高斯分支的支配程度，稀疏比由路由信号的局部对比度决定。训练结合逐点监督、有限差分H^1梯度项和频带谱一致性正则化。
ai.result: 在1D Burgers、Kuramoto-Sivashinsky、KdV、2D平流、Allen-Cahn、Navier-Stokes、Darcy流及3D跨声速可压缩Navier-Stokes等基准测试中，U-HNO在多数任务上达到最先进的滚动精度（相对L^2和H^1指标），尤其在局部尖锐特征主导的问题上提升最大。消融实验表明移除任一组件会显著降低滚动误差。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何设计混合神经算子以同时处理PDE解中的光滑和局部尖锐特征，提供自适应路由和分层融合的实用方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将SPAR扩展到三维时空域，或结合注意力机制实现更细粒度的路由决策，以处理更复杂的非线性PDE系统。
idea_spark.risk: 硬掩码可能导致梯度不连续，影响训练稳定性；稀疏比依赖局部对比度，可能对噪声敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受图像分割中U-Net和动态网络设计的启发，将硬路由与多尺度特征融合结合，实现空间自适应计算。

en.tldr: Proposes U-HNO, a U-shaped hybrid neural operator with Sparse-Point Adaptive Routing (SPAR) that dynamically selects between global Fourier and local multi-scale Gaussian branches, effectively handling coexisting smooth transport and localized sharp features in non-stationary PDE solutions.
en.motivation: Existing neural operators struggle with PDE solutions that simultaneously exhibit smooth global transport and localized sharp features (e.g., shock fronts, thin interfaces). Fourier-based architectures under-resolve local non-smooth features, while local architectures sacrifice long-range propagation and rollout stability. Current hybrid operators use fixed uniform fusion, unable to adapt to different regions.
en.method: Designs a U-shaped encoder-bottleneck-decoder backbone with Sparse-Point Adaptive Routing (SPAR): a per-pixel hard mask selects the dominance of the global Fourier branch or the local multi-scale Gaussian branch at each spatial location, with the sparsity ratio determined by the local contrast of the routing signal. Training combines pointwise supervision, a finite-difference H^1 gradient term, and a band-wise spectral consistency regularizer.
en.result: Across benchmarks including 1D Burgers, Kuramoto-Sivashinsky, KdV, 2D advection, Allen-Cahn, Navier-Stokes, Darcy flow, and 3D transonic compressible Navier-Stokes from PDEBench, U-HNO achieves state-of-the-art rollout accuracy on most tasks in both relative L^2 and H^1 metrics, with largest gains on problems dominated by sharp localized features. Ablations show that removing any single component substantially degrades rollout error.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to design hybrid neural operators that simultaneously handle smooth and sharp features in PDE solutions, providing practical methods for adaptive routing and hierarchical fusion.

idea_spark.idea: Extend SPAR to 3D spatiotemporal domains, or incorporate attention mechanisms for finer-grained routing decisions, to handle more complex nonlinear PDE systems.
idea_spark.risk: Hard masks may cause gradient discontinuities, affecting training stability; sparsity ratio depends on local contrast, which may be sensitive to noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by U-Net in image segmentation and dynamic network design, combining hard routing with multi-scale feature fusion for spatially adaptive computation.

papers.title: Topology-Preserving Neural Operator Learning via Hodge Decomposition
papers.summary: In this paper, we study solution operators of physical field equations on geometric meshes from a function-space perspective. We reveal that Hodge orthogonality fundamentally resolves spectral interference by isolating unlearnable topological degrees of freedom from learnable geometric dynamics, enabling an additive approximation confined to structure-preserving subspaces. Building on Hodge theory and operator splitting, we derive a principled operator-level decomposition. The result is a Hybrid Eulerian-Lagrangian architecture with an algebraic-level inductive bias we call Hodge Spectral Duality (HSD). In our framework, we use discrete differential forms to capture topology-dominated components and an orthogonal auxiliary ambient space to represent complex local dynamics. Our method achieves superior accuracy and efficiency on geometric graphs with enhanced fidelity to physical invariants. Our code is available at https://github.com/ContinuumCoder/Hodge-Spectral-Duality

ai.tldr: 本文从函数空间视角研究几何网格上物理场方程的解算子，利用Hodge正交性分离不可学习的拓扑自由度与可学习的几何动力学，提出混合欧拉-拉格朗日架构Hodge Spectral Duality (HSD)，在几何图上实现高精度、高效率且保物理不变量的算子学习。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子方法在几何网格上常受频谱干扰，难以同时捕获拓扑主导的全局结构与复杂局部动力学。
ai.method: 基于Hodge理论与算子分裂，推导算子级分解；采用离散微分形式表示拓扑分量，并用正交辅助环境表示局部动力学，形成混合欧拉-拉格朗日架构。
ai.result: 在几何图上，HSD方法在精度、效率及物理不变量保真度上均优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 若您研究几何网格上的物理场模拟或算子学习，本方法提供了保拓扑的分解框架，可提升模型对物理不变量的遵守程度。

idea_spark.idea: 将Hodge正交性作为归纳偏置引入图神经网络，设计显式拓扑保持层。
idea_spark.risk: 离散微分形式的实现复杂度较高，可能增加模型训练与推理的计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Hodge理论中正交分解的启发，联想到在物理信息神经网络中显式分离可学习与不可学习自由度。

idea_spark.idea: 将Hodge正交性作为归纳偏置引入图神经网络，设计显式拓扑保持层。
idea_spark.risk: 离散微分形式的实现复杂度较高，可能增加模型训练与推理的计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Hodge理论中正交分解的启发，联想到在物理信息神经网络中显式分离可学习与不可学习自由度。

en.tldr: This paper studies solution operators of physical field equations on geometric meshes from a function-space perspective, using Hodge orthogonality to separate unlearnable topological degrees of freedom from learnable geometric dynamics, and proposes a Hybrid Eulerian-Lagrangian architecture called Hodge Spectral Duality (HSD) that achieves superior accuracy, efficiency, and fidelity to physical invariants on geometric graphs.
en.motivation: Existing neural operator methods on geometric meshes suffer from spectral interference and struggle to simultaneously capture topology-dominated global structures and complex local dynamics.
en.method: Based on Hodge theory and operator splitting, an operator-level decomposition is derived; discrete differential forms represent topological components, and an orthogonal auxiliary ambient space represents local dynamics, forming a Hybrid Eulerian-Lagrangian architecture.
en.result: On geometric graphs, HSD outperforms existing methods in accuracy, efficiency, and adherence to physical invariants.
en.help_to_user: If you study physical field simulation or operator learning on geometric meshes, this method provides a topology-preserving decomposition framework that improves model fidelity to physical invariants.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce Hodge orthogonality as an inductive bias into graph neural networks by designing explicit topology-preserving layers.
idea_spark.risk: The implementation complexity of discrete differential forms may increase computational overhead during training and inference.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Hodge theory's orthogonal decomposition, the idea of explicitly separating learnable and unlearnable degrees of freedom in physics-informed neural networks emerged.

papers.title: Frequency Bias and OOD Generalization in Neural Operators under a Variable-Coefficient Wave Equation
papers.summary: Neural operators learn to map initial conditions to the terminal solution of partial differential equations (PDEs), providing a surrogate for the full operator mapping. This enables rapid prediction across different input configurations. While recent neural operator architectures have demonstrated strong performance on diverse PDE tasks, their behavior under structured distribution shifts remains insufficiently understood. To investigate this, we study operator learning in a wave propagation setting governed by a one-dimensional variable-coefficient wave equation, using two representative architectures, the Fourier Neural Operator (FNO) and the Deep Operator Network (DeepONet). To examine their generalization under distribution shifts, we consider structured out-of-distribution (OOD) settings that independently vary input frequency and coefficient smoothness. The results show that under smoothness shifts, both models maintain stable performance, with FNO achieving lower error. In contrast, under frequency shifts, FNO exhibits a sharp increase in error under unseen high-frequency inputs, whereas DeepONet shows milder degradation despite higher overall error. Our analysis reveals that these differences arise from how each architecture represents and responds to variations in frequency structure. Together, these findings highlight a fundamental gap between strong in-distribution performance and generalization under distribution shifts in operator learning, underscoring the role of architectural representation bias in developing more reliable neural operators for physics-based PDE simulations beyond the training distribution.

ai.tldr: 研究神经算子在变系数波动方程下的频率偏差和分布外泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 神经算子在PDE任务中表现优异，但在结构化分布偏移下的行为尚不明确。
ai.method: 使用FNO和DeepONet两种架构，在变系数波动方程中独立改变输入频率和系数平滑度进行实验。
ai.result: 平滑度偏移下两者性能稳定，FNO误差更低；频率偏移下FNO对高频输入误差剧增，DeepONet退化较缓。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解神经算子架构的表示偏差如何影响分布外泛化。

idea_spark.idea: 设计频率自适应机制或混合架构以缓解高频泛化失败。
idea_spark.risk: 自适应机制可能增加计算复杂度或过拟合训练分布。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信号处理中借鉴多尺度分解或频谱正则化方法。

en.tldr: Investigates frequency bias and out-of-distribution generalization of neural operators under a variable-coefficient wave equation.
en.motivation: Neural operators perform well on PDE tasks, but their behavior under structured distribution shifts is not well understood.
en.method: Experiments with FNO and DeepONet on a 1D variable-coefficient wave equation, independently varying input frequency and coefficient smoothness.
en.result: Both models are stable under smoothness shifts, with FNO achieving lower error; under frequency shifts, FNO shows sharp error increase for high-frequency inputs, while DeepONet degrades more mildly.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how architectural representation bias affects out-of-distribution generalization in neural operators.

idea_spark.idea: Design frequency-adaptive mechanisms or hybrid architectures to mitigate high-frequency generalization failures.
idea_spark.risk: Adaptive mechanisms may increase computational cost or overfit to training distribution.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow multi-scale decomposition or spectral regularization methods from signal processing.

papers.title: Coreset-Induced Conditional Velocity Flow Matching
papers.summary: We propose Coreset-Induced Conditional Velocity Flow Matching (CCVFM), a generative model that augments hierarchical rectified flow with a data-informed source distribution. Hierarchical flow matching models the full conditional velocity law in velocity space, but its inner flow is asked to transport isotropic Gaussian noise to a multimodal target velocity distribution from scratch. Our key observation is that this inner source can be replaced by a closed-form surrogate built from a coreset of the target. CCVFM first compresses the target into weighted atoms using an entropic Sinkhorn coreset and lifts them to a Gaussian mixture. The induced conditional velocity law is then a closed-form Gaussian mixture that can be sampled without a learned neural sampler. A lightweight correction flow, trained from this exact surrogate source, then refines the remaining surrogate-to-target residual rather than learning an entire noise-to-data map. We prove that the surrogate transport cost equals the target--surrogate Wasserstein gap under an explicit compression assumption, whereas the noise-source analogue has a dimension-scale lower bound. We further characterize the conditional second moment of the direct surrogate-source training target and show that its source-dependent excess is small when the surrogate conditional law is close to the true conditional velocity law in mean and covariance. Empirically, on MNIST, CIFAR-10, ImageNet-32, and CelebA-HQ, the proposed method reaches competitive few-step generation under matched architectures.

ai.tldr: 提出CCVFM，用数据驱动的核心集替代高斯噪声作为源分布，加速层次化整流流生成。
ai.motivation: 层次化整流流的内流需从高斯噪声学习完整映射，效率低；核心集可提供更优的闭合形式源分布。
ai.method: 用熵Sinkhorn核心集压缩目标为加权原子，提升为高斯混合；以此构造条件速度律的闭合形式，再训练轻量校正流修正残差。
ai.result: 在MNIST、CIFAR-10、ImageNet-32、CelebA-HQ上，以匹配架构达到有竞争力的少步生成。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种用核心集初始化流匹配、减少训练负担的生成建模思路。

idea_spark.idea: 用核心集构造闭合形式条件速度律，替代神经网络采样器。
idea_spark.risk: 核心集质量影响最终生成效果，对高维复杂数据可能压缩不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将最优传输中的核心集思想引入生成模型，实现从噪声到数据的更高效路径。

en.tldr: CCVFM replaces isotropic Gaussian noise with a data-informed coreset surrogate as the source distribution for hierarchical rectified flow, achieving competitive few-step generation.
en.motivation: The inner flow in hierarchical rectified flow must learn the full noise-to-data map from scratch, which is inefficient; a coreset-based surrogate provides a closed-form source distribution closer to the target.
en.method: Compress the target into weighted atoms via entropic Sinkhorn coreset, lift to a Gaussian mixture, derive closed-form conditional velocity law, then train a lightweight correction flow on the surrogate-to-target residual.
en.result: On MNIST, CIFAR-10, ImageNet-32, and CelebA-HQ, the method achieves competitive few-step generation under matched architectures.
en.help_to_user: Offers a way to initialize flow matching with a coreset surrogate, reducing training burden in generative modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Use a coreset to construct a closed-form conditional velocity law, eliminating the need for a learned neural sampler.
idea_spark.risk: Coreset quality may degrade for high-dimensional complex data, limiting compression fidelity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow coreset ideas from optimal transport to design more efficient paths from noise to data in generative models.

papers.title: Path-independent Flow Matching for Multi-parameter Generative Dynamics
papers.summary: Flow Matching is a powerful framework for learning transport maps between probability distributions. Yet its standard single-parameter formulation is not designed to capture multi-parameter variations where the resulting transport should be path-independent. Path independence is crucial because it ensures that transformations depend only on the initial and target distributions, not on the specific path. In this work, we introduce Path-independent Flow Matching (PiFM), a method for learning vector fields whose induced flows yield path-independent transport between distributions. We show that PiFM generalizes Flow Matching to higher-dimensional parameter domains while enforcing structural conditions that ensure consistency of composed transformations. In addition, we show that, under suitable assumptions, PiFM approximates the Wasserstein barycenter, linking the framework to a notion of distributional interpolation. To enable practical training, we propose a tractable, simulation-free objective that regresses onto multi-parameter conditional probability paths. We showcase empirically that PiFM outperforms other approaches on both synthetic and real world data in interpolating path-independent trajectories and generating desired out of distribution samples.

ai.tldr: 提出路径无关流匹配（PiFM），将流匹配推广到多参数域，实现路径无关的分布变换，并近似Wasserstein重心。
ai.motivation: 标准流匹配仅支持单参数，无法处理多参数变化中路径无关的传输需求。
ai.method: 通过强制结构条件学习向量场，使诱导流在多参数域中路径无关，并设计可训练的模拟自由目标函数。
ai.result: 在合成和真实数据上，PiFM在路径无关轨迹插值和分布外样本生成上优于其他方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种新的生成框架，适用于需要路径无关变换的多参数动态系统建模。

idea_spark.idea: 将路径无关性约束与Wasserstein重心结合，可能用于多模态数据融合或公平性生成。
idea_spark.risk: 高维参数域中结构条件的计算复杂度可能成为瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受最优传输理论中路径无关性的启发，将流匹配扩展至多参数场景。

en.tldr: Proposes Path-independent Flow Matching (PiFM), extending flow matching to multi-parameter domains for path-independent distribution transport and approximating Wasserstein barycenters.
en.motivation: Standard flow matching handles only a single parameter and cannot capture path-independent transport required for multi-parameter variations.
en.method: Learns vector fields with structural constraints to ensure path-independence in multi-parameter domains, and designs a tractable simulation-free objective.
en.result: PiFM outperforms other methods on synthetic and real data for path-independent trajectory interpolation and out-of-distribution sample generation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new generative framework suitable for modeling multi-parameter dynamical systems requiring path-independent transformations.

idea_spark.idea: Combining path-independence constraints with Wasserstein barycenters may enable multimodal data fusion or fair generation.
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity of structural constraints in high-dimensional parameter spaces could be a bottleneck.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by path independence in optimal transport theory, extending flow matching to multi-parameter scenarios.

papers.title: A new proof of the transfer of regularity for kinetic equations
papers.summary: We present a new trajectory-based approach to transfer-of-regularity estimates à la Bouchut-Hörmander for kinetic equations at the weak scale of local diffusion. The method avoids explicit computations in Fourier variables and does not rely on the fundamental solution, while still yielding sharp, scale-invariant homogeneous estimates.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种新的基于轨迹的方法，用于证明动力学方程在局部扩散弱尺度下的正则性传递估计。
ai.motivation: 避免使用傅里叶变量和基本解，简化正则性传递的证明。
ai.method: 采用轨迹方法，不依赖傅里叶变换或基本解，直接推导出尖锐的尺度不变齐次估计。
ai.result: 得到了与Bouchut-Hörmander类型相当的尺度不变齐次正则性传递估计。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种更简洁的证明思路，可用于理解或扩展动力学方程的正则性理论。

idea_spark.idea: 利用粒子轨迹的几何性质替代傅里叶分析，可能简化高维或非线性问题的估计。
idea_spark.risk: 轨迹方法在强非线性或非局部扩散情形下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理直观的轨迹思想转化为严格的数学估计，启发其他方程的正则性研究。

en.motivation: Avoid Fourier variables and fundamental solutions to simplify the proof of regularity transfer.
en.method: Trajectory-based approach without Fourier transform or fundamental solution, yielding sharp scale-invariant homogeneous estimates.
en.result: Sharp scale-invariant homogeneous estimates comparable to Bouchut-Hörmander type.
en.help_to_user: Provides a simpler proof strategy for understanding or extending regularity theory of kinetic equations.

idea_spark.idea: Use geometric properties of particle trajectories to replace Fourier analysis, potentially simplifying estimates in high-dimensional or nonlinear problems.
idea_spark.risk: May fail for strongly nonlinear or nonlocal diffusion cases.
idea_spark.inspiration: Translating physical intuition of trajectories into rigorous estimates inspires regularity studies for other equations.

papers.title: Discrete MeanFlow: One-Step Generation via Conditional Transition Kernels
papers.summary: MeanFlow enables one-step generation in continuous spaces by learning an average velocity over a time interval rather than the instantaneous velocity field of flow matching. However, discrete state spaces do not have smooth trajectories or spatial derivatives, so the continuous formulation does not directly apply. We introduce Discrete MeanFlow, which replaces the motion of a point with the transport of probability mass over finite states. Our key object is the conditional transition kernel of a continuous-time Markov chain (CTMC), from which we define a mean discrete rate that measures the average change in transition probability over a time interval. We prove a Discrete MeanFlow identity that relates this finite-interval rate to the instantaneous CTMC generator at the endpoint, with the Kolmogorov forward equation replacing the spatial chain rule of continuous MeanFlow. Based on this identity, we parameterize the transition kernel directly using a boundary-by-construction design that guarantees valid probability outputs and exact boundary conditions without auxiliary losses. Since the learned kernel is itself a probability distribution, generation reduces to a single forward pass followed by one categorical draw meaning no iterative denoising, ODE integration, or multi-step refinement is required. We validate the framework on exact finite-state Markov chains, where the learned kernel recovers the analytical ground truth to high precision, and on factorized synthetic sequence generation tasks with varying alphabet sizes and sequence lengths.

ai.tldr: 提出离散均值流，通过条件转移核实现离散空间的一步生成，无需迭代去噪。
ai.motivation: 连续空间中的均值流方法无法直接应用于离散状态空间，因为离散空间缺乏平滑轨迹和空间导数。
ai.method: 用连续时间马尔可夫链的条件转移核替代点的运动，定义平均离散率，并证明离散均值流恒等式，直接参数化转移核。
ai.result: 在有限状态马尔可夫链上精确恢复解析解，在合成序列生成任务中验证有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种离散空间一步生成的新框架，简化生成流程。

idea_spark.idea: 将连续均值流思想推广到离散空间，利用条件转移核实现一步采样。
idea_spark.risk: 对大规模离散空间的计算复杂度可能较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续均值流与马尔可夫链的类比中获得启发。

en.tldr: Proposes Discrete MeanFlow, achieving one-step generation in discrete spaces via conditional transition kernels, eliminating iterative denoising.
en.motivation: Continuous MeanFlow cannot be directly applied to discrete state spaces due to lack of smooth trajectories and spatial derivatives.
en.method: Replaces point motion with transport of probability mass using conditional transition kernels of continuous-time Markov chains, defines mean discrete rate, proves Discrete MeanFlow identity, and directly parameterizes the kernel.
en.result: Recovers analytical ground truth on finite-state Markov chains and validates on synthetic sequence generation tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new framework for one-step generation in discrete spaces, simplifying the generation process.

idea_spark.idea: Extends continuous MeanFlow to discrete spaces using conditional transition kernels for one-step sampling.
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity may be high for large discrete spaces.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the analogy between continuous MeanFlow and Markov chains.

papers.title: Bayesian Nonparametric Mixed-Effect ODEs with Gaussian Processes
papers.summary: Dynamical modelling is central to many scientific domains, including pharmacometrics, systems biology, physiology, and epidemiology. In these settings, heterogeneity is often intrinsic: different subjects or units follow related but distinct continuous-time dynamics. Classical nonlinear mixed-effects Ordinary Differential Equation (ODE) models address this by combining population-level structure with subject-specific effects, but they rely on a parametric vector field and are therefore vulnerable to structural misspecification and unmodelled mechanisms. This motivates nonparametric approaches that can retain principled uncertainty quantification, yet existing nonparametric ODE methods typically assume a single shared dynamical system rather than an explicit mixed-effect hierarchy over subject-specific dynamics. We propose MEGPODE, a Bayesian nonparametric mixed-effect ODE model in which each subject's vector field is decomposed into a shared population component and a subject-specific deviation, both endowed with Gaussian process (GP) priors. To avoid repeated ODE solves per subject during training, we combine state-space GP trajectory priors with virtual collocation observations, yielding Kalman-smoothing trajectory updates and closed-form regressions for the vector fields. Across controlled heterogeneous ODE benchmarks spanning oscillatory, biomedical systems, MEGPODE improves population-field recovery and subject-level trajectory prediction relative to strong baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出MEGPODE，一种贝叶斯非参数混合效应ODE模型，利用高斯过程分解群体和个体动态，避免参数化假设。
ai.motivation: 经典非线性混合效应ODE模型依赖参数化向量场，易受结构错误设定影响，且现有非参数ODE方法缺乏混合效应层次。
ai.method: 将每个受试者的向量场分解为共享群体成分和个体偏差，均赋予高斯过程先验；结合状态空间GP轨迹先验与虚拟搭配观测，实现卡尔曼平滑轨迹更新和向量场闭式回归。
ai.result: 在振荡和生物医学等异构ODE基准上，MEGPODE在群体场恢复和个体轨迹预测方面优于强基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户建模具有个体异质性的连续时间动态系统，提供不确定性量化且无需预设参数形式。

idea_spark.idea: 将高斯过程混合效应与状态空间ODE求解结合，避免逐受试者重复ODE求解。
idea_spark.risk: 高斯过程先验的尺度参数选择可能影响模型性能；虚拟观测点的密度需谨慎设定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经典非线性混合效应模型与非参数贝叶斯方法融合的启发，旨在平衡灵活性与计算效率。

en.tldr: Proposes MEGPODE, a Bayesian nonparametric mixed-effect ODE model that uses Gaussian processes to decompose population and individual dynamics, avoiding parametric assumptions.
en.motivation: Classical nonlinear mixed-effect ODE models rely on parametric vector fields, making them vulnerable to structural misspecification, and existing nonparametric ODE methods lack a mixed-effect hierarchy.
en.method: Decomposes each subject's vector field into a shared population component and a subject-specific deviation, both with GP priors; combines state-space GP trajectory priors with virtual collocation observations for Kalman-smoothing trajectory updates and closed-form regressions.
en.result: On heterogeneous ODE benchmarks including oscillatory and biomedical systems, MEGPODE improves population-field recovery and subject-level trajectory prediction over strong baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users model continuous-time dynamical systems with individual heterogeneity, providing principled uncertainty quantification without parametric form assumptions.

idea_spark.idea: Combines Gaussian process mixed effects with state-space ODE solving to avoid repeated ODE solves per subject.
idea_spark.risk: Choice of GP prior length scales may affect performance; density of virtual collocation points needs careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the fusion of classical nonlinear mixed-effects models and nonparametric Bayesian methods, aiming to balance flexibility and computational efficiency.

papers.title: Ergodic Trajectory Design by Learned Pushforward Maps: Provable Coverage via Conditional Flow Matching
papers.summary: Designing continuous trajectories whose time-averaged occupancy provably matches a prescribed spatial density (the \emph{ergodic coverage} problem) is central to UAV-assisted data collection and sensing, robotic exploration, and mobile monitoring. For flying agents in particular, this challenge is acute: trajectories must balance coverage fidelity against tight energy budgets, no-fly zones, and acceleration limits. Existing methods either re-optimize each trajectory online (with cost growing in the horizon and re-running for every target, agent, and realization) or rely on bespoke analytical constructions that must be re-derived for each new constraint. We propose a \emph{epushforward} framework that decouples ergodicity from density matching: an analytic latent trajectory provides exact uniform ergodicity on a simple annular domain, and a single map, learned offline by optimal-transport conditional flow matching, transports this latent occupancy onto the prescribed target density. The composed trajectory is then asymptotically ergodic with respect to the learned pushforward distribution, with deviation from the target controlled by the flow-matching training loss. Once trained for a given target density and constraint set, the map serves an unbounded number of trajectories and a multi-agent fleet without per-agent retraining, and many differentiable operational constraints (no-fly zones, acceleration ceilings, or fairness penalties) enter as additive soft penalties in the training loss without re-deriving the design. We prove three results (an acceleration-energy bound, an $O(1/\sqrt{K})$ ergodic convergence rate in the number of trajectory cycles $K$, and an approximation-error bound) that combine into an end-to-end coverage bound estimable from CFM training diagnostics (certified given an architectural Lipschitz bound on $v_θ$).

ai.tldr: 提出epushforward框架，通过条件流匹配学习推前映射，将解析潜轨迹的均匀遍历性迁移到目标密度上，实现可证明覆盖的无人机轨迹设计。
ai.motivation: 现有遍历轨迹方法要么在线重优化成本高，要么依赖需重新推导的解析构造，缺乏可扩展性。
ai.method: 设计解析潜轨迹提供均匀遍历性，用最优传输条件流匹配离线学习推前映射，将潜轨迹占用映射到目标密度，约束作为软惩罚加入训练损失。
ai.result: 证明了加速度-能量界、O(1/√K)遍历收敛率和近似误差界，组合成端到端覆盖界，可从CFM训练诊断估计。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可证明的、可扩展的无人机遍历轨迹设计方法，支持多智能体、多约束场景，无需每轨迹重优化。

en.tldr: Proposes epushforward framework that uses conditional flow matching to learn a pushforward map, transferring uniform ergodicity from an analytic latent trajectory to a target density, enabling provable coverage for UAV trajectory design.
en.motivation: Existing ergodic trajectory methods either re-optimize online with high cost or rely on bespoke analytical constructions that must be re-derived, lacking scalability.
en.method: Designs an analytic latent trajectory with exact uniform ergodicity, learns a pushforward map offline via optimal-transport conditional flow matching to transport latent occupancy to target density, with constraints as additive soft penalties in training loss.

idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by success of optimal transport and flow matching in generative modeling, applied to density transport in trajectory design.

papers.title: Hierarchical Transformer Preconditioning for Interactive Physics Simulation
papers.summary: Neural preconditioners for real-time physics simulation offer promising data-driven priors, but they often fail to capture long-range couplings efficiently because they inherit local message passing or sparse-operator access patterns. We introduce the Hierarchical Transformer Preconditioner, a neural preconditioner anchored to a weak-admissibility H-matrix partition. The partition provides a multiscale structural prior (dense diagonal leaves plus coarsening off-diagonal tiles) that enables full-graph approximate-inverse computation with O(N) scaling at fixed block sizes. The network models the inverse through low-rank far-field factors and uses highway connections (axial buffers plus a global summary token) to propagate context across transformer depth. At each PCG iteration, preconditioner application reduces to batched dense GEMMs with regular memory access.   The key training contribution is a cosine-Hutchinson probe objective that learns the action of MA on convergence-critical spectral subspaces, optimizing angular alignment of MAz with z rather than forcing eigenvalue clusters to a prescribed location. This removes unnecessary spectral-placement constraints from SAI-style objectives and improves conditioning on irregular spectra. Because both inference and apply are dense, dependency-free tensor programs, the full solve loop is captured as a single CUDA Graph.   On stiff multiphase Poisson systems (up to 100:1 density contrast, N = 1,024-16,384), the solver runs from ~143 to ~21 fps. At N = 8,192, it reaches 17.9 ms/frame, with 2.2x speedup over GPU Jacobi, ~28x over GPU IC/DILU (AMGX multicolor_dilu), and 2.7x over neural SPAI retrained per scale on the same benchmark.

ai.tldr: 提出分层Transformer预处理器，利用弱可容许H矩阵分区实现O(N)复杂度的全图近似逆计算，在刚性多相泊松系统上达到实时性能。
ai.motivation: 现有神经预处理器因局部消息传递或稀疏算子访问模式，难以高效捕获长程耦合。
ai.method: 基于弱可容许H矩阵分区构建多尺度结构先验，使用低秩远场因子和高速连接（轴向缓冲+全局摘要令牌）建模逆矩阵，并通过余弦-哈钦森探针目标优化谱对齐。
ai.result: 在N=8192时每帧17.9毫秒，比GPU Jacobi快2.2倍，比GPU IC/DILU快约28倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何设计高效神经预处理器以加速交互式物理仿真。

idea_spark.idea: 将分层Transformer预处理器与自适应网格细化结合，处理非均匀问题。
idea_spark.risk: 密集GEMM操作可能在大规模问题中遇到内存瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受H矩阵数值线性代数启发，将多尺度结构先验融入Transformer架构。

en.motivation: Existing neural preconditioners fail to capture long-range couplings efficiently due to local message passing or sparse-operator access patterns.

papers.title: scShapeBench: Discovering geometry from high dimensional scRNAseq data
papers.summary: High-dimensional point cloud data arise across many scientific domains, especially single-cell biology. The shapes or topologies of these datasets determine the types of information that can be extracted. For example, clustered data supports cell-type identification, trajectory structures support transition analysis, and archetypal structures capture continua of cellular behaviors. Existing analysis pipelines often assume a specific shape. The standard Seurat pipeline combines UMAP visualization with Louvain clustering and therefore assumes clustered data, while tools such as Monocle and SPADE assume tree-like structures, and flow-based models such as MIOFlow and Conditional Flow Matching target trajectories. Choosing which pipeline to apply is therefore often left to bioinformaticians who visually inspect datasets before selecting an analysis strategy. With the rise of agentic AI scientists, automating shape detection is increasingly important for selecting downstream analysis pipelines. To address this problem, we introduce scShapeBench, a benchmark dataset for shape detection containing both synthetic and expert-annotated single-cell datasets. Synthetic datasets are sampled from ground-truth skeleton graphs with controlled variance. Real single-cell datasets are curated from diverse sources and annotated by experts into four categories: clusters, single trajectory, multi-branching, and archetypal. We additionally introduce scReebTower, a baseline method that uses diffusion geometry to extract Reeb graphs and connect visualization with pipeline selection. We provide topology-aware evaluation metrics and compare scReebTower against PAGA and Mapper on synthetic and real data. Our results indicate that scReebTower outperforms existing baselines. Overall, our contributions span benchmarks, evaluation metrics, and a baseline for automated shape detection in single-cell data.

ai.tldr: 提出了scShapeBench基准数据集和scReebTower方法，用于自动检测单细胞RNA-seq数据中的几何形状（聚类、轨迹、多分支、原型），以指导下游分析流程的选择。
ai.motivation: 现有单细胞分析流程通常假设数据具有特定形状（如聚类或树状），但实际数据形状未知，手动选择流程依赖专家经验。随着AI科学家的发展，自动形状检测变得重要。
ai.method: 构建包含合成数据和专家标注真实数据的scShapeBench基准，提出基于扩散几何提取Reeb图的scReebTower方法，并设计拓扑感知评估指标。
ai.result: scReebTower在合成和真实数据上优于PAGA和Mapper等基线方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助生物信息学家或AI系统自动识别单细胞数据的几何结构，从而选择最合适的下游分析流程。

idea_spark.idea: 结合扩散几何与Reeb图，将可视化与流程选择连接，实现无监督形状分类。
idea_spark.risk: 真实数据标注主观性强，可能引入偏差；合成数据与真实数据分布差异可能影响泛化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受单细胞分析中形状假设问题的启发，将拓扑数据分析与自动化流程选择结合。

en.tldr: Proposes scShapeBench benchmark and scReebTower method for automatic shape detection (clusters, trajectories, multi-branching, archetypal) in single-cell RNA-seq data to guide downstream pipeline selection.
en.motivation: Existing single-cell analysis pipelines often assume a specific data shape (e.g., clusters or trees), but real data shapes are unknown, and manual selection relies on expert inspection. With the rise of AI scientists, automated shape detection becomes crucial.
en.method: Constructs scShapeBench with synthetic and expert-annotated real datasets, proposes scReebTower that extracts Reeb graphs via diffusion geometry, and designs topology-aware evaluation metrics.
en.result: scReebTower outperforms baselines like PAGA and Mapper on both synthetic and real data.
en.help_to_user: Helps bioinformaticians or AI systems automatically identify the geometric structure of single-cell data to select the most appropriate downstream analysis pipeline.

idea_spark.idea: Combine diffusion geometry with Reeb graphs to connect visualization and pipeline selection for unsupervised shape classification.
idea_spark.risk: Subjectivity in real data annotation may introduce bias; distribution mismatch between synthetic and real data could affect generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the problem of shape assumptions in single-cell analysis, integrating topological data analysis with automated pipeline selection.

papers.title: GeoFlowVLM: Geometry-Aware Joint Uncertainty for Frozen Vision-Language Embedding
papers.summary: Standard dual-encoder vision-language models that map images and text to deterministic points on a shared unit hypersphere through $\ell_2$ normalization typically expose neither \emph{aleatoric} uncertainty (cross-modal ambiguity) nor \emph{epistemic} uncertainty (lack of training-distribution support). Existing post-hoc methods either recover at most one of the two uncertainty components, or ignore the hyperspherical geometry of these models' embeddings. We propose \textbf{GeoFlowVLM} as a post-hoc adapter that learns the joint distribution of paired $\ell_2$-normalised dual-encoder VLM embeddings on the product hypersphere $\mathbb{S}^{d-1} \times \mathbb{S}^{d-1}$ via Riemannian flow matching with a single masked velocity field. A consistency result shows that, in the population limit, the trained network exposes the joint flow and both cross-modal conditional flows as valid Riemannian flow-matching velocity fields on their respective domains. We derive two quantities from this single model: a conditional retrieval entropy that quantifies aleatoric ambiguity with a decision-theoretic interpretation via a Fano-type bound, and a marginal-typicality epistemic score justified by an exact chain-rule decomposition of the joint NLL. This decomposition isolates a cross-modal pointwise-mutual-information term that is structurally discriminative rather than epistemic, and is empirically the only consistently uninformative standalone component. Empirically, the entropy tracks Recall@1 with near-ideal monotonic calibration across three retrieval benchmarks in both directions, and the marginal-typicality sum yields consistently calibrated selective accuracy across four zero-shot classification benchmarks.

ai.tldr: 提出GeoFlowVLM，通过黎曼流匹配学习双编码器VLM嵌入在乘积超球面上的联合分布，同时量化偶然不确定性和认知不确定性。
ai.motivation: 标准双编码器视觉语言模型将图像和文本映射到单位超球面上的确定点，无法表达偶然不确定性（跨模态歧义）和认知不确定性（训练分布外支持不足），现有后处理方法最多只能恢复其中一种不确定性，且忽略了嵌入的超球面几何。
ai.method: 设计后适配器GeoFlowVLM，在乘积超球面S^{d-1}×S^{d-1}上通过单掩码速度场的黎曼流匹配学习配对ℓ2归一化VLM嵌入的联合分布；从该模型导出条件检索熵（量化偶然不确定性）和边缘典型性认知分数（通过联合NLL的精确链式分解）。
ai.result: 在三个检索基准上，熵与Recall@1具有近乎理想的单调校准；在四个零样本分类基准上，边缘典型性总和产生一致校准的选择性准确率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解模型何时可能出错，通过不确定性分数实现更可靠的检索和分类决策。

idea_spark.idea: 利用黎曼流匹配在超球面乘积空间上建模联合分布，同时捕获两种不确定性，并通过链式分解分离出结构判别信息。
idea_spark.risk: 计算开销可能较高，且对超球面几何的依赖可能限制其在非归一化嵌入上的适用性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信息论和黎曼几何交叉视角出发，将不确定性分解与流匹配生成模型结合，启发更通用的不确定性量化框架。

idea_spark.inspiration: Cross-pollination of information theory and Riemannian geometry with flow matching generative models inspires a general framework for uncertainty quantification.

papers.title: A new selection problem for degenerate viscous Hamilton-Jacobi equations
papers.summary: We study a selection problem for degenerate viscous Hamilton--Jacobi equations with convex Hamiltonians, in which the approximation procedure combines a nonlinear discounted approximation with a small potential perturbation. A key question is how their simultaneous effects influence the asymptotic selection of viscosity solutions of the associated ergodic problem. Based on the nonlinear adjoint method, we establish the uniform convergence of the approximating solutions to a distinguished solution of the ergodic problem and derive a formula for the selected limit in terms of generalized Mather measures and the potential. As an application, we show that this selection principle is sufficiently flexible to realize any prescribed solution of the ergodic problem, with an explicit convergence rate.

ai.tldr: 研究退化粘性Hamilton-Jacobi方程中非线性折扣近似与小势能扰动共同作用下的选择问题，通过非线性伴随方法证明近似解一致收敛到遍历问题的特解，并给出极限公式。
ai.motivation: 理解退化粘性Hamilton-Jacobi方程中折扣近似和势能扰动如何联合影响解的渐近选择。
ai.method: 采用非线性伴随方法，结合广义Mather测度和势能，建立近似解到遍历问题特解的收敛性。
ai.result: 证明了近似解一致收敛到遍历问题的特解，并推导出极限的显式公式，且该选择原理能实现任意预设解，具有明确收敛速率。
ai.help_to_user: 为退化Hamilton-Jacobi方程的解选择问题提供理论框架和收敛性保证，可用于相关偏微分方程的渐近分析。

idea_spark.idea: 将折扣近似与势能扰动结合，可能为多尺度或随机动力系统提供新的选择机制。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖凸Hamiltonian假设，非凸情形可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Mather测度在动力系统中的应用启发，将测度论工具引入PDE选择问题。

en.tldr: Studies a selection problem for degenerate viscous Hamilton-Jacobi equations where nonlinear discounted approximation and small potential perturbation jointly influence the asymptotic selection of viscosity solutions, proving uniform convergence to a distinguished solution via the nonlinear adjoint method.
en.motivation: To understand how discounted approximation and potential perturbation together affect the asymptotic selection of solutions in degenerate viscous Hamilton-Jacobi equations.
en.method: Uses the nonlinear adjoint method combined with generalized Mather measures and potential to establish convergence of approximating solutions to a distinguished solution of the ergodic problem.
en.result: Proves uniform convergence of approximating solutions to a distinguished solution, derives an explicit formula for the selected limit, and shows the selection principle can realize any prescribed solution with an explicit convergence rate.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretical framework and convergence guarantees for solution selection in degenerate Hamilton-Jacobi equations, useful for asymptotic analysis of related PDEs.

idea_spark.idea: Combining discounted approximation with potential perturbation may offer new selection mechanisms for multiscale or stochastic dynamical systems.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on convex Hamiltonians and may fail in non-convex settings.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Mather measures in dynamical systems, bringing measure-theoretic tools into PDE selection problems.

papers.title: Rescaled Asynchronous SGD: Optimal Distributed Optimization under Data and System Heterogeneity
papers.summary: Asynchronous stochastic gradient descent (ASGD) is a standard way to exploit heterogeneous compute resources in distributed learning: instead of forcing fast workers to wait for slow ones, the server updates the model whenever a gradient arrives. Vanilla ASGD applies each arriving gradient with the same weight. When local data distributions are heterogeneous, this becomes problematic: faster workers contribute more updates, and we show theoretically that the method is biased toward a frequency-weighted average of the local objectives rather than the desired global objective. Existing remedies typically move away from the simple ASGD template by introducing gathering phases, buffering, or extra memory. We show that this is unnecessary. Keeping the standard ASGD mechanism, we recover the correct objective by rescaling worker-specific stepsizes in proportion to their computation times, so that each worker contributes the same aggregate learning rate over a cycle. In the non-convex setting, under smoothness and bounded heterogeneity assumptions, we prove that the resulting method, Rescaled ASGD, converges to stationary points of the correct global objective in the fixed-computation model. Its time complexity matches the known lower bound in the leading term, while the effects of staleness and data heterogeneity appear only in lower-order terms. Experiments confirm that the method converges to the correct objective and is competitive with state-of-the-art baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出Rescaled ASGD，通过按计算时间缩放步长，在异步SGD中消除数据异构导致的偏差，收敛到正确的全局目标。
ai.motivation: 标准异步SGD在数据异构时偏向频率加权平均，现有修复方法复杂，本文希望保持简单异步模板的同时恢复正确目标。
ai.method: 保持标准ASGD机制，按每个工作节点的计算时间缩放其步长，使各节点在一个周期内贡献相同的总学习率。
ai.result: 在非凸光滑和有界异构假设下，证明Rescaled ASGD收敛到正确全局目标的驻点，时间复杂度匹配下界，实验验证效果。
ai.help_to_user: 如果你在分布式训练中遇到数据异构或系统异构导致的模型偏差，本方法提供了一种简单有效的修正方案。

idea_spark.idea: 能否将这种按计算时间缩放步长的策略与梯度压缩或通信延迟补偿结合，进一步提升异构环境下的效率？
idea_spark.risk: 缩放因子依赖于准确的计算时间测量，在高度动态或噪声环境中可能不稳定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“每个工作节点贡献相同总学习率”的视角出发，重新审视异步SGD的偏差来源，启发更通用的异构优化设计。

en.tldr: Proposes Rescaled ASGD, which rescales worker-specific stepsizes proportionally to their computation times to eliminate bias from data heterogeneity in asynchronous SGD, converging to the correct global objective.
en.motivation: Standard ASGD is biased toward a frequency-weighted average under heterogeneous data; existing fixes are complex. This work aims to recover the correct objective while keeping the simple ASGD template.
en.method: Maintains the standard ASGD mechanism but rescales each worker's stepsize by its computation time, so that each worker contributes the same aggregate learning rate over a cycle.
en.result: Under non-convex smoothness and bounded heterogeneity assumptions, proves convergence to stationary points of the correct global objective, with time complexity matching the lower bound; experiments confirm effectiveness.
en.help_to_user: If you face model bias due to data or system heterogeneity in distributed training, this method offers a simple and effective correction.

idea_spark.idea: Could combining this rescaling strategy with gradient compression or communication delay compensation further improve efficiency under heterogeneity?
idea_spark.risk: The rescaling factor relies on accurate measurement of computation times, which may be unstable in highly dynamic or noisy environments.
idea_spark.inspiration: Viewing the problem from the perspective of 'each worker contributes the same total learning rate' provides a fresh insight into bias in asynchronous SGD and inspires more general heterogeneous optimization designs.

papers.title: Kernel-based guarantees for nonlinear parametric models in Bayesian optimization
papers.summary: Modern Bayesian optimization and adaptive sampling methods increasingly rely on nonlinear parametric models, yet theoretical guarantees for such models under adaptive data collection remain limited. Existing analyses largely focus on Gaussian processes, kernel machines, linear models, or linearized neural approximations, leaving a gap between theory and the nonlinear models used in practice. We develop a kernel based framework for analyzing regularized nonlinear parametric models trained on adaptively collected data. Our approach uses kernels over the parameter space to induce reproducing kernel Hilbert space structures over the corresponding model class, yielding confidence bounds for models trained with broad classes of regularized convex losses. We show how these bounds can support convergence guarantees for nonlinear acquisition and surrogate models, including randomized regularized policies that select points by maximizing a trained random model. These results provide a unified route to analyzing nonlinear parametric models in Bayesian optimization and related adaptive optimization settings.

ai.tldr: 提出基于核的框架，为自适应数据收集下的非线性参数模型提供理论保证，支持贝叶斯优化的收敛性分析。
ai.motivation: 现有贝叶斯优化理论主要针对高斯过程或线性模型，缺乏对实践中常用的非线性参数模型的自适应数据收集保证。
ai.method: 在参数空间上定义核，诱导出模型类的再生核希尔伯特空间结构，从而对正则化凸损失训练的非线性模型给出置信界。
ai.result: 这些置信界可用于证明非线性采集函数和代理模型的收敛性，包括随机正则化策略。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解非线性参数模型在贝叶斯优化中的理论可靠性，指导算法设计与选择。

idea_spark.idea: 将参数空间核与正则化损失结合，统一分析非线性模型的自适应数据收集。
idea_spark.risk: 核选择可能影响实际性能，理论界可能较保守。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从核方法视角桥接非线性模型与贝叶斯优化理论。

en.tldr: Proposes a kernel-based framework to provide theoretical guarantees for nonlinear parametric models under adaptive data collection, supporting convergence analysis in Bayesian optimization.
en.motivation: Existing Bayesian optimization theory mainly covers Gaussian processes or linear models, lacking guarantees for nonlinear parametric models commonly used in practice under adaptive data collection.
en.method: Defines kernels over the parameter space to induce reproducing kernel Hilbert space structures over the model class, yielding confidence bounds for nonlinear models trained with regularized convex losses.
en.result: These bounds enable convergence guarantees for nonlinear acquisition and surrogate models, including randomized regularized policies.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the theoretical reliability of nonlinear parametric models in Bayesian optimization, guiding algorithm design and selection.

idea_spark.idea: Combine parameter-space kernels with regularized losses to unify analysis of nonlinear models under adaptive data collection.
idea_spark.risk: Kernel choice may affect practical performance, and theoretical bounds may be conservative.
idea_spark.inspiration: Bridging nonlinear models and Bayesian optimization theory from a kernel perspective.

papers.title: Support-Conditioned Flow Matching Is Kernel Smoothing
papers.summary: Generative models are often conditioned on a small set of examples via cross-attention. Under the Gaussian optimal-transport path, we show that the exact velocity field induced by a finite support set is a Nadaraya--Watson kernel smoother whose bandwidth decreases with flow time, from broad averaging at early steps to nearest-neighbor at late steps. A single Gaussian-kernel attention head exactly computes this field, connecting cross-attention conditioning to classical kernel theory. The theory predicts three failure regimes: nearest-neighbor collapse of the kernel at high dimension, mismatch between the isotropic kernel and the data geometry, and insufficient support for nonparametric estimation. Experiments on Gaussian mixtures, spherical shells, and DINOv2 ImageNet features confirm that learned conditioning improves in precisely these regimes, and that IP-Adapter's cross-attention implements approximate NW smoothing in practice.

ai.tldr: 本文证明在最优传输路径下，支持条件流匹配的精确速度场等价于Nadaraya-Watson核平滑器，并指出其在高维、各向同性核与数据几何不匹配、以及支持集不足时的失败模式。
ai.motivation: 理解基于交叉注意力的条件生成模型（如IP-Adapter）的工作原理，揭示其与经典核平滑理论的联系。
ai.method: 理论推导：在Gaussian最优传输路径下，有限支持集诱导的速度场等价于带宽随时间减小的Nadaraya-Watson核平滑器；单个高斯核注意力头可精确计算该场。
ai.result: 实验验证了理论预测的三种失败模式，并表明IP-Adapter的交叉注意力在实践中实现了近似的NW平滑。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解条件流匹配模型的理论基础，指导模型设计以避免核塌缩、各向同性假设失效等问题。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应各向异性核的注意力机制，以匹配数据几何结构。
idea_spark.risk: 自适应核可能增加计算复杂度，且理论保证需重新推导。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从经典非参数统计中借鉴带宽选择方法，用于优化生成模型的采样过程。

en.tldr: This paper shows that under the Gaussian optimal-transport path, the exact velocity field of support-conditioned flow matching is equivalent to a Nadaraya-Watson kernel smoother, and identifies three failure regimes: high-dimensional collapse, mismatch with data geometry, and insufficient support.
en.motivation: To understand the working mechanism of cross-attention-based conditional generative models (e.g., IP-Adapter) and connect them to classical kernel smoothing theory.
en.method: Theoretical derivation: under the Gaussian optimal-transport path, the velocity field induced by a finite support set equals a Nadaraya-Watson kernel smoother with time-decreasing bandwidth; a single Gaussian-kernel attention head computes this field exactly.
en.result: Experiments on Gaussian mixtures, spherical shells, and DINOv2 ImageNet features confirm the predicted failure regimes and show that IP-Adapter's cross-attention implements approximate NW smoothing in practice.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the theoretical foundation of support-conditioned flow matching and guides model design to avoid issues like kernel collapse and isotropic assumption mismatch.

idea_spark.idea: Design adaptive anisotropic kernel attention mechanisms to match data geometry.
idea_spark.risk: Adaptive kernels may increase computational cost and require re-derivation of theoretical guarantees.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow bandwidth selection methods from classical nonparametric statistics to optimize the sampling process of generative models.

papers.title: Robust approximation error estimates for analysis-suitable $G^1$ isogeometric multi-patch discretizations
papers.summary: We prove $p$-robust approximation error estimates for $H^2$-conforming isogeometric discretizations over planar multi-patch domains. Possible applications are fourth order boundary value problems, like the biharmonic equation or Kirchhoff-Love plates. Using Isogeometric Analysis, such conforming discretizations can be constructed effortlessly for the single-patch case. In order to obtain a globally $H^2$-conforming discretization in the multi-patch case, the functions must be $C^1$-smooth across the interfaces between the patches. To obtain optimal approximation properties, those $C^1$-smooth spaces must also reproduce splines of sufficiently high degree for traces and transversal derivatives at all patch interfaces. Such constructions are based on some assumptions on the geometry. We restrict ourselves to the class of analysis-suitable $G^1$ (AS-$G^1$) multi-patch domains, which is the subset of $C^0$-matching multi-patch domains that allows the definition of spline spaces that yield the necessary reproduction properties without the need to locally increase the degree. While approximation error estimates have been established for single-patch and $C^0$ isogeometric multi-patch spaces, corresponding results for the $C^1$ multi-patch setting have been missing. The resulting bounds on the approximation error depend on the geometry parameterization and on the Sobolev regularity of the target function, but are independent of the spline degree $p$.

ai.tldr: 针对分析适用的G1多片域，证明了H2协调等几何离散化的p鲁棒逼近误差估计。
ai.motivation: 现有C0多片空间的误差估计已建立，但C1多片设置的结果缺失。
ai.method: 基于分析适用G1多片域假设，利用C1光滑空间和样条再现性质推导误差界。
ai.result: 误差界依赖于几何参数化和目标函数的Sobolev正则性，与样条次数p无关。
ai.help_to_user: 为高阶偏微分方程（如双调和方程）的等几何分析提供理论保证。

idea_spark.idea: 利用几何参数化与样条再现性质解耦误差界与多项式次数。
idea_spark.risk: 假设局限于分析适用G1域，可能不适用于一般几何。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发设计更鲁棒的C1多片等几何格式用于板壳问题。

en.tldr: We prove p-robust approximation error estimates for H2-conforming isogeometric discretizations over analysis-suitable G1 multi-patch domains.
en.motivation: Error estimates exist for single-patch and C0 multi-patch spaces, but results for the C1 multi-patch setting were missing.
en.method: Based on the class of analysis-suitable G1 multi-patch domains, we use C1-smooth spaces with spline reproduction properties to derive error bounds.
en.result: The error bounds depend on the geometry parameterization and Sobolev regularity of the target function, but are independent of the spline degree p.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical guarantees for isogeometric analysis of fourth-order boundary value problems such as the biharmonic equation and Kirchhoff-Love plates.

idea_spark.idea: Decouple the error bound from polynomial degree by leveraging geometry parameterization and spline reproduction properties.
idea_spark.risk: The assumptions are limited to analysis-suitable G1 domains, which may not cover general geometries.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the design of more robust C1 multi-patch isogeometric schemes for plate and shell problems.

papers.title: Beyond Oversquashing: Understanding Signal Propagation in GNNs Via Observables
papers.summary: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) perform computations on graphs by routing the signal between graph regions using a graph shift operator or a message passing scheme. Often, the propagation of the signal leads to a loss of information, where the signal tends to diffuse across the graph instead of being deliberately routed between regions of interest. Two notions that depict this phenomenon are oversmoothing and oversquashing. In this paper, we propose an alternative approach for modeling signal propagation, inspired by quantum mechanics, using the notion of observables. Specifically, we model the place in the graph where the signal lies, how much the signal is concentrated there, and how much of the signal is propagated towards a location of interest when applying a GNN. Using these new concepts, we prove that standard spectral GNNs have poor signal propagation capabilities. We then propose a new type of spectral GNN, termed Schrödinger GNN, which we show has a superior capacity to route the signal across the graph.

ai.tldr: 受量子力学启发，提出用可观测量建模GNN中的信号传播，证明标准谱GNN传播能力差，并设计出更优的薛定谔GNN。
ai.motivation: 现有GNN中信号传播常导致信息丢失（过平滑、过挤压），缺乏精确建模信号路由的方法。
ai.method: 引入量子力学中的可观测量概念，建模信号在图中的位置、集中程度及向目标位置传播的比例，并基于此设计薛定谔GNN。
ai.result: 理论上证明标准谱GNN信号传播能力差，而薛定谔GNN具有更强的跨图信号路由能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解GNN中信号传播的瓶颈，并提供一种可解释的改进架构。

idea_spark.idea: 将量子力学中的可观测量与GNN结合，设计新的谱图卷积算子。
idea_spark.risk: 量子力学类比可能增加模型复杂度，且实际性能提升需在更多数据集上验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从量子力学中借用的可观测量概念为GNN信号传播提供了新的理论视角和设计空间。

zh.tldr: 受量子力学启发，提出用可观测量建模GNN中的信号传播，证明标准谱GNN传播能力差，并设计出更优的薛定谔GNN。
zh.motivation: 现有GNN中信号传播常导致信息丢失（过平滑、过挤压），缺乏精确建模信号路由的方法。
zh.method: 引入量子力学中的可观测量概念，建模信号在图中的位置、集中程度及向目标位置传播的比例，并基于此设计薛定谔GNN。
zh.result: 理论上证明标准谱GNN信号传播能力差，而薛定谔GNN具有更强的跨图信号路由能力。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解GNN中信号传播的瓶颈，并提供一种可解释的改进架构。

idea_spark.idea: 将量子力学中的可观测量与GNN结合，设计新的谱图卷积算子。
idea_spark.risk: 量子力学类比可能增加模型复杂度，且实际性能提升需在更多数据集上验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从量子力学中借用的可观测量概念为GNN信号传播提供了新的理论视角和设计空间。

en.tldr: Inspired by quantum mechanics, we model signal propagation in GNNs using observables, prove standard spectral GNNs have poor propagation, and propose Schrödinger GNN with superior routing.
en.motivation: Signal propagation in GNNs often loses information (oversmoothing, oversquashing), lacking precise modeling of signal routing.
en.method: Introduce quantum-mechanical observables to model signal location, concentration, and propagation fraction; design Schrödinger GNN accordingly.
en.result: Theoretically prove poor signal propagation in standard spectral GNNs; Schrödinger GNN shows enhanced cross-graph signal routing.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into GNN signal propagation bottlenecks and offers an interpretable improved architecture.

idea_spark.idea: Combine quantum observables with GNNs to design new spectral graph convolution operators.
idea_spark.risk: Quantum analogy may increase model complexity; actual performance gains need validation on more datasets.
idea_spark.inspiration: Observables from quantum mechanics offer a fresh theoretical perspective and design space for GNN signal propagation.

papers.title: Hydrodynamic limits for TASEP with space-time discontinuities
papers.summary: We develop a hydrodynamic theory for a height-dependent version of the totally asymmetric simple exclusion process in which the jump rate at a growth site is sampled from a macroscopic two-dimensional speed function evaluated at the spatial coordinate and the current height level. The speed function is allowed to have discontinuities along locally finitely many curves. Through the TASEP height-function representation, the process is coupled to an inhomogeneous directed last-passage percolation model whose exponential rates vary discontinuously in the two macroscopic LPP coordinates. Combining the law of large numbers for this last-passage model with an extension of the variational coupling method, we prove a hydrodynamic limit for the height function and for the associated particle density.   The limiting current is characterised by a Lax-Oleinik type variational formula built from the discontinuous last-passage shape function. We then identify the first-order PDE structure selected by the microscopic dynamics. At points of differentiability of the limiting current and continuity of the sampled coefficient, the current solves a Hamilton-Jacobi equation whose Hamiltonian depends discontinuously on the spatial variable and on the value of the solution itself. At discontinuities, the variational formula leads to a natural envelope-based discontinuous viscosity formulation, and we prove that the limiting current satisfies this formulation. Finally, when the coefficient has only spatial discontinuities, we prove uniqueness of the Hamilton-Jacobi solution in the natural class of nondecreasing Lipschitz currents, and identify its spatial derivative as the maximal-current weak solution of the associated scalar conservation law with discontinuous flux.

ai.tldr: 本文为具有时空间断跳跃率的TASEP建立了水动力学极限理论，通过变分耦合方法将过程与不连续末程渗流模型关联，证明了高度函数和粒子密度的极限，并刻画了极限电流满足的Hamilton-Jacobi方程及间断粘性公式。
ai.motivation: 研究具有高度依赖性和时空间断跳跃率的TASEP的水动力学极限，填补现有理论在间断系数情形下的空白。
ai.method: 利用TASEP高度函数表示将过程耦合到不连续指数率的末程渗流模型，结合大数定律和变分耦合方法证明极限，并通过Lax-Oleinik型变分公式识别PDE结构。
ai.result: 证明了高度函数和粒子密度的水动力学极限，极限电流满足带有间断Hamilton量的Hamilton-Jacobi方程，在间断处满足包络型粘性公式；当系数仅有空间间断时，证明了唯一性并识别为间断通量守恒律的最大电流弱解。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解具有时空间断系数的排斥过程提供了严格的水动力学极限框架，可用于分析非均匀生长或交通流等系统中的宏观行为。

idea_spark.idea: 将间断系数下的水动力学极限与不连续末程渗流模型结合，通过变分公式直接刻画间断处的粘性解。
idea_spark.risk: 间断情形下唯一性证明依赖于系数仅空间间断的假设，更一般的时空间断情形可能需额外条件。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用Lax-Oleinik变分公式处理间断Hamilton-Jacobi方程的思路可启发其他非光滑PDE的微观推导。

en.tldr: This paper develops a hydrodynamic theory for TASEP with space-time discontinuous jump rates, coupling the process to an inhomogeneous last-passage percolation model via variational coupling, proving limits for the height function and particle density, and characterizing the limiting current via a Hamilton-Jacobi equation with discontinuous Hamiltonian and an envelope-based discontinuous viscosity formulation.
en.motivation: To establish hydrodynamic limits for TASEP with height-dependent and space-time discontinuous jump rates, filling a gap in existing theory for discontinuous coefficients.
en.method: Using the TASEP height-function representation to couple the process to a last-passage percolation model with discontinuous exponential rates, combining the law of large numbers with an extension of the variational coupling method, and identifying the PDE structure via a Lax-Oleinik type variational formula.
en.result: Hydrodynamic limits for the height function and particle density are proved; the limiting current satisfies a Hamilton-Jacobi equation with discontinuous Hamiltonian, and at discontinuities an envelope-based discontinuous viscosity formulation holds. For spatially discontinuous coefficients only, uniqueness is proved and the derivative is identified as the maximal-current weak solution of a scalar conservation law with discontinuous flux.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous hydrodynamic limit framework for exclusion processes with space-time discontinuous rates, useful for analyzing macroscopic behavior in inhomogeneous growth or traffic flow systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combining hydrodynamic limits for discontinuous coefficients with last-passage percolation and directly characterizing viscosity solutions at discontinuities via variational formulas.
idea_spark.risk: Uniqueness proof relies on the assumption of only spatial discontinuities; more general space-time discontinuities may require additional conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: The use of Lax-Oleinik variational formulas to handle discontinuous Hamilton-Jacobi equations can inspire microscopic derivations for other non-smooth PDEs.

papers.title: Reward-Weighted On-Policy Distillation with an Open Property-Equivalence Verifier for NL-to-SVA Generation
papers.summary: LLM-based generation of SystemVerilog Assertions (SVA) is often reported as nearing saturation, with the strongest specialized model reaching ${\sim}76\%$ accuracy on NL2SVA-Human. We show that this aggregate hides a temporal gap: models that appear strong overall still collapse to a few implication templates on bounded-delay and liveness specifications. The core issue is that the dominant recipe, supervised fine-tuning on NL/SVA pairs, optimizes token-level mimicry rather than the \emph{property equivalence} that defines SVA correctness. We introduce \emph{Reward-Weighted On-Policy Distillation} (RWOPD), an on-policy distillation method that samples student rollouts, scores them with an open SymbiYosys+Z3 Property-Equivalence Checker (PEC), and applies a verifier-reward-weighted forward-KL gradient from a frozen 14B teacher on verifier-passable rollouts. This keeps the supervision dense at every response token while grounding both selection and loss weight in property-equivalent behavior. RWOPD distills CodeV-SVA-14B into a Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct student that sets a new state of the art on NL2SVA-Human and NL2SVA-Machine across pass@1, pass@5, and pass@10, surpassing both specialized prior SOTA models and 671B general-purpose baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出奖励加权在线蒸馏方法，利用属性等价验证器提升LLM生成SystemVerilog断言的正确性，在NL2SVA基准上达到新最优。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM生成SVA的方法在时序规范上存在模板坍塌问题，监督微调仅优化词级模仿，未保证属性等价性。
ai.method: RWOPD：学生模型在线采样，经SymbiYosys+Z3属性等价检查器评分，用验证器奖励加权的前向KL散度从冻结的14B教师蒸馏，仅对通过验证的样本施加损失。
ai.result: 将CodeV-SVA-14B蒸馏至Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct，在NL2SVA-Human和NL2SVA-Machine上pass@1/5/10均超越专用SOTA和671B通用模型。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种利用形式验证器作为奖励信号来提升LLM生成代码正确性的通用蒸馏框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将属性等价验证器替换为领域特定验证器（如定理证明器、模型检查器），实现更广泛的正确性引导蒸馏。
idea_spark.risk: 验证器可能成为瓶颈，复杂属性验证耗时；奖励稀疏时蒸馏效率下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用开源形式验证工具作为可微分奖励，将验证反馈直接融入梯度更新，避免传统RL的采样低效。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种利用形式验证器作为奖励信号来提升LLM生成代码正确性的通用蒸馏框架。

en.tldr: Proposes Reward-Weighted On-Policy Distillation with a property-equivalence verifier to improve LLM-generated SystemVerilog Assertions, achieving new SOTA on NL2SVA benchmarks.
en.motivation: Existing LLM-based SVA generation suffers from template collapse on temporal specifications; supervised fine-tuning optimizes token-level mimicry rather than property equivalence.
en.method: RWOPD: on-policy sampling of student rollouts, scoring via SymbiYosys+Z3 Property-Equivalence Checker, and applying verifier-reward-weighted forward-KL gradient from a frozen 14B teacher on verifier-passable rollouts.
en.result: Distills CodeV-SVA-14B into Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct, surpassing both specialized prior SOTA and 671B general-purpose models on NL2SVA-Human and NL2SVA-Machine at pass@1/5/10.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general distillation framework that leverages formal verifiers as reward signals to improve correctness of LLM-generated code.

idea_spark.idea: Replace the property-equivalence verifier with domain-specific verifiers (e.g., theorem provers, model checkers) for broader correctness-guided distillation.
idea_spark.risk: Verifier may become a bottleneck; complex property verification is time-consuming; sparse rewards reduce distillation efficiency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage open-source formal verification tools as differentiable rewards, integrating verification feedback directly into gradient updates to avoid sampling inefficiency of traditional RL.

papers.title: On Hallucinations in Inverse Problems: Fundamental Limits and Provable Assessment Methods
papers.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) has transformed imaging inverse problems, from medical diagnostics to Earth observation. Yet deep neural networks can produce hallucinations, realistic-looking but incorrect details, undermining their reliability, especially when ground truth data is unavailable. We develop a theoretical framework showing that such hallucinations are not merely artifacts of particular models, but can arise from the ill-posed nature of the inverse problem itself. We derive necessary and sufficient conditions for hallucinations, together with computable bounds on their magnitude that depend only on the forward model. Building on this theory, we introduce algorithms to: (1) estimate the minimum hallucination magnitude achievable by any reconstruction model for a given input; (2) assess the faithfulness of reconstructed details by a given reconstruction model. Experiments across three imaging tasks demonstrate that our approach applies broadly, including to modern generative models, and provides a principled way to quantify and evaluate AI hallucinations.

ai.tldr: 提出理论框架证明逆问题中的幻觉源于问题本身的病态性，并给出可计算的界限与评估算法。
ai.motivation: 深度神经网络在成像逆问题中会产生看似真实但错误的细节（幻觉），缺乏可靠评估方法。
ai.method: 推导幻觉存在的充要条件及仅依赖前向模型的可计算界限，并设计两种算法：估计最小幻觉幅度、评估重建细节的忠实度。
ai.result: 在三个成像任务上验证方法适用于包括生成模型在内的多种模型，提供量化评估幻觉的原则性途径。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解逆问题中幻觉的根本原因，并提供工具评估和量化重建结果的可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用可计算界限设计自适应正则化策略，在重建过程中主动抑制幻觉。
idea_spark.risk: 界限的紧性可能依赖前向模型的精确性，实际应用中模型误差可能影响评估效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将幻觉的数学定义与可解释性结合，开发面向用户的置信度可视化工具。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将幻觉的数学定义与可解释性结合，开发面向用户的置信度可视化工具。

en.tldr: A theoretical framework showing hallucinations in inverse problems stem from ill-posedness, with computable bounds and assessment algorithms.
en.motivation: Deep neural networks produce realistic but incorrect details (hallucinations) in imaging inverse problems, lacking reliable evaluation methods.
en.method: Derives necessary and sufficient conditions for hallucinations and computable bounds depending only on the forward model, then introduces algorithms to estimate minimum hallucination magnitude and assess faithfulness of reconstructed details.
en.result: Experiments on three imaging tasks demonstrate broad applicability, including to modern generative models, providing a principled way to quantify and evaluate AI hallucinations.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the fundamental causes of hallucinations in inverse problems and provides tools to evaluate and quantify the reliability of reconstructions.

idea_spark.idea: Design adaptive regularization strategies that actively suppress hallucinations during reconstruction using the computable bounds.
idea_spark.risk: Tightness of bounds may depend on accuracy of the forward model; model errors in practice could affect evaluation effectiveness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine the mathematical definition of hallucinations with interpretability to develop user-facing confidence visualization tools.

papers.title: A Bayesian Adaptive Latent Mixture Model for Zero-Inflated Weighted Brain Connectome Analysis
papers.summary: Replicated weighted networks often exhibit many structural zeros alongside heterogeneous non-zero edge strengths. In structural connectomics, this zero-inflation coincides with subjects expressing overlapping, rather than discrete, connectivity patterns. To address these features, we propose a Bayesian adaptive latent mixture model for zero-inflated weighted networks. Our approach represents each subject network as a simplex mixture of shared low-rank latent score matrices, integrated with a hurdle likelihood that separates edge existence from conditional edge strength. A sparsity-coupling parameter enables absent edges to be either independent of, or informative about, the latent connectivity. For computation, we employ transformed Hamiltonian Monte Carlo on unconstrained coordinates, selecting the number of templates via predictive fit, held-out link prediction, and template stability. Theoretically, we establish posterior consistency, local asymptotic normality, a Bernstein--von Mises approximation, and predictive consistency for an identifiable quotient-space estimand under a fixed-template scenario. Simulations demonstrate performance gains over topology-only baselines in settings with mixed memberships or structure-informed sparsity. Applied to Human Connectome Project data, the model recovers stable latent score patterns and heterogeneous subject-level mixtures, with behavioural analyses serving strictly as exploratory annotations rather than confirmatory biomarker claims.

ai.tldr: 提出贝叶斯自适应潜在混合模型，用于分析零膨胀加权脑连接组网络，同时处理结构零点和异质边权重。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以同时处理脑连接组中大量结构零点和异质边权重，且受试者连接模式存在重叠而非离散。
ai.method: 将每个受试者网络表示为共享低秩潜在得分矩阵的单纯形混合，结合障碍似然分离边存在性与条件边强度，并通过稀疏耦合参数控制零边与潜在连接的关系。
ai.result: 模拟显示优于仅拓扑基线；HCP数据恢复稳定潜在得分模式和异质受试者混合，行为分析仅作探索性注释。
ai.help_to_user: 提供处理零膨胀加权网络的可解释贝叶斯框架，适用于脑连接组等结构化网络数据。

idea_spark.idea: 利用稀疏耦合参数区分零边是否携带信息，可启发设计更灵活的网络缺失机制。
idea_spark.risk: 计算复杂度高，模板数量选择依赖预测拟合，可能过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合障碍似然与潜在混合，为处理异质零膨胀数据提供新思路。
