en.tldr: Proposes a voltage-aware grid aggregation method that preserves voltage levels and transformers, significantly improving transformer expansion cost accuracy in a European case study.
en.motivation: Existing grid aggregation methods neglect voltage level differences, underestimating the role of transformers in expansion planning and compromising investment decisions.
en.method: Develops a voltage-aware network partitioning and aggregation methodology that retains individual voltage levels and transformers, solved with PyPSA for a European expansion problem.
en.result: Compared to voltage-unaware aggregation, the new method preserves up to 70% of transformer expansion costs from the full grid model, markedly improving decision accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Helps energy system modelers more accurately assess transformer expansion needs, enhancing the reliability of aggregated grid models in planning.

idea_spark.idea: Combine voltage-aware aggregation with machine learning to automatically identify critical transformer nodes for further computational reduction.
idea_spark.risk: Preserving voltage levels may increase model size, requiring a trade-off between accuracy and computational efficiency.
idea_spark.inspiration: The voltage-aware concept can inspire aggregation methods that consider other equipment, such as converter stations or reactive power compensators.

papers.title: Leveraging Speech to Identify Signatures of Insight and Transfer in Problem Solving
papers.summary: Many problems seem to require a flash of insight to solve. What form do these sudden insights take, and what impact do they have on how people approach similar problems in the future? In this work, we prompted participants (N = 189) to talk aloud as they attempted to solve a sequence of five "matchstick-arithmetic" problems. These problems either all relied on the same kind of non-obvious solution (Same group) or a different kind each time (Different group). We found that Same participants improved more rapidly than Different participants, and as they improved, they talked more and talked about different things when solving later problems. Specifically, they were more likely to spontaneously categorize the problem they were working on. Taken together, these findings suggest that a hallmark of transferable insights is their accessibility for verbal report, even if the underlying precursors of insight remain difficult to articulate.

ai.tldr: 通过让参与者大声说出解决火柴算术问题的过程，发现能够转移的洞察力更易被口头报告，且随着熟练度提高，参与者会更多谈论并自发分类问题。
ai.motivation: 探究洞察力的形式及其对未来类似问题解决的影响。
ai.method: 189名参与者被分为相同或不同问题组，在解决五个火柴算术问题时进行有声思维报告。
ai.result: 相同组进步更快，且在后来的问题中说话更多、内容不同，更可能自发分类问题。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解洞察力的可转移性及其与语言报告的关系。

idea_spark.idea: 利用语音分析自动检测问题解决中的洞察时刻和知识转移。
idea_spark.risk: 口头报告可能干扰洞察过程，且洞察的潜在前兆难以捕捉。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将有声思维协议与机器学习结合，开发实时洞察检测工具。

en.tldr: By prompting participants to talk aloud while solving matchstick-arithmetic problems, we found that transferable insights are more accessible for verbal report, and as participants improved, they talked more and spontaneously categorized problems.
en.motivation: To investigate the form of sudden insights and their impact on future problem solving.
en.method: 189 participants were assigned to same or different problem groups and used think-aloud protocols while solving five matchstick-arithmetic problems.
en.result: Same group improved faster, talked more and about different things in later problems, and were more likely to spontaneously categorize problems.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the transferability of insights and their relationship to verbal report.

idea_spark.idea: Use speech analysis to automatically detect insight moments and knowledge transfer in problem solving.
idea_spark.risk: Verbal report may interfere with insight process, and underlying precursors are hard to capture.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine think-aloud protocols with machine learning to build real-time insight detection tools.

papers.title: Comparing the Performance of Heterogeneous Conjugate Gradient and Cholesky Solvers on Various Hardware Using SYCL
papers.summary: Many important real-world applications, such as System Identification with Gaussian Processes, involve solving linear systems with symmetric positive-definite matrices. The iterative CG method and direct solvers based on the Cholesky decomposition are two popular methods that can be applied in this case. Since often very large systems have to be solved when dealing with such real-world scenarios, GPUs are commonly used to accelerate the computations. However, homogeneous approaches that only leverage the GPU in the system do not take full advantage of the often powerful CPUs located in modern HPC systems. In this work, we present multi-vendor, heterogeneous implementations of the CG method and the Cholesky decomposition that leverage the CPU and GPU of a heterogeneous system simultaneously using SYCL. Furthermore, we compare their runtime behavior to traditional, homogeneous approaches. The results show that for large matrices, our heterogeneous implementation is up to 32 percent faster for the CG method and up to 29 percent faster for the Cholesky decomposition compared to the corresponding GPU-only implementations. In addition, for large matrices, our heterogeneous implementation of the Cholesky decomposition can achieve at least 12 percent faster runtimes across several systems with GPUs from NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel.

ai.tldr: 使用SYCL在多种硬件上实现了共轭梯度和Cholesky分解的异构版本，相比纯GPU实现最高提速32%和29%。
ai.motivation: 纯GPU方法未充分利用现代HPC系统中CPU的计算能力，需要异构方案来提升性能。
ai.method: 基于SYCL编写CPU+GPU协同的共轭梯度法和Cholesky分解，并在NVIDIA、AMD、Intel GPU上测试。
ai.result: 大矩阵下异构CG比纯GPU快32%，异构Cholesky快29%，且跨厂商GPU均至少快12%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种跨厂商、可移植的异构线性求解器实现，可直接用于对称正定矩阵问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将矩阵分块，CPU处理小规模或稀疏部分，GPU处理大规模稠密部分，动态负载均衡。
idea_spark.risk: CPU-GPU通信开销可能抵消加速收益，需精细调优分块大小。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴此思路，设计异构版本的稀疏直接求解器用于有限元分析。

zh.tldr: 使用SYCL在多种硬件上实现了共轭梯度和Cholesky分解的异构版本，相比纯GPU实现最高提速32%和29%。
zh.motivation: 纯GPU方法未充分利用现代HPC系统中CPU的计算能力，需要异构方案来提升性能。

zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种跨厂商、可移植的异构线性求解器实现，可直接用于对称正定矩阵问题。

en.tldr: Heterogeneous implementations of conjugate gradient and Cholesky decomposition using SYCL achieve up to 32% and 29% speedup over GPU-only versions on large matrices.
en.motivation: GPU-only approaches underutilize CPUs in modern HPC systems; heterogeneous solutions are needed to improve performance.
en.method: Implemented CPU-GPU cooperative conjugate gradient and Cholesky decomposition with SYCL, tested on NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel GPUs.
en.result: For large matrices, heterogeneous CG is up to 32% faster and heterogeneous Cholesky up to 29% faster than GPU-only; at least 12% faster across all vendors.
en.help_to_user: Provides a portable, multi-vendor heterogeneous linear solver implementation directly applicable to symmetric positive-definite problems.

idea_spark.idea: Partition the matrix: CPU handles small or sparse parts, GPU handles large dense parts, with dynamic load balancing.
idea_spark.risk: CPU-GPU communication overhead may offset speedup; requires careful tuning of partition sizes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Apply this approach to design a heterogeneous sparse direct solver for finite element analysis.

papers.title: Backbone is All You Need: Assessing Vulnerabilities of Frozen Foundation Models in Synthetic Image Forensics
papers.summary: As AI-generated synthetic images become increasingly realistic, Vision Transformers (ViTs) have emerged as a cornerstone of modern deepfake detection. However, the prevailing reliance on frozen, pre-trained backbones introduces a subtle yet critical vulnerability. In this work, we present the Surrogate Iterative Adversarial Attack (SIAA), a gray-box attack that exploits knowledge of the detector's ViT backbone alone and operates entirely within the target detector's feature space to craft highly effective adversarial examples. Through our experiments, involving multiple ViT-based detectors and diverse gray-box scenarios, including few-shot learning, complete training misalignment and attack transferability tests, we demonstrate that this vulnerability consistently yields high attack success rates, often approaching white-box performance. By doing so, we reveal that backbone knowledge alone is sufficient to undermine detector reliability, highlighting the urgent need for more resilient defenses in adversarial multimedia forensics.

ai.tldr: 提出SIAA攻击，证明仅利用冻结的ViT骨干网络知识即可高效生成对抗样本，突破多种深度伪造检测器。
ai.motivation: 当前深度伪造检测器普遍依赖冻结的预训练骨干网络，这种设计可能引入隐蔽的安全漏洞。
ai.method: 设计灰盒攻击SIAA，在目标检测器的特征空间内迭代优化对抗扰动，仅需骨干网络信息。
ai.result: 在多种ViT检测器和灰盒场景（包括少样本学习、训练不匹配、迁移性测试）中，攻击成功率接近白盒水平。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解冻结骨干网络在多媒体取证中的脆弱性，并警示需要更鲁棒的防御策略。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在训练阶段引入对抗鲁棒性正则化，或设计动态骨干网络以抵抗此类攻击。
idea_spark.risk: 攻击方法可能被恶意用于绕过现有检测系统，需谨慎公开细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发研究者重新审视预训练模型在安全敏感场景中的使用方式，推动更安全的骨干网络设计。

idea_spark.risk: 攻击方法可能被恶意用于绕过现有检测系统，需谨慎公开细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发研究者重新审视预训练模型在安全敏感场景中的使用方式，推动更安全的骨干网络设计。

en.tldr: Proposes SIAA, a gray-box attack that exploits only the frozen ViT backbone to craft effective adversarial examples, compromising multiple deepfake detectors.
en.motivation: Current deepfake detectors heavily rely on frozen pre-trained backbones, which may introduce subtle security vulnerabilities.
en.method: Designs SIAA, a gray-box attack that iteratively optimizes adversarial perturbations within the target detector's feature space, requiring only backbone knowledge.
en.result: Achieves high attack success rates across various ViT-based detectors and gray-box scenarios, including few-shot learning, training misalignment, and transferability tests, often approaching white-box performance.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the vulnerability of frozen backbones in multimedia forensics and highlights the need for more robust defenses.

idea_spark.idea: Explore adversarial robustness regularization during training or design dynamic backbones to resist such attacks.
idea_spark.risk: The attack could be misused to bypass existing detection systems; details should be disclosed cautiously.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires researchers to reconsider the use of pre-trained models in security-sensitive applications and promote safer backbone designs.

papers.title: IndicMedDialog: A Parallel Multi-Turn Medical Dialogue Dataset for Accessible Healthcare in Indic Languages
papers.summary: Most existing medical dialogue systems operate in a single-turn question--answering paradigm or rely on template-based datasets, limiting conversational realism and multilingual applicability. We introduce IndicMedDialog, a parallel multi-turn medical dialogue dataset spanning English and nine Indic languages: Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Hindi, Marathi, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, and Urdu. The dataset extends MDDial with LLM-generated synthetic consultations, translated using TranslateGemma, verified by native speakers, and refined through a script-aware post-processing pipeline to correct phonetic, lexical, and character-spacing errors. Building on this dataset, we fine-tune IndicMedLM via parameter-efficient adaptation of a quantized small language model, incorporating optional patient pre-context to personalise multi-turn symptom elicitation. We evaluate against zero-shot multilingual baselines, conduct systematic error analysis across ten languages, and validate clinical plausibility through medical expert evaluation.

ai.tldr: 提出了一个覆盖英语和九种印度语言的平行多轮医疗对话数据集，并基于小语言模型微调实现多轮症状询问。
ai.motivation: 现有医疗对话系统多为单轮问答或模板数据集，缺乏多轮真实性和多语言适用性。
ai.method: 扩展MDDial，用LLM生成合成咨询，经TranslateGemma翻译、母语者验证和脚本后处理校正错误；基于量化小语言模型进行参数高效微调，融入患者预上下文实现个性化多轮症状询问。
ai.result: 在十种语言上进行了零样本基线对比和系统错误分析，并通过医学专家评估验证了临床合理性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员构建多语言、多轮医疗对话系统，尤其适用于低资源印度语言场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用LLM生成合成数据并配合翻译和后处理，可低成本扩展多语言医疗对话资源。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据可能引入临床不准确或文化偏差，需专家验证；翻译质量依赖基座模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合患者预上下文进行个性化症状询问，可提升对话系统的实用性和患者参与度。

idea_spark.risk: 合成数据可能引入临床不准确或文化偏差，需专家验证；翻译质量依赖基座模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合患者预上下文进行个性化症状询问，可提升对话系统的实用性和患者参与度。

en.tldr: Introduces a parallel multi-turn medical dialogue dataset covering English and nine Indic languages, fine-tuned on a small language model for multi-turn symptom elicitation.
en.motivation: Existing medical dialogue systems are mostly single-turn or template-based, lacking conversational realism and multilingual coverage.
en.method: Extends MDDial with LLM-generated synthetic consultations, translated via TranslateGemma, verified by native speakers, and refined by a script-aware post-processing pipeline; fine-tunes a quantized small language model with parameter-efficient adaptation and optional patient pre-context.
en.result: Evaluated against zero-shot baselines across ten languages, with systematic error analysis and clinical plausibility validation by medical experts.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers build multilingual, multi-turn medical dialogue systems, especially for low-resource Indic languages.

idea_spark.idea: Using LLM-generated synthetic data with translation and post-processing can cost-effectively expand multilingual medical dialogue resources.
idea_spark.risk: Synthetic data may introduce clinical inaccuracies or cultural biases, requiring expert validation; translation quality depends on the base model.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporating patient pre-context for personalized symptom elicitation can enhance dialogue system practicality and patient engagement.

papers.title: Affiliated operators for classical and quantum control
papers.summary: Using techniques from the theory of von Neumann algebras, we propose a framework for addressing questions of controllability of bilinear systems on infinite dimensional Hilbert spaces. In the setup, we assume only that the drift and control terms arising in a bilinear control system are affiliated with a von Neumann algebra of finite type acting on the same Hilbert space. When the control terms satisfy basic norm bound conditions, we prove existence of time-optimal controls. In the more general setting where all operators may be unbounded, we show how the dynamical Lie algebra for the system is still well-defined and may be used to check approximate controllability of the system in question. We discuss how this approach can be applied to classical dynamical systems through the Koopman operator formalism, and investigate potential candidates for the von Neumann algebra which may guide the choice of controls. We illustrate how an affiliation relation naturally arises in both classical and quantum control systems with a few examples.

ai.tldr: 利用冯·诺依曼代数理论，为无限维希尔伯特空间上的双线性系统提出可控性分析框架，证明时间最优控制存在性，并展示经典与量子控制中的关联关系。
ai.motivation: 经典与量子控制系统中，无限维空间上的可控性问题缺乏统一数学框架，现有方法难以处理无界算子情形。
ai.method: 基于有限型冯·诺依曼代数，将漂移项与控制项视为与该代数关联的算子，利用动力学李代数检验近似可控性，并通过Koopman算子联系经典系统。
ai.result: 在控制项满足范数有界条件时证明时间最优控制存在；对无界算子情形，动力学李代数仍可定义并用于近似可控性判定；给出经典与量子控制实例。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究无限维双线性控制系统的可控性提供代数化工具，尤其适用于量子控制与经典动力学的统一分析。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Koopman算子将经典非线性系统嵌入线性算子框架，再通过冯·诺依曼代数分析其可控性。
idea_spark.risk: 实际系统中冯·诺依曼代数的选取可能依赖先验知识，且无界算子情形下李代数生成可能复杂。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将代数结构引入控制理论，可能启发新的数值算法或控制律设计，如基于代数不变量的最优控制。

zh.tldr: 利用冯·诺依曼代数理论，为无限维希尔伯特空间上的双线性系统提出可控性分析框架，证明时间最优控制存在性，并展示经典与量子控制中的关联关系。
zh.motivation: 经典与量子控制系统中，无限维空间上的可控性问题缺乏统一数学框架，现有方法难以处理无界算子情形。
zh.method: 基于有限型冯·诺依曼代数，将漂移项与控制项视为与该代数关联的算子，利用动力学李代数检验近似可控性，并通过Koopman算子联系经典系统。
zh.result: 在控制项满足范数有界条件时证明时间最优控制存在；对无界算子情形，动力学李代数仍可定义并用于近似可控性判定；给出经典与量子控制实例。
zh.help_to_user: 为研究无限维双线性控制系统的可控性提供代数化工具，尤其适用于量子控制与经典动力学的统一分析。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Koopman算子将经典非线性系统嵌入线性算子框架，再通过冯·诺依曼代数分析其可控性。
idea_spark.risk: 实际系统中冯·诺依曼代数的选取可能依赖先验知识，且无界算子情形下李代数生成可能复杂。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将代数结构引入控制理论，可能启发新的数值算法或控制律设计，如基于代数不变量的最优控制。

en.tldr: Using von Neumann algebra theory, we propose a framework for controllability of bilinear systems on infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces, prove existence of time-optimal controls, and illustrate affiliation relations in classical and quantum control.
en.motivation: Classical and quantum control systems on infinite-dimensional spaces lack a unified mathematical framework, especially for unbounded operators.
en.method: Based on finite type von Neumann algebras, drift and control terms are affiliated with the algebra; dynamical Lie algebra checks approximate controllability; Koopman operator links classical systems.
en.result: Time-optimal controls exist under norm-bounded conditions; dynamical Lie algebra remains well-defined for unbounded operators and tests approximate controllability; examples from classical and quantum control.
en.help_to_user: Provides an algebraic tool for controllability analysis of infinite-dimensional bilinear systems, particularly useful for quantum control and unified classical dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Use Koopman operator to embed classical nonlinear systems into linear operator framework, then analyze controllability via von Neumann algebras.
idea_spark.risk: Selection of von Neumann algebra may rely on prior knowledge; Lie algebra generation with unbounded operators can be complex.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing algebraic structures into control theory may inspire new numerical algorithms or control law designs, e.g., optimal control based on algebraic invariants.

papers.title: Code-Centric Detection of Vulnerability-Fixing Commits: A Unified Benchmark and Empirical Study
papers.summary: Automated detection of vulnerability-fixing commits (VFCs) is critical for timely security patch deployment, as advisory databases lag patch releases by a median of 25 days and many fixes never receive advisories. We present a comprehensive evaluation of code language model based VFC detection through a unified framework consolidating over 20 fragmented datasets spanning more than 180000 commits. Across over 180 experiments with fine-tuned models from 125 M to 14 B parameters, we find no evidence that models acquire transferable security-relevant code understanding from code changes alone. When commit messages are available, they dominate model attention, and when removed, an attribution analysis shows that enriching diffs with additional intra-procedural semantic context does not shift model attention toward the code changes. Group-stratified evaluation exposes approximately 17% performance drops compared to random splits, while temporal splits on aggregated datasets prove unreliable due to compositional shift in the underlying project distributions. At a false positive rate of 0.5% all fine-tuned code-only models miss over 93% of vulnerabilities. Larger and more diverse training data or generative approaches show preliminary improvements but do not resolve the underlying limitations. To support future research on code-centric VFC detection, we release our unified framework and evaluation suite.

ai.tldr: 本文通过统一基准和大量实验，系统评估了基于代码语言模型的漏洞修复提交检测方法，发现模型未能从代码变更中习得可迁移的安全理解，且存在高漏报率等根本性局限。
ai.motivation: 漏洞修复提交的自动检测对及时部署安全补丁至关重要，但现有公告数据库平均滞后25天，且许多修复从未发布公告。
ai.method: 整合超过20个碎片化数据集（涵盖18万+提交），对1.25亿至140亿参数的微调模型进行180+实验，并采用分组分层评估和时间分割等方法。
ai.result: 模型未从代码变更中习得可迁移安全理解；提交消息主导注意力；代码仅模型在0.5%假阳性率下漏报超93%漏洞；分组分层评估显示性能下降约17%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员了解当前代码中心化漏洞修复提交检测方法的局限，并提供统一框架和评估套件以支持未来研究。

idea_spark.idea: 结合代码变更的语义上下文与外部知识（如漏洞数据库）来增强模型对安全相关代码的理解。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能过度依赖表面特征（如提交消息），导致泛化能力不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 考虑引入多模态信息（如代码结构、依赖关系）或采用生成式方法生成补丁描述以弥补信息缺失。

en.tldr: This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of code language model based vulnerability-fixing commit detection through a unified benchmark, finding that models fail to acquire transferable security-relevant code understanding and suffer from high miss rates.
en.motivation: Automated detection of vulnerability-fixing commits is critical for timely security patch deployment, as advisory databases lag patch releases by a median of 25 days and many fixes never receive advisories.
en.method: Consolidated over 20 fragmented datasets spanning 180,000+ commits, conducted 180+ experiments with fine-tuned models from 125M to 14B parameters, and employed group-stratified evaluation and temporal splits.
en.result: Models did not acquire transferable security-relevant code understanding; commit messages dominated attention; code-only models missed over 93% of vulnerabilities at 0.5% false positive rate; group-stratified evaluation showed ~17% performance drop.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers understand the limitations of current code-centric VFC detection methods and provides a unified framework and evaluation suite for future research.

idea_spark.idea: Enhance model understanding of security-relevant code by combining semantic context of code changes with external knowledge (e.g., vulnerability databases).
idea_spark.risk: Models may over-rely on superficial features (e.g., commit messages), leading to poor generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Consider incorporating multimodal information (e.g., code structure, dependencies) or using generative approaches to generate patch descriptions to compensate for missing information.

papers.title: Unifying Physically-Informed Weather Priors in A Single Model for Image Restoration Across Multiple Adverse Weather Conditions
papers.summary: Image restoration under multiple adverse weather conditions aims to develop a single model to recover the underlying scene with high visibility. Weather-related artifacts vary with the particle's distance to the camera according to the established scene visibility analysis, where close and faraway regions are more affected by falling drops and fog effects, respectively. Existing methods fail to consider this weather-specific physical visual process; thus, the restoration performance is limited. In this work, we analyze the common visual factors in adverse weather conditions and present a unified imaging model that considers the individually visible particles and fog-like aggregate scattering effects. Further, we design a novel weather-prior-based network, which leverages the weather-related prior information to help recover the scene by enhancing the features using the estimated occlusion and transmission. Experimental results in multiple adverse scenarios show the superiority of our method against state-of-the-art methods.

ai.tldr: 提出统一物理先验的单模型，用于多种恶劣天气下的图像恢复。
ai.motivation: 现有方法未考虑天气相关的物理视觉过程，恢复性能受限。
ai.method: 分析恶劣天气的共性视觉因素，建立统一成像模型，并设计基于天气先验的网络，利用估计的遮挡和透射增强特征。
ai.result: 在多种恶劣天气场景下优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用物理先验统一处理多种天气退化。

idea_spark.idea: 将天气先验作为可学习模块嵌入通用图像恢复网络。
idea_spark.risk: 先验假设可能不适用于极端或混合天气场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从场景可见性分析出发，区分近处雨滴和远处雾效的物理机制。

zh.tldr: 提出统一物理先验的单模型，用于多种恶劣天气下的图像恢复。
zh.motivation: 现有方法未考虑天气相关的物理视觉过程，恢复性能受限。
zh.method: 分析恶劣天气的共性视觉因素，建立统一成像模型，并设计基于天气先验的网络，利用估计的遮挡和透射增强特征。

idea_spark.risk: 先验假设可能不适用于极端或混合天气场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从场景可见性分析出发，区分近处雨滴和远处雾效的物理机制。

en.tldr: Proposes a single model unifying physically-informed weather priors for image restoration under multiple adverse weather conditions.
en.motivation: Existing methods fail to consider weather-specific physical visual processes, limiting restoration performance.
en.method: Analyzes common visual factors in adverse weather, builds a unified imaging model, and designs a weather-prior-based network that enhances features using estimated occlusion and transmission.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on multiple adverse weather scenarios.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to leverage physical priors to jointly handle multiple weather degradations.

idea_spark.idea: Embed weather priors as learnable modules into a general image restoration network.
idea_spark.risk: Prior assumptions may not hold for extreme or mixed weather conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Derives inspiration from scene visibility analysis, distinguishing the physical mechanisms of near-field raindrops and far-field fog effects.

papers.title: GateKD: Confidence-Gated Closed-Loop Distillation for Robust Reasoning
papers.summary: Distilling multi-step reasoning abilities from large language models (LLMs) into compact student models remains challenging due to noisy rationales, hallucinated supervision, and static teacher-student interactions. Existing reasoning distillation methods, including mentor-based approaches, predominantly operate in an open-loop manner, implicitly assuming uniform teacher reliability and consequently propagating erroneous intermediate reasoning. We propose GateKD, a confidence-gated closed-loop distillation framework that enables robust reasoning transfer by treating the teacher as a dynamic gatekeeper rather than a static oracle. GateKD introduces three complementary mechanisms: (i) confidence-gated soft supervision that selectively distills reliable predictive signals, (ii) gated hidden-state evolution that aligns intermediate representations only when teacher confidence is high, and (iii) reliability-filtered attention distillation that preserves stable reasoning structures while suppressing noisy patterns. These components jointly form a closed feedback loop in which teacher confidence continuously modulates the distillation process, reducing hallucination transfer and stabilizing student reasoning. Extensive experiments across commonsense, logical, and symbolic reasoning benchmarks, using T5 and Flan-T5 backbones of varying sizes, demonstrate that GateKD consistently outperforms strong open-loop distillation baselines. Notably, GateKD yields substantial gains in logical and symbolic reasoning, remains robust under low-resource distillation settings, and shows clear performance degradation when any gating component is removed. Our results highlight that confidence-gated closed-loop supervision is critical for building reliable and scalable small reasoning models.

ai.tldr: 提出GateKD框架，通过置信度门控的闭环蒸馏，将大语言模型的多步推理能力稳健地迁移到小模型，有效减少幻觉传播并提升推理稳定性。
ai.motivation: 现有推理蒸馏方法采用开环方式，假设教师模型始终可靠，导致错误中间推理被传播。
ai.method: 引入三种互补机制：置信度门控软监督、门控隐藏状态演化、可靠性过滤注意力蒸馏，形成教师置信度持续调节蒸馏过程的闭环反馈。
ai.result: 在常识、逻辑和符号推理基准上，使用T5和Flan-T5骨干网络，GateKD持续优于强开环蒸馏基线，尤其在逻辑和符号推理上提升显著，且低资源设置下依然稳健。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过置信度门控的闭环蒸馏构建更可靠的小型推理模型，减少幻觉并提升推理质量。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将教师置信度作为动态调节信号，不仅用于蒸馏，还可用于自适应课程学习或在线模型更新。
idea_spark.risk: 置信度估计本身可能不准确，导致门控失效；闭环机制增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受强化学习中置信度驱动的探索-利用平衡启发，将教师视为动态门卫而非静态预言机。

idea_spark.risk: 置信度估计本身可能不准确，导致门控失效；闭环机制增加计算开销。

en.tldr: Proposes GateKD, a confidence-gated closed-loop distillation framework that robustly transfers multi-step reasoning from LLMs to compact student models, reducing hallucination propagation and stabilizing reasoning.
en.motivation: Existing open-loop reasoning distillation methods assume uniform teacher reliability, propagating erroneous intermediate reasoning.
en.method: Introduces three complementary mechanisms: confidence-gated soft supervision, gated hidden-state evolution, and reliability-filtered attention distillation, forming a closed feedback loop where teacher confidence continuously modulates distillation.
en.result: On commonsense, logical, and symbolic reasoning benchmarks with T5 and Flan-T5 backbones, GateKD consistently outperforms strong open-loop baselines, with substantial gains in logical and symbolic reasoning and robustness under low-resource settings.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how confidence-gated closed-loop distillation builds reliable small reasoning models, reducing hallucination and improving reasoning quality.

idea_spark.idea: Explore using teacher confidence as a dynamic modulation signal not only for distillation but also for adaptive curriculum learning or online model updates.
idea_spark.risk: Confidence estimation may be inaccurate, causing gating failures; closed-loop adds computational overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by confidence-driven exploration-exploitation trade-offs in reinforcement learning, treating the teacher as a dynamic gatekeeper rather than a static oracle.

papers.title: Sleeper Channels and Provenance Gates: Persistent Prompt Injection in Always-on Autonomous AI Agents
papers.summary: Always-on AI agents (OpenClaw, Hermes Agent) run as a single persistent process under the owner's identity, folding messaging, memory, self-authored skills, scheduling, and shell into one authority boundary. This configuration opens what we call \emph{sleeper channels}: an untrusted input to one surface persists as a memory, skill, scheduled job, or filesystem patch, then fires later through a different surface with no attacker present. Two independent axes define the class: persistence substrate and firing-separation. We walk a confused-deputy cron attack end-to-end through OpenClaw at a pinned commit. The defense is tiered (D1, D2, D3), and D2 carries a soundness theorem against seven named deployment invariants. D2 keys on a canonical action-instance digest with one-shot owner attestations, defeating paraphrase laundering, multi-input grant reuse, and replay. A companion artifact ships the gate, a static audit over the vendored source, and a runtime adapter realising five of the ten mediation hooks (H1, H2, H3, H6, H9) around the cron path (42 tests, Node~$\geq{}20$, at \href{https://github.com/maloyan/sleeper-channels}{github.com/maloyan/sleeper-channels}). Empirical evaluation is preregistered as follow-on.

ai.tldr: 始终在线的自主AI代理存在持久性提示注入漏洞，攻击者可通过不同输入面植入恶意指令，在后续无攻击者在场时触发。
ai.motivation: 现有AI安全研究多关注单次交互中的提示注入，忽略了始终在线代理中跨会话、跨表面的持久性攻击风险。
ai.method: 定义sleeper channels攻击类，基于持久化基质和触发分离两个维度分类；提出分层防御D1-D3，其中D2采用规范动作实例摘要与一次性所有者认证，防御释义清洗、多输入授权复用和重放攻击。
ai.result: 在OpenClaw上实现端到端攻击，并开源防御门控、静态审计和运行时适配器（含42个测试）。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解始终在线AI代理的新型攻击面，并提供可落地的分层防御方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将sleeper channels概念应用于多模态AI代理，探索跨视觉、语音、文本输入面的持久性攻击。
idea_spark.risk: 防御依赖特定部署不变式，若环境变化可能失效；一次性认证可能带来用户体验开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴操作系统中的权限分离和审计日志思想，为AI代理设计类似的安全边界。

idea_spark.risk: 防御依赖特定部署不变式，若环境变化可能失效；一次性认证可能带来用户体验开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴操作系统中的权限分离和审计日志思想，为AI代理设计类似的安全边界。

en.tldr: Always-on autonomous AI agents are vulnerable to persistent prompt injection, where untrusted inputs persist across sessions and surfaces, triggering later without the attacker present.
en.motivation: Existing AI security research focuses on single-turn prompt injection, overlooking persistent cross-surface attacks in always-on agents.
en.method: Defines sleeper channels attack class along two axes (persistence substrate and firing separation); proposes tiered defense D1-D3, with D2 using canonical action-instance digest and one-shot owner attestations to defeat paraphrase laundering, multi-input grant reuse, and replay.
en.result: End-to-end attack demonstrated on OpenClaw; open-sourced defense gate, static audit, and runtime adapter with 42 tests.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand novel attack surfaces in always-on AI agents and provides a practical tiered defense.

idea_spark.idea: Apply sleeper channels concept to multimodal AI agents, exploring persistent attacks across vision, speech, and text input surfaces.
idea_spark.risk: Defense relies on specific deployment invariants that may break in changing environments; one-shot attestations may degrade user experience.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from OS permission separation and audit logging to design similar security boundaries for AI agents.

papers.title: Diagrammatic technique for Vogel's universality
papers.summary: In his 1999 preprint "Universal Lie Algebra", P. Vogel put forward a hypothesis on the existence of a universal Lie algebra. Although this hypothesis remains open, it is known that many quantities in Lie theory admit universal descriptions. Remarkably, almost all such universal formulas have been obtained through the representation theory of simple Lie (super)algebras, whereas Vogel's original framework was based on a more abstract diagrammatic algebra. Nevertheless, the diagrammatic approach has received little attention over the past two decades, since the last contributions by P.Vogel and J.Kneissler.   In this work, we revive the diagrammatic technique grounded in Vogel's $Λ$-algebra and show that it enables truly universal computations. We examine numerous examples and discuss them.

ai.tldr: 本文复兴了基于Vogel Λ-代数的图解法，展示了其在李理论中实现真正通用计算的能力，并讨论了多个例子。
ai.motivation: Vogel关于通用李代数的假设尚未解决，但许多李理论量具有通用描述；图解法在过去二十年中被忽视，本文旨在复兴并展示其有效性。
ai.method: 基于Vogel Λ-代数的图解法，进行通用计算并检验多个实例。
ai.result: 图解法能够实现真正通用的计算，并通过大量例子得到验证。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解图解法在李理论通用计算中的应用和潜力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Λ-代数图解法系统化推导李理论中的通用公式。
idea_spark.risk: Vogel假设本身未解决，图解法可能依赖未证明的假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从被忽视的经典方法中寻找解决现代问题的工具。

en.tldr: This work revives the diagrammatic technique based on Vogel's Λ-algebra, demonstrating its ability to perform truly universal computations in Lie theory, and discusses numerous examples.
en.motivation: Vogel's hypothesis on a universal Lie algebra remains open, but many quantities in Lie theory admit universal descriptions; the diagrammatic approach has been neglected for two decades, and this work aims to revive it and show its effectiveness.
en.method: Diagrammatic technique grounded in Vogel's Λ-algebra, applied to universal computations and examined through multiple examples.
en.result: The diagrammatic technique enables truly universal computations, validated by numerous examples.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the application and potential of diagrammatic techniques in universal computations of Lie theory.

idea_spark.idea: Systematically derive universal formulas in Lie theory using the Λ-algebra diagrammatic approach.
idea_spark.risk: Vogel's hypothesis itself is unresolved, and the diagrammatic technique may rely on unproven assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Finding tools for modern problems from neglected classical methods.

papers.title: Human face perception reflects inverse-generative and naturalistic discriminative objectives
papers.summary: The perceptual representations supporting our ability to recognize faces remain a computational mystery. Deep neural networks offer mechanistic hypotheses for human face perception, but theoretically distinct models often make indistinguishable representational predictions for randomly sampled faces. To expose diagnostic differences among these hypotheses, we compared six neural network models sharing an architecture but trained on distinct tasks, using face pairs optimized to elicit contrasting model predictions ("controversial" pairs) alongside randomly sampled pairs. We tested model predictions against face-dissimilarity judgments from 864 human participants across stimulus sets differing in realism and pose variation. Models prioritizing high-level, invariant structures (trained via inverse rendering, face identification, or object classification) most robustly matched human judgments. Furthermore, models trained on natural images typically outperformed synthetic-trained counterparts. Together, these findings suggest that human face perception is shaped by mechanisms that infer latent causes of facial appearance, discount nuisance variation, and are tuned by natural image statistics.

ai.tldr: 通过对比六种不同训练目标的神经网络模型，发现人类面部感知更接近逆渲染、面部识别和物体分类等强调高层不变结构的模型，且自然图像训练优于合成图像。
ai.motivation: 揭示人类面部感知背后的计算机制，区分不同神经网络模型在解释人类行为上的差异。
ai.method: 使用争议性面部对和随机面部对，比较六种架构相同但训练任务不同的模型，并与864名人类参与者的面部相似性判断进行对比。
ai.result: 逆渲染、面部识别和物体分类模型最匹配人类判断；自然图像训练模型优于合成图像训练模型。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解人类视觉系统如何高效识别面部，并为设计更类人的计算机视觉模型提供指导。

idea_spark.idea: 结合逆渲染与自然图像先验，构建能同时处理光照、姿态等干扰的面部识别模型。
idea_spark.risk: 逆渲染可能过度依赖物理先验，在非自然场景下泛化能力下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 人类面部感知的“逆生成”特性启发我们，视觉系统可能通过内部生成模型来解析输入，而非单纯依赖判别特征。

en.tldr: By comparing six neural network models with different training objectives, we found that human face perception aligns best with models emphasizing high-level invariant structures (inverse rendering, face identification, object classification) and natural image training outperforms synthetic training.
en.motivation: To uncover the computational principles underlying human face perception by distinguishing among competing neural network hypotheses.
en.method: We used controversial and random face pairs to compare six architecturally identical models trained on distinct tasks, and tested their predictions against similarity judgments from 864 human participants across varying realism and pose conditions.
en.result: Models trained on inverse rendering, face identification, or object classification best matched human judgments; natural-image-trained models outperformed synthetic-trained ones.
en.help_to_user: This work clarifies how human vision achieves robust face recognition and offers insights for building more human-like computer vision systems.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a face recognition model that jointly optimizes inverse rendering and natural image priors to handle variations in illumination and pose.
idea_spark.risk: Inverse rendering may rely too heavily on physical priors, leading to poor generalization in unnatural scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: The 'inverse-generative' nature of human face perception suggests that the visual system may use an internal generative model to parse inputs, rather than relying solely on discriminative features.

papers.title: CoGE: Sim-to-Real Online Geometric Estimation for Monocular Colonoscopy
papers.summary: Geometric estimation including depth estimation and scene reconstruction is a crucial technique for colonoscopy which can provide surgeons with 3D spatial perception and navigation. However, geometric ground truth in colonoscopy is difficult to obtain due to narrow and enclosed space of the colon, while there is a large feature gap between simulated data and realistic data caused by artifacts and illumination. In this paper, we present CoGE, a novel framework for online monocular geometric estimation during colonoscopy. Firstly, we propose an illumination-aware supervision module based on the Retinex theory to address illumination diversity in different colonoscopy scenes. Moreover, a structure-aware perception module is proposed based on wavelet decomposition to extract common structural and local features of the colon. Both quantitative and qualitative results demonstrate that the proposed model solely trained on simulated data achieves state-of-the-art performance in geometric estimation for both simulated and realistic scenes.

ai.tldr: 提出CoGE框架，仅用模拟数据训练即可实现结肠镜在线单目几何估计，在模拟和真实场景中均达到最优性能。
ai.motivation: 结肠镜中几何真值难以获取，且模拟与真实数据间存在特征差异。
ai.method: 基于Retinex理论的照明感知监督模块和基于小波分解的结构感知模块。
ai.result: 定量和定性结果均表明模型在模拟和真实场景中达到最优。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助医生在结肠镜检查中获得3D空间感知和导航。

idea_spark.idea: 结合Retinex理论和小波分解处理光照和结构差异，实现模拟到真实的泛化。
idea_spark.risk: 真实场景中极端光照或遮挡可能影响性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用经典视觉理论（Retinex）和信号处理工具（小波）解决医学图像域适应问题。

papers.title: Sparse Code Uplifting for Efficient 3D Language Gaussian Splatting
papers.summary: 3D Language Gaussian Splatting (3DLGS) augments 3D Gaussian Splatting with language-aligned visual features for open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding. A core challenge is efficiently associating high-dimensional vision-language embeddings with millions of 3D Gaussians while preserving efficient feature rendering for text-based querying. Existing methods either store dense features directly on Gaussians, causing high storage costs and slow rendering, or learn compact representations through expensive per-scene optimization with repeated feature rasterization. No existing method simultaneously achieves fast 3D semantic reconstruction, efficient storage, and fast rendering. We propose SCOUP (Sparse COde UPlifting), which addresses all three by decoupling language representation learning from 3D Gaussian optimization. Rather than working directly in 3D, we learn sparse codebook-based representations entirely using features associated with 2D image regions, associating each region with a sparse set of codebook coefficients. We then uplift these coefficients to 3D Gaussians with our weighted sparse aggregation using Gaussian-to-pixel associations, where each Gaussian accumulates coefficients over codebook atoms across views. Top-$K$ filtering then extracts the most dominant multi-view coefficients per Gaussian, enabling efficient storage and fast rendering. Our method achieves up to $400\times$ training speedup while being $3\times$ more memory efficient during training compared to the state-of-the-art in rendering speed. Across multiple benchmarks, SCOUP matches or outperforms existing methods in open-vocabulary querying accuracy.

ai.tldr: 提出SCOUP方法，通过稀疏码本和特征提升，实现高效3D语言高斯泼溅，在训练速度、存储和渲染上均优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有3D语言高斯泼溅方法在存储、渲染速度和训练效率上难以兼顾，无法同时实现快速语义重建、高效存储和快速渲染。
ai.method: 将语言表示学习与3D高斯优化解耦，在2D图像区域上学习稀疏码本表示，然后通过加权稀疏聚合将系数提升到3D高斯，并用Top-K过滤提取主导系数。
ai.result: 训练速度提升高达400倍，训练内存效率提升3倍，在多个基准上匹配或超越现有方法的开放词汇查询精度。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效构建和查询3D场景的语义信息，支持开放词汇的自然语言交互。

idea_spark.idea: 将2D视觉语言特征通过稀疏编码和跨视图聚合提升到3D，避免直接在3D空间学习高维特征。
idea_spark.risk: 稀疏码本可能丢失部分细粒度语义信息，影响复杂场景的查询精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受稀疏编码和特征提升在2D视觉中的成功启发，将其扩展到3D高斯泼溅框架。

idea_spark.risk: 稀疏码本可能丢失部分细粒度语义信息，影响复杂场景的查询精度。

en.tldr: Proposes SCOUP, using sparse codebook and feature uplifting for efficient 3D Language Gaussian Splatting, achieving superior training speed, memory, and rendering.
en.motivation: Existing 3D Language Gaussian Splatting methods cannot simultaneously achieve fast semantic reconstruction, efficient storage, and fast rendering.
en.method: Decouples language representation learning from 3D Gaussian optimization, learns sparse codebook representations on 2D image regions, then uplifts coefficients to 3D Gaussians via weighted sparse aggregation and Top-K filtering.
en.result: Up to 400x training speedup, 3x more memory efficient during training, matches or surpasses state-of-the-art in open-vocabulary query accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Enables efficient construction and querying of semantic 3D scenes with open-vocabulary natural language.

idea_spark.idea: Uplift 2D vision-language features to 3D via sparse coding and cross-view aggregation, avoiding direct high-dimensional feature learning in 3D.
idea_spark.risk: Sparse codebook may lose fine-grained semantic details, affecting query accuracy in complex scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of sparse coding and feature uplifting in 2D vision, extended to the 3D Gaussian splatting framework.

papers.title: All Circuits Lead to Rome: Rethinking Functional Anisotropy in Circuit and Sheaf Discovery for LLMs
papers.summary: In this paper, we present empirical and theoretical evidence against a central but largely implicit assumption in circuit and sheaf discovery (CSD), which we term the Functional Anisotropy Hypothesis: the idea that functions in large language models (LLMs) are localised to a unique or near-unique internal mechanism. We show that a single LLM task can instead be supported by multiple, structurally distinct circuits or sheaves that are simultaneously faithful, sparse, and complete. To systematically uncover such competing mechanisms, we introduce Overlap-Aware Sheaf Repulsion, a method that augments the CSD objective with an explicit penalty on structural overlap across multiple discovery runs, enabling the discovery of circuits or sheaves with strong task performance but minimal shared structure across a plethora of common CSD benchmarks. We find that this phenomenon becomes increasingly pronounced as the number of discovered sheaves grows and persists robustly across major CSD methods. We further identify an ultra-sparse three-edge sheaf and show that none of its edges is individually indispensable, undermining even weakened notions of canonical or essential components. To explain these findings, we propose a Distributive Dense Circuit Hypothesis and provide a theoretical analysis demonstrating that non-unique, low-overlap circuit explanations arise naturally from high-dimensional superposition under mild assumptions. Together, our results suggest that mechanistic explanations in LLMs are inherently non-canonical and call for a rethinking of how CSD results should be interpreted and evaluated.

ai.tldr: 本文通过实验和理论证明，大语言模型中的功能并非唯一地定位于单一内部机制，而是可以由多个结构不同但同样忠实、稀疏且完整的电路或层束支持。
ai.motivation: 现有电路与层束发现方法隐含假设功能各向异性，即每个任务对应唯一内部机制，但该假设缺乏验证。
ai.method: 提出重叠感知层束排斥方法，在多个发现运行中显式惩罚结构重叠，从而系统性地揭示竞争性机制。
ai.result: 发现多个低重叠的电路/层束都能达到强任务性能，且存在超稀疏三边层束，其每条边均非不可或缺。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究者理解LLM机制解释的非唯一性，避免过度依赖单一电路发现结果。

idea_spark.idea: 利用超稀疏层束作为最小解释单元，设计更鲁棒的模型编辑或知识定位方法。
idea_spark.risk: 过度强调非唯一性可能导致解释的随意性，需要建立更严格的评估标准。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从高维叠加理论出发，启发我们重新思考神经网络中功能与结构的对应关系。

en.tldr: This paper provides empirical and theoretical evidence that functions in LLMs are not localized to a unique internal mechanism; multiple structurally distinct circuits or sheaves can simultaneously support the same task.
en.motivation: The implicit Functional Anisotropy Hypothesis in circuit and sheaf discovery lacks validation, and the paper aims to challenge it.
en.method: Overlap-Aware Sheaf Repulsion is introduced to penalize structural overlap across multiple discovery runs, enabling the discovery of competing mechanisms.
en.result: Multiple low-overlap circuits/sheaves achieve strong task performance, and an ultra-sparse three-edge sheaf has no individually indispensable edge.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers understand the non-uniqueness of mechanistic explanations in LLMs, cautioning against over-reliance on single circuit discovery results.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage ultra-sparse sheaves as minimal explanation units to design more robust model editing or knowledge localization methods.
idea_spark.risk: Overemphasizing non-uniqueness may lead to arbitrariness in explanations, necessitating stricter evaluation standards.
idea_spark.inspiration: Drawing from high-dimensional superposition theory, it inspires a rethinking of the function-structure correspondence in neural networks.

papers.title: Empowering IoT Security: On-Device Intrusion Detection in Resource Constrained Devices
papers.summary: IoT devices particularly microcontrollers are challenged by their inherent limitations in processing capabilities, memory capacity, and energy conservation. Securing communication within IoT networks is further complicated by the heterogeneity of devices and the myriad of potential security threats. Our study introduces a lightweight model that utilises machine learning algorithms to achieve a notable detection accuracy of 99% using a decision tree method and 96% using a neural network in identifying cyber threats, including Denial of Service and Man-in-the-Middle attacks which make up the majority of the attacks these devices face. While the decision tree method offers higher accuracy, it requires more computational resources, whereas the neural network approach, despite a slightly lower accuracy, is more memory-efficient. Both methods enhance the real-time monitoring and defence of IoT networks, safeguarding the transmission of data. Additionally, our approach is tailored to conserve memory and optimise computational demands, rendering it suitable for deployment on microcontrollers with limited resources.

ai.tldr: 提出一种轻量级机器学习模型，在资源受限的物联网微控制器上实现99%准确率的入侵检测。
ai.motivation: 物联网设备（尤其是微控制器）计算、内存和能源有限，传统安全方案难以部署，需要高效轻量的入侵检测方法。
ai.method: 分别采用决策树和神经网络两种机器学习算法，在设备本地进行实时威胁检测。
ai.result: 决策树准确率99%，神经网络96%；决策树计算开销大，神经网络更省内存。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助开发者在资源受限的物联网设备上选择合适的安全检测方案，平衡准确率与资源消耗。

idea_spark.idea: 结合决策树的高准确率和神经网络的内存效率，设计混合模型或自适应切换策略。
idea_spark.risk: 混合模型可能增加系统复杂度，需验证在极端资源限制下的可行性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模型压缩或知识蒸馏进一步降低神经网络资源需求，同时保持高准确率。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助开发者在资源受限的物联网设备上选择合适的安全检测方案，平衡准确率与资源消耗。

idea_spark.risk: 混合模型可能增加系统复杂度，需验证在极端资源限制下的可行性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模型压缩或知识蒸馏进一步降低神经网络资源需求，同时保持高准确率。

en.tldr: Proposes a lightweight machine learning model achieving 99% intrusion detection accuracy on resource-constrained IoT microcontrollers.
en.motivation: IoT devices, especially microcontrollers, have limited computation, memory, and energy, making traditional security solutions infeasible; efficient lightweight intrusion detection is needed.
en.method: Employs decision tree and neural network machine learning algorithms for on-device real-time threat detection.
en.result: Decision tree achieves 99% accuracy, neural network 96%; decision tree has higher computational cost, neural network is more memory-efficient.
en.help_to_user: Helps developers select appropriate security detection schemes for resource-constrained IoT devices, balancing accuracy and resource consumption.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the high accuracy of decision trees with the memory efficiency of neural networks to design a hybrid model or adaptive switching strategy.
idea_spark.risk: A hybrid model may increase system complexity; feasibility under extreme resource constraints needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use model compression or knowledge distillation to further reduce neural network resource requirements while maintaining high accuracy.

papers.title: Half-space problem on the Boltzmann equation with zero Mach number at infinity
papers.summary: We study the long-time dynamics of the time-evolutionary Boltzmann equation with hard sphere collisions in the three-dimensional half-space \( \mathbb{R}^2 \times \mathbb{R}^+\), subject to diffuse reflection boundary conditions and small perturbations around a global Maxwellian equilibrium. The far-field velocity is assumed to be at rest; namely, we take the zero Mach number at infinity. In the first goal, we construct global-in-time low-regularity solutions near Maxwellians. We leverage time-decay properties along the two-dimensional tangential direction to establish polynomial decay rates of solutions matching the 2D heat equation. In the second goal, we further prove the propagation of Gevrey regularity: analyticity (Gevrey index 1) in the tangential spatial variable \(x_\parallel\), and Gevrey class with index 2 in the tangential velocity variable \(v_\parallel\), under suitably regular initial data. The proofs combine an \(L^1_k \cap L^p_k\) Fourier-space approach for decay estimates, macro-micro decomposition with \(L^2 - L^\infty\) frameworks adapted to unbounded domains, and weighted Gevrey norms to control regularity propagation, overcoming challenges from boundary effects and nonlinear interactions.

ai.tldr: 研究三维半空间上硬球碰撞Boltzmann方程在零马赫数远场条件下的长时间动力学，构造全局低正则解并证明Gevrey正则性传播。
ai.motivation: 理解半空间边界效应对Boltzmann方程长时间行为的影响，特别是远场静止（零马赫数）情形下的解存在性与正则性。
ai.method: 结合L^1_k∩L^p_k傅里叶空间方法、宏观-微观分解与L^2-L∞框架，以及加权Gevrey范数控制正则性传播。
ai.result: 构造了全局低正则解，获得多项式衰减率；证明了切向空间变量的解析性和切向速度变量的Gevrey类正则性。
ai.help_to_user: 为半空间边界条件下Boltzmann方程的理论分析提供新工具和结果，可用于流体动力学边界层研究。

idea_spark.idea: 利用切向方向的时间衰减性质匹配二维热方程，结合宏观-微观分解处理边界非线性。
idea_spark.risk: 高维半空间边界效应可能破坏全局解存在性，需精细控制边界层奇性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从热方程衰减类比出发，将半空间问题转化为二维退化问题处理。

zh.tldr: 研究三维半空间上硬球碰撞Boltzmann方程在零马赫数远场条件下的长时间动力学，构造全局低正则解并证明Gevrey正则性传播。
zh.motivation: 理解半空间边界效应对Boltzmann方程长时间行为的影响，特别是远场静止（零马赫数）情形下的解存在性与正则性。

en.tldr: We study the long-time dynamics of the Boltzmann equation with hard sphere collisions in a 3D half-space with zero Mach number at infinity, constructing global low-regularity solutions and proving Gevrey regularity propagation.
en.motivation: To understand the influence of boundary effects on the long-time behavior of the Boltzmann equation, especially under the far-field rest condition (zero Mach number).
en.method: Combining an L^1_k∩L^p_k Fourier-space approach, macro-micro decomposition with L^2-L^∞ frameworks, and weighted Gevrey norms to control regularity propagation.
en.result: Global low-regularity solutions with polynomial decay rates are constructed; analyticity in tangential spatial variables and Gevrey class 2 in tangential velocity variables are proved.
en.help_to_user: Provides new tools and results for the theoretical analysis of the Boltzmann equation with boundary conditions, applicable to boundary layer studies in fluid dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage time-decay along tangential directions to match the 2D heat equation, combined with macro-micro decomposition to handle boundary nonlinearities.
idea_spark.risk: Boundary effects in higher-dimensional half-spaces may disrupt global existence; careful control of boundary layer singularities is required.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw analogy from heat equation decay to reduce the half-space problem to a 2D degenerate problem.

papers.title: Orientation in Poisson Cluster Processes via Imaginary Bispectra
papers.summary: We study what remains detectable about one-sided Poisson cluster processes after cluster orientation is erased. We construct matched reversible cluster nulls preserving intensity and the full Bartlett spectrum, showing that second-order structure alone need not identify temporal direction. For stationary Poisson branching clusters, we derive the Fourier--Stieltjes transform of the reduced third cumulant and show that, in the $L^1$ third-cumulant regime, a nonzero imaginary factorial bispectrum certifies orientation. We also give explicit orientation-erased nulls, reversible spectral matches for monotone Hawkes kernels, and finite-window third-order orientation contrasts.

ai.tldr: 研究在擦除簇方向后，单侧泊松簇过程中仍可检测的方向信息，通过虚双谱和第三累积量识别方向。
ai.motivation: 探究在仅保留二阶结构时，泊松簇过程的时间方向是否可识别，并寻找更高阶统计量来检测方向。
ai.method: 构造可逆的簇零模型，匹配强度和Bartlett谱；推导第三累积量的傅里叶-斯蒂尔杰斯变换，利用虚阶乘双谱判断方向。
ai.result: 在L1第三累积量条件下，非零虚阶乘双谱可证明方向存在；给出了单调Hawkes核的可逆谱匹配和有限窗口三阶方向对比。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解点过程方向检测的理论基础，以及高阶统计量在方向识别中的作用。

idea_spark.idea: 将方向检测方法应用于神经科学中的尖峰序列分析，判断神经活动的时间方向性。
idea_spark.risk: 实际数据可能不满足L1累积量条件，导致方法失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用高阶谱分析揭示隐藏的时间不对称性，类似方法可用于因果推断。

en.tldr: Investigates what remains detectable about one-sided Poisson cluster processes after cluster orientation is erased, using imaginary bispectra and third cumulants to certify orientation.
en.motivation: To determine whether temporal direction in Poisson cluster processes can be identified from second-order structure alone, and to use higher-order statistics for orientation detection.
en.method: Constructs reversible cluster nulls matching intensity and Bartlett spectrum; derives Fourier-Stieltjes transform of the reduced third cumulant and uses nonzero imaginary factorial bispectrum to certify orientation.
en.result: Under L1 third-cumulant regime, nonzero imaginary factorial bispectrum certifies orientation; provides explicit orientation-erased nulls, reversible spectral matches for monotone Hawkes kernels, and finite-window third-order orientation contrasts.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical foundations for direction detection in point processes and the role of higher-order statistics in orientation identification.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the orientation detection method to neural spike train analysis to determine temporal directionality of neural activity.
idea_spark.risk: Real data may not satisfy the L1 cumulant condition, causing the method to fail.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using higher-order spectral analysis to reveal hidden temporal asymmetries, similar approaches could be used for causal inference.

papers.title: SINAPSE: A lightweight deep learning framework for accurate and explainable neutron-$γ$ discrimination
papers.summary: Traditionally, neutron-$γ$ discrimination in organic scintillators relies on techniques such as time-of-flight (ToF) selection and pulse-shape discrimination (PSD). However, particle identification through graphical cuts remains challenging in the low-charge regime due to poor signal-to-noise ratios (SNR). In this work, we propose SINAPSE, a lightweight deep learning framework for accurate and explainable neutron-$γ$ discrimination in the low-charge regime. The framework employs a dual-branch architecture that combines a 1-dimensional convolutional autoencoder for waveform denoising with a classifier for particle identification. Random augmentations are applied to high-SNR waveforms to simulate low-charge conditions, enabling robust extrapolation into regimes where conventional PSD labels are unreliable. We show that SINAPSE achieves superior denoising performance compared to conventional digital signal processing techniques, and outputs well-calibrated probabilities, consistent with traditional graphical cuts. Finally, we apply SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values to show that model decisions are driven by physically meaningful pulse-shape features, confirming consistency with established PSD principles.

ai.tldr: 提出轻量级深度学习框架SINAPSE，用于有机闪烁体中低电荷区的中子-伽马甄别，结合卷积自编码器降噪与分类器，并利用SHAP解释模型决策。
ai.motivation: 传统脉冲形状甄别（PSD）在低信噪比低电荷区难以准确区分中子与伽马，需要一种鲁棒且可解释的方法。
ai.method: 双分支架构：一维卷积自编码器对波形降噪，分类器进行粒子识别；通过高信噪比波形随机增强模拟低电荷条件，实现外推。
ai.result: 降噪性能优于传统数字信号处理，输出概率与图形切割一致，SHAP值表明决策基于物理有意义的脉冲形状特征。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种轻量、可解释的深度学习方案，可应用于辐射探测中的粒子识别任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用SHAP解释性验证模型是否学习到物理先验，可推广至其他需要物理一致性的深度学习应用。
idea_spark.risk: 低电荷区模拟依赖高信噪比波形增强，可能无法完全覆盖真实噪声分布，需实验验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将可解释性工具（如SHAP）与物理约束结合，提升深度学习在科学领域的可信度。

en.tldr: Proposes SINAPSE, a lightweight deep learning framework for neutron-gamma discrimination in organic scintillators at low charge, combining a convolutional autoencoder for denoising with a classifier, and using SHAP for explainability.
en.motivation: Traditional pulse-shape discrimination (PSD) struggles in the low-charge regime due to poor SNR, requiring a robust and explainable method.
en.method: Dual-branch architecture: 1D convolutional autoencoder for waveform denoising and a classifier for particle identification; random augmentations of high-SNR waveforms simulate low-charge conditions for robust extrapolation.
en.result: Denoising outperforms conventional digital signal processing; output probabilities align with graphical cuts; SHAP values confirm decisions are driven by physically meaningful pulse-shape features.
en.help_to_user: Provides a lightweight, explainable deep learning solution for particle identification in radiation detection.

idea_spark.idea: Using SHAP to verify that the model learns physical priors can be extended to other deep learning applications requiring physical consistency.
idea_spark.risk: Simulation of low-charge conditions via augmentation of high-SNR waveforms may not fully capture real noise distributions; experimental validation of generalization is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining explainability tools (e.g., SHAP) with physical constraints enhances trustworthiness of deep learning in scientific domains.

papers.title: HADAR-Based Thermal Infrared Hyperspectral Image Restoration
papers.summary: Thermal-infrared (TIR) hyperspectral imagery (HSI) provides critical scene information for various applications. However, its practical utility is severely limited by unique sensor degradations beyond the capabilities of existing restoration methods, which are ignorant of underlying thermal physics. Here, we propose HAIR (HADAR-based Image Restoration) as a physics-driven framework for ground-based TIR-HSI restoration. HAIR utilizes the HADAR rendering equation (HRE) and combines it with the atmospheric downwelling radiative transfer equation (RTE) to model TIR-HSI using temperature, emissivity, and texture (TeX) physical triplets. This physical model leads to a TeX decompose-synthesize strategy that guarantees physical consistency and spatio-spectral noise resilience, in stark contrast to existing approaches. Moreover, our framework uses a forward-modeled atmospheric downwelling reference, along with spectral smoothness of emissivity and blackbody radiation, to enable spectral calibration and generation that would otherwise be elusive. Our extensive experiments on the outdoor DARPA Invisible Headlights dataset and in-lab FTIR measurements show that HAIR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods across denoising, inpainting, spectral calibration, and spectral super-resolution, establishing a benchmark in objective accuracy and visual quality.

ai.tldr: 提出基于HADAR物理模型的热红外高光谱图像恢复框架HAIR，利用温度-发射率-纹理三元组实现去噪、修复、定标和超分辨率。
ai.motivation: 现有方法忽略热红外物理机制，无法处理传感器特有的退化。
ai.method: 结合HADAR渲染方程与大气下行辐射传输方程，采用TeX分解-合成策略保证物理一致性与噪声鲁棒性。
ai.result: 在DARPA数据集和FTIR测量上，HAIR在客观精度和视觉质量上全面超越现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用物理模型恢复热红外高光谱图像，提升数据质量。

idea_spark.idea: 利用大气下行辐射参考和发射率光谱平滑性实现无参考定标。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖精确的大气模型参数，实际场景中参数获取可能困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理渲染方程与图像恢复结合，为物理感知的底层视觉提供新范式。

zh.tldr: 提出基于HADAR物理模型的热红外高光谱图像恢复框架HAIR，利用温度-发射率-纹理三元组实现去噪、修复、定标和超分辨率。
zh.motivation: 现有方法忽略热红外物理机制，无法处理传感器特有的退化。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖精确的大气模型参数，实际场景中参数获取可能困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理渲染方程与图像恢复结合，为物理感知的底层视觉提供新范式。

en.tldr: Proposes HAIR, a physics-driven framework for thermal infrared hyperspectral image restoration using temperature-emissivity-texture triplets, achieving denoising, inpainting, calibration, and super-resolution.
en.motivation: Existing methods ignore thermal physics and fail to handle unique sensor degradations in TIR-HSI.
en.method: Combines HADAR rendering equation with atmospheric downwelling radiative transfer equation, employing a TeX decompose-synthesize strategy for physical consistency and noise resilience.
en.result: HAIR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods on DARPA Invisible Headlights dataset and in-lab FTIR measurements in both objective accuracy and visual quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage physical models for restoring thermal hyperspectral images, improving data quality.

idea_spark.idea: Uses atmospheric downwelling reference and spectral smoothness of emissivity for reference-free calibration.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accurate atmospheric model parameters, which may be difficult to obtain in real-world scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining physical rendering equations with image restoration provides a new paradigm for physics-aware low-level vision.

papers.title: Where Does Reasoning Break? Step-Level Hallucination Detection via Hidden-State Transport Geometry
papers.summary: Large language models hallucinate during multi-step reasoning, but most existing detectors operate at the trace level: they assign one confidence score to a full output, fail to localize the first error, and often require multiple sampled completions. We frame hallucination instead as a property of the hidden-state trajectory produced during a single forward pass. Correct reasoning moves through a stable manifold of locally coherent transitions; a first error appears as a localized excursion in transport cost away from this manifold. We operationalize this view with a label-conditioned teacher that builds a trace-specific contrastive PCA lens and scores each step with seven geometric transition features, and a deployable BiLSTM student distilled from the teacher that operates on raw hidden states without inference-time labels. We prove that contrastive PCA is the optimal projection for a transport-separation objective between first error and correct states, and that single-pass first error localization holds whenever the first error creates a positive transport margin over preceding correct transitions. On ProcessBench, PRM800K, HaluEval, and TruthfulQA, both models outperform entropy-based, probing-based, and attention-based baselines in-domain; the teacher transfers stably across language models and datasets, while the student collapses under shift, a gap our distillation theory predicts. These results recast step-level hallucination detection as a problem of trajectory dynamics and identify the central obstacle to deployment: preserving the contrastive transport margin under distribution shift.

ai.tldr: 提出通过隐藏状态轨迹的传输几何来检测大语言模型在多步推理中的第一步错误，并设计了教师-学生框架。
ai.motivation: 现有幻觉检测方法只能对完整输出给出置信度，无法定位第一步错误，且需要多次采样。
ai.method: 利用标签条件教师构建对比PCA透镜，提取七个几何过渡特征，再蒸馏为BiLSTM学生模型，在单次前向传播中定位第一步错误。
ai.result: 在多个基准上优于熵、探针和注意力基线；教师跨模型和数据集稳定迁移，但学生在分布偏移下失效。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户精确识别多步推理中首次出错的位置，提升模型可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 将隐藏状态轨迹视为流形，利用对比PCA分离正确与错误状态，实现单步检测。
idea_spark.risk: 学生模型对分布偏移敏感，实际部署时需持续适应新数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从动力系统视角重新定义幻觉，启发基于轨迹稳定性的检测方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将隐藏状态轨迹视为流形，利用对比PCA分离正确与错误状态，实现单步检测。
idea_spark.risk: 学生模型对分布偏移敏感，实际部署时需持续适应新数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从动力系统视角重新定义幻觉，启发基于轨迹稳定性的检测方法。

en.tldr: Proposes detecting the first error in multi-step reasoning of LLMs via hidden-state trajectory transport geometry, with a teacher-student framework.
en.motivation: Existing hallucination detectors assign a single confidence score to the full output, fail to localize the first error, and require multiple sampled completions.
en.method: Uses a label-conditioned teacher to build a contrastive PCA lens, extracts seven geometric transition features, and distills into a BiLSTM student for single-pass first-error localization.
en.result: Outperforms entropy, probing, and attention baselines on multiple benchmarks; teacher transfers stably across models and datasets, but student collapses under distribution shift.
en.help_to_user: Helps users precisely identify the first error in multi-step reasoning, improving model reliability.

idea_spark.idea: Treat hidden-state trajectories as a manifold and use contrastive PCA to separate correct from error states for step-level detection.
idea_spark.risk: The student model is sensitive to distribution shift, requiring continuous adaptation for deployment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reframes hallucination from a dynamical systems perspective, inspiring trajectory-stability-based detection methods.

papers.title: Decoding Product Codes and Staircase Codes with Iteration-Independent Weighting Coefficients
papers.summary: This paper presents an improved FEC decoder design outperforming Chase-Pyndiah decoding of product codes by $0.23$ dB. To achieve this, the decoder does not require iteration-dependent coefficients, making it implementation-friendly for sliding-window decoding of staircase codes.

ai.tldr: 提出一种改进的前向纠错解码器，在乘积码上比Chase-Pyndiah解码好0.23 dB，且无需迭代依赖系数，便于阶梯码的滑动窗解码实现。
ai.motivation: 现有Chase-Pyndiah解码需要迭代依赖系数，不利于硬件实现，尤其不适合阶梯码的滑动窗解码。
ai.method: 设计一种不依赖迭代系数的解码器，通过改进加权系数计算方式，在乘积码和阶梯码上实现性能提升。
ai.result: 相比Chase-Pyndiah解码，获得0.23 dB的编码增益，且解码器结构更简单、实现友好。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在不增加迭代复杂度的情况下提升FEC解码性能，适用于通信系统硬件设计。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将迭代无关加权系数应用于深度学习辅助的解码器，以简化训练和推理过程。
idea_spark.risk: 性能增益可能随码型或信道条件变化，需验证在不同场景下的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从硬件实现友好的角度重新设计传统迭代解码算法，可能带来意想不到的性能提升。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在不增加迭代复杂度的情况下提升FEC解码性能，适用于通信系统硬件设计。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从硬件实现友好的角度重新设计传统迭代解码算法，可能带来意想不到的性能提升。

en.tldr: This paper presents an improved FEC decoder that outperforms Chase-Pyndiah decoding of product codes by 0.23 dB without requiring iteration-dependent coefficients, making it implementation-friendly for sliding-window decoding of staircase codes.
en.motivation: Existing Chase-Pyndiah decoding relies on iteration-dependent coefficients, which complicates hardware implementation, especially for sliding-window decoding of staircase codes.
en.method: A decoder is designed with iteration-independent weighting coefficients, improving performance on product codes and staircase codes while simplifying the decoder structure.
en.result: Achieves a 0.23 dB coding gain over Chase-Pyndiah decoding with a more implementation-friendly design.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to improve FEC decoding performance without increasing iteration complexity, useful for hardware design in communication systems.

idea_spark.idea: Explore applying iteration-independent weighting to deep learning-assisted decoders to simplify training and inference.
idea_spark.risk: Performance gains may vary with code type or channel conditions; robustness needs verification across different scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Redesigning traditional iterative decoding algorithms from a hardware-friendly perspective may yield unexpected performance improvements.

papers.title: Generating synthetic computed tomography for radiotherapy: SynthRAD2025 challenge report
papers.summary: Radiation therapy (RT) requires precise dose delivery over multiple fractions, with CT fundamental for treatment planning due to its electron density information. Repeated CT acquisitions impose radiation exposure and logistical burdens, MRI lacks electron density, and cone-beam CT (CBCT) requires correction for dose calculation. Synthetic CT (sCT) generation addresses these by converting MRI or CBCT into CT-equivalent images with accurate Hounsfield Unit (HU) values, enabling MRI-only RT and CBCT-based adaptive workflows. Building on SynthRAD2023, SynthRAD2025 benchmarked sCT methods on 2,362 patients from five European centers across head and neck, thorax, and abdomen. Two tasks: MRI-to-CT (890 cases) and CBCT-to-CT (1,472 cases), evaluated via image similarity (MAE, PSNR, MS-SSIM), segmentation (Dice, HD95), and dosimetric metrics from photon and proton plans. With 803 participants and 12/13 valid submissions, Task 1 top performance reached MAE $64.8\pm21.3$ HU, PSNR $\sim$30 dB, MS-SSIM $\sim$0.936, Dice 0.79, photon $γ_{2\%/2\text{mm}}>98\%$, proton $γ\approx85\%$. Task 2 improved: MAE $48.3\pm13.4$ HU, PSNR 32.6 dB, MS-SSIM 0.968, Dice 0.86, photon $γ>99\%$, proton $γ\approx89\%$. Strong image--segmentation correlations ($ρ=0.78$--$0.79$) but moderate dose correlations confirmed image quality is insufficient as a dosimetric surrogate. Head-and-neck cases were most consistent; thoracic and abdominal cases showed greater variability. Residual errors at tissue interfaces propagate along beam paths, affecting proton dose more than photon. SynthRAD2025 demonstrates that deep learning yields clinically relevant sCTs, especially for CBCT-to-CT, while identifying persistent MRI-to-CT challenges and underscoring dose-based evaluation as essential for clinical validation.

ai.tldr: SynthRAD2025 挑战赛报告了基于深度学习的合成 CT 生成方法，在 2362 名患者数据上评估了 MRI 到 CT 和 CBCT 到 CT 两个任务，发现 CBCT 到 CT 性能更优，但图像质量不足以替代剂量学评估。
ai.motivation: 放疗需要多次 CT 扫描，但重复 CT 带来辐射和负担，MRI 缺乏电子密度信息，CBCT 需要校正。合成 CT 可解决这些问题，实现仅 MRI 放疗和基于 CBCT 的自适应工作流。
ai.method: 使用来自五个欧洲中心的 2362 名患者数据，分为 MRI 到 CT（890 例）和 CBCT 到 CT（1472 例）两个任务，通过图像相似度、分割和剂量学指标评估。
ai.result: 任务 1 最佳 MAE 64.8 HU，PSNR ~30 dB，MS-SSIM ~0.936，Dice 0.79；任务 2 更优：MAE 48.3 HU，PSNR 32.6 dB，MS-SSIM 0.968，Dice 0.86。图像与分割相关性高（ρ=0.78-0.79），但与剂量相关性中等，表明图像质量不能完全替代剂量学验证。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解当前合成 CT 生成技术的性能水平、主要挑战（如组织界面残留误差影响质子剂量）以及临床验证中剂量学评估的必要性。

idea_spark.idea: 结合剂量引导的损失函数或对抗训练，直接优化合成 CT 的剂量学准确性，而非仅依赖图像相似度。
idea_spark.risk: 剂量引导训练可能过度拟合特定治疗计划，降低泛化能力；计算成本高，且需要大量配对剂量数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用图像-剂量相关性分析，设计混合评估指标，在训练中同时优化图像质量和剂量学指标，可能提升临床适用性。

zh.tldr: SynthRAD2025 挑战赛报告了基于深度学习的合成 CT 生成方法，在 2362 名患者数据上评估了 MRI 到 CT 和 CBCT 到 CT 两个任务，发现 CBCT 到 CT 性能更优，但图像质量不足以替代剂量学评估。
zh.motivation: 放疗需要多次 CT 扫描，但重复 CT 带来辐射和负担，MRI 缺乏电子密度信息，CBCT 需要校正。合成 CT 可解决这些问题，实现仅 MRI 放疗和基于 CBCT 的自适应工作流。
zh.method: 使用来自五个欧洲中心的 2362 名患者数据，分为 MRI 到 CT（890 例）和 CBCT 到 CT（1472 例）两个任务，通过图像相似度、分割和剂量学指标评估。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解当前合成 CT 生成技术的性能水平、主要挑战（如组织界面残留误差影响质子剂量）以及临床验证中剂量学评估的必要性。
