en.tldr: Proposes SFRF framework that jointly handles registration and fusion of infrared and visible images in spatial-frequency domains via uncertainty estimation and thermal radiation distribution consistency, effectively mitigating registration error accumulation.
en.motivation: Existing infrared-visible fusion methods under unregistered conditions suffer from accumulated registration errors that severely degrade fusion results.
en.method: Builds a Multi-scale Iterative Registration (MIR) framework with uncertainty estimation at each scale; employs thermal radiation distribution consistency as frequency-domain supervision; designs a Dual-branch Spatial-Frequency Fusion (DSFF) module.
en.result: Achieves impressive performance on multiple datasets, improving registration accuracy and fusion quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how uncertainty-aware and frequency-domain constraints can address error accumulation in multi-modal registration and fusion.

idea_spark.idea: Using thermal radiation distribution consistency as frequency-domain supervision may inspire other physics-prior-based frequency alignment methods.
idea_spark.risk: Frequency-domain supervision may not apply to non-thermal infrared modalities; physical constraints need redesign.
idea_spark.inspiration: The idea of dynamic uncertainty estimation in multi-scale iterative registration can be generalized to any alignment task requiring stepwise refinement.

papers.title: Collision Dynamics of False-Vacuum Oscillons
papers.summary: We study the collision dynamics of localized oscillons in two classes of $(1+1)$-dimensional scalar field theories with metastable false vacua, a normal class with a positive quartic self-interaction term and an inverted class with a negative quartic term. We construct small-amplitude oscillon solutions around the false vacuum using the Fodor {\emph{et al.}} expansion, and show that the force between oscillons decays exponentially at large separation, with a strength modulated by their relative phase. Numerical simulations of two-oscillon collisions exhibit reflection, crossing, and formation of excited oscillons. Resonance windows occur, similar to those found in kink-antikink collisions. In the normal theory, if the oscillons have sufficient energy, the field can pass over a sphaleron barrier and evolve into a kink-antikink pair, initiating a phase transition to the true vacuum. We also simulate the collision of oscillons evolved from a slightly perturbed sphaleron.

ai.tldr: 研究了(1+1)维标量场理论中假真空振荡子的碰撞动力学，发现了反射、穿越、激发振荡子形成以及共振窗口等行为，并观察到通过sphaleron屏障的相变。
ai.motivation: 理解假真空振荡子碰撞的动力学行为及其在相变中的作用。
ai.method: 使用Fodor展开构造小振幅振荡子解，并通过数值模拟研究两振荡子碰撞。
ai.result: 振荡子间力随距离指数衰减，受相对相位调制；碰撞出现反射、穿越和激发振荡子；存在共振窗口；高能振荡子可越过sphaleron屏障引发相变。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了假真空振荡子碰撞的详细动力学图景，有助于理解早期宇宙或凝聚态系统中的非平衡相变。

idea_spark.idea: 利用振荡子碰撞的共振窗口控制相变速率，或设计实验模拟系统。
idea_spark.risk: 数值结果可能依赖特定参数和维度，推广需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 振荡子碰撞的共振行为类似于孤子碰撞，可启发新的相变控制策略。

en.tldr: We study the collision dynamics of false-vacuum oscillons in (1+1)-dimensional scalar field theories, revealing reflection, crossing, excited oscillon formation, resonance windows, and phase transitions via sphaleron barrier crossing.
en.motivation: To understand the collision dynamics of false-vacuum oscillons and their role in phase transitions.
en.method: Small-amplitude oscillon solutions are constructed using the Fodor expansion, and two-oscillon collisions are simulated numerically.
en.result: The force between oscillons decays exponentially with separation, modulated by relative phase; collisions exhibit reflection, crossing, and excited oscillon formation; resonance windows appear; energetic oscillons can cross the sphaleron barrier to trigger a phase transition.
en.help_to_user: Provides a detailed dynamical picture of false-vacuum oscillon collisions, useful for understanding nonequilibrium phase transitions in early universe or condensed matter systems.

idea_spark.idea: Use resonance windows in oscillon collisions to control phase transition rates or design experimental analogs.
idea_spark.risk: Numerical results may depend on specific parameters and dimensions; generalization requires caution.
idea_spark.inspiration: The resonant behavior of oscillon collisions resembles soliton collisions, inspiring new strategies for phase transition control.

papers.title: Ergodic Optimization and Ground States: a brief Introduction
papers.summary: Our goal in this short note is to briefly and succinctly describe some basic concepts and properties of Ergodic Optimization for readers unfamiliar with the subject. We avoid technical issues in order to provide a global overview of this topic. We will not attempt to cover all of the many contributions of various authors, who have greatly enriched the theory with invaluable results. The author has made a personal selection of the topics to be addressed, keeping in mind two main objectives: to motivate the reasons for studying the subject, and to describe schematically and pictorially its relationship with relevant concepts and properties of Statistical Mechanics, which is one of the sources of inspiration for the theory. We will not present new results or detailed proofs. Some examples will be provided. We describe some procedures that may help in obtaining explicit solutions. We present some references that by no means aim to exhaust the bibliography on the subject, where possible, minimizing the number of references.

en.tldr: This note briefly introduces basic concepts and properties of ergodic optimization and ground states, avoiding technical details, providing a global overview, and relating them to statistical mechanics.
en.motivation: To offer an accessible introduction to ergodic optimization for readers unfamiliar with the subject, and to motivate its study.
en.method: Conceptual descriptions, schematic illustrations, and a few examples are used in a non-technical manner; no new results or detailed proofs are presented.
en.result: Relationships between ergodic optimization and statistical mechanics concepts are outlined, along with procedures for obtaining explicit solutions.
en.help_to_user: Helps beginners quickly grasp the core ideas and application background of ergodic optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the partition function idea from statistical mechanics to design new algorithms for approximating ground states of complex systems.
idea_spark.risk: The non-technical overview may omit critical details, leading to unforeseen difficulties in practical applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: The cross-fertilization between statistical mechanics and dynamical systems inspires the introduction of a temperature parameter into optimization to escape local optima.

papers.title: Chewing gums, snakes and candle cakes
papers.summary: The aim of these lecture notes, based on lectures given by the second author at the CIME school in Cetraro, is to illustrate a range of ideas surrounding higher Teichmuller spaces of Riemann surfaces with marked boundaries through explicit and computationally tractable examples. After reviewing the classical Teichmuller space of hyperbolic Riemann surfaces with boundary and its combinatorial description in terms of Thurston shear coordinates on a fat-graph, we explain how the bordered cusped Teichmuller space arises as a confluent limit when two boundary components in the Riemann surface collide via the so-called chewing-gum move giving rise to a candle cake. We then revisit these constructions from the Fock-Goncharov perspective, explaining snake calculus for transport matrices in PSL_n(R) and explain how the chewing gum move is the inverse of amalgamation. Rather than focusing on formal proofs, our goal is to illustrate the underlying theorems and constructions in a concrete and intuitive way.

ai.tldr: 这些讲义通过具体可计算的例子，阐释了带标记边界的黎曼曲面的高阶Teichmüller空间，重点介绍了咀嚼口香糖移动和蜡烛蛋糕等构造。
ai.motivation: 通过显式例子直观理解高阶Teichmüller空间及其相关构造。
ai.method: 回顾经典Teichmüller空间及其Thurston剪切坐标描述，引入边界尖点Teichmüller空间作为汇聚极限，并从Fock-Goncharov角度重新审视。
ai.result: 展示了咀嚼口香糖移动是合并的逆操作，并解释了蛇形演算。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解高阶Teichmüller空间中的关键概念和构造。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从具体例子出发的直观方法可用于教学或探索新理论。

papers.title: DiffST: Spatiotemporal-Aware Diffusion for Real-World Space-Time Video Super-Resolution
papers.summary: Diffusion-based models have shown strong performance in video super-resolution (VSR) and video frame interpolation (VFI). However, their role in the coupled space-time video super-resolution (STVSR) setting remains limited. Existing diffusion-based STVSR approaches suffer from two issues: (1) low inference efficiency and (2) insufficient utilization of spatiotemporal information. These limitations impede deployment. To address these issues, we introduce DiffST, an efficient spatiotemporal-aware video diffusion framework for real-world STVSR. To improve efficiency, we adapt a pre-trained diffusion model for one-step sampling and process the entire video directly rather than operating on individual frames. Furthermore, to enhance spatiotemporal information utilization, we introduce cross-frame context aggregation (CFCA) and video representation guidance (VRG). The CFCA module aggregates information across multiple keyframes to produce intermediate frames. The VRG module extracts video-level global features to guide the diffusion process. Extensive experiments show that DiffST obtains leading results on real-world STVSR tasks. It also maintains high inference efficiency, running about 17$\times$ faster than previous diffusion-based STVSR methods. Code is available at: https://github.com/zhengchen1999/DiffST.

ai.tldr: 提出DiffST，一种高效时空感知视频扩散框架，用于真实世界时空视频超分辨率。
ai.motivation: 现有扩散STVSR方法推理效率低且时空信息利用不足。
ai.method: 适配预训练扩散模型实现单步采样，直接处理整个视频；引入跨帧上下文聚合和视频表示引导。
ai.result: 在真实STVSR任务上取得领先结果，推理速度比先前扩散方法快约17倍。
ai.help_to_user: 提供高效且高质量的时空视频超分辨率解决方案，便于实际部署。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从视频压缩和光流估计中借鉴跨帧信息聚合思想。

en.tldr: Proposes DiffST, an efficient spatiotemporal-aware video diffusion framework for real-world space-time video super-resolution.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion-based STVSR methods suffer from low inference efficiency and insufficient spatiotemporal information utilization.

en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and high-quality solution for space-time video super-resolution, suitable for real-world deployment.

idea_spark.idea: Leverages pre-trained diffusion model for one-step sampling, combined with cross-frame aggregation and video-level global feature guidance.
idea_spark.risk: One-step sampling may sacrifice some generative diversity; trade-off between efficiency and quality needs careful balancing.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from video compression and optical flow for cross-frame information aggregation.

papers.title: M2Retinexformer: Multi-Modal Retinexformer for Low-Light Image Enhancement
papers.summary: Low-light image enhancement is challenging due to complex degradations, including amplified noise, artifacts, and color distortion. While Retinex-based deep learning methods have achieved promising results, they primarily rely on single-modality RGB information. We propose M2Retinexformer (Multi-Modal Retinexformer), a novel framework that extends Retinexformer by incorporating depth cues, luminance priors, and semantic features within a progressive refinement pipeline. Depth provides geometric context that is invariant to lighting variations, while luminance and semantic features offer explicit guidance on brightness distribution and scene understanding. Modalities are extracted at multiple scales and fused through cross-attention, with adaptive gating dynamically balancing illumination-guided self-attention and cross-attention based on the reliability of auxiliary cues. Evaluations on the LOL, SID, SMID, and SDSD benchmarks demonstrate overall improvements over Retinexformer and recent state-of-the-art methods. Code and pretrained weights are available at https://github.com/YoussefAboelwafa/M2Retinexformer

ai.tldr: 提出M2Retinexformer框架，通过融合深度、亮度和语义等多模态信息，在低光图像增强任务上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有Retinex方法仅依赖RGB单模态信息，难以应对复杂退化（噪声、伪影、颜色失真）。
ai.method: 在Retinexformer基础上引入多尺度深度、亮度先验和语义特征，通过交叉注意力与自适应门控动态融合。
ai.result: 在LOL、SID、SMID、SDSD基准上取得整体改进，超越Retinexformer及最新方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供低光图像增强的实用方案，代码和预训练权重已开源。

idea_spark.idea: 利用深度图作为光照不变几何先验，结合亮度与语义引导注意力。
idea_spark.risk: 多模态输入在无深度传感器场景下需额外估计，可能引入误差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 自适应门控机制可根据模态可靠性动态调整融合权重，启发鲁棒多模态设计。

en.tldr: Proposes M2Retinexformer, a multi-modal framework integrating depth, luminance, and semantic features for low-light image enhancement, outperforming prior methods.
en.motivation: Existing Retinex methods rely solely on RGB modality, struggling with complex degradations like noise, artifacts, and color distortion.
en.method: Extends Retinexformer by incorporating multi-scale depth, luminance priors, and semantic features, fused via cross-attention and adaptive gating.
en.result: Achieves overall improvements on LOL, SID, SMID, and SDSD benchmarks, surpassing Retinexformer and recent state-of-the-art.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical solution for low-light enhancement with open-source code and pretrained weights.

idea_spark.idea: Uses depth as illumination-invariant geometric prior, combined with luminance and semantic guidance for attention.
idea_spark.risk: Requires depth estimation in scenarios without depth sensors, potentially introducing errors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Adaptive gating dynamically balances fusion based on modality reliability, inspiring robust multi-modal designs.

papers.title: State-Centric Decision Process
papers.summary: Language environments such as web browsers, code terminals, and interactive simulations emit raw text rather than states, and provide none of the runtime structure that MDP analysis requires. No explicit state space, no observation-to-state mapping, no certified transitions, and no termination criterion. We introduce the State-Centric Decision Process (SDP), a runtime framework that constructs these missing inputs by having the agent build them, predicate by predicate, as it acts. At each step the agent commits to a natural-language predicate describing how the world should look, takes an action to make it true, and checks the observation against it. Predicates that pass become certified states, and the resulting trajectory carries the four objects language environments do not provide, namely a task-induced state space, an observation-to-state mapping, certified transitions, and a termination criterion. We evaluate SDP on five benchmarks spanning planning, scientific exploration, web reasoning, and multi-hop question answering. SDP achieves the best training-free results on all five, with the advantage widening as the horizon grows. The certified trajectories additionally support analyses unavailable to reactive agents, including per-predicate credit assignment, failure localization, partial-progress measurement, and modular operator replacement.

ai.tldr: 提出状态中心决策过程（SDP），让智能体在语言环境中通过自然语言谓词逐步构建状态空间、观测映射、认证转移和终止条件，实现无训练的最优结果。
ai.motivation: 语言环境（如浏览器、代码终端）只输出原始文本，缺乏MDP所需的状态空间、观测映射、认证转移和终止条件，导致传统强化学习无法直接应用。
ai.method: 智能体每一步承诺一个自然语言谓词描述期望世界状态，执行动作使谓词成立，检查观测是否匹配，通过谓词构建认证状态，形成完整的决策过程。
ai.result: 在五个基准（规划、科学探索、网页推理、多跳问答）上均取得无训练最佳结果，且优势随任务长度增加而扩大；认证轨迹支持信用分配、故障定位、进度测量和模块替换。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在缺乏结构化状态的语言环境中构建可认证的决策过程，提升智能体在复杂任务中的可靠性和可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 将谓词承诺机制与大型语言模型结合，让智能体自主定义并验证状态，实现零样本规划与推理。
idea_spark.risk: 谓词设计可能不完整或模糊，导致状态构建错误；长序列中谓词一致性维护困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类通过语言描述目标并逐步验证的认知过程启发，将外部环境的结构化缺失转化为智能体主动构建的机会。

en.tldr: Introduces State-Centric Decision Process (SDP), where agents build state space, observation mapping, certified transitions, and termination criterion via natural-language predicates, achieving best training-free results on five benchmarks.
en.motivation: Language environments emit raw text without the structured components (state space, observation mapping, transitions, termination) required by MDP, hindering direct application of reinforcement learning.
en.method: At each step, the agent commits to a natural-language predicate describing the desired world state, takes an action to satisfy it, and checks the observation against the predicate, thereby constructing certified states and a complete decision process.
en.result: SDP achieves state-of-the-art training-free performance on five benchmarks (planning, scientific exploration, web reasoning, multi-hop QA), with advantage increasing over longer horizons; certified trajectories enable credit assignment, failure localization, progress measurement, and modular operator replacement.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to build certifiable decision processes in unstructured language environments, enhancing agent reliability and interpretability in complex tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Combine predicate commitment with large language models to let agents autonomously define and verify states, enabling zero-shot planning and reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Predicate design may be incomplete or ambiguous, leading to state construction errors; maintaining predicate consistency over long sequences is challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the human cognitive process of describing goals in language and verifying them step by step, turning the lack of structure in external environments into an opportunity for active construction.

papers.title: Causality-Aware End-to-End Autonomous Driving via Ego-Centric Joint Scene Modeling
papers.summary: End-to-end autonomous driving, which bypasses traditional modular pipelines by directly predicting future trajectories from sensor inputs, has recently achieved substantial progress. However, existing methods often overlook the causal inter-dependencies in ego-vehicle planning, ignoring the reciprocal relations between the ego vehicle and surrounding agents. This causal oversight leads to inconsistent and unreliable trajectory predictions, especially in interaction-critical scenarios where ego decisions and neighboring agent behaviors must be reasoned about jointly. To address this limitation, we propose CaAD, a Causality-aware end-to-end Autonomous Driving framework that captures these dependencies within a shared latent scene representation. First, we propose a ego-centric joint-causal modeling module that builds on the marginal prediction branch, and learns causal dependencies between the ego vehicle and interaction-relevant agents. Second, we employ a causality-aware policy alignment stage implemented with joint-mode embeddings to align the stochastic ego policy with planning-oriented closed-loop feedback computed from surrounding traffic and map context. On the Bench2Drive and NAVSIM benchmarks, CaAD demonstrates strong closed-loop planning performance, achieving a Driving Score of 87.53 and Success Rate of 71.81 on Bench2Drive, and a PDMS of 91.1 on NAVSIM.

ai.tldr: 提出CaAD框架，通过自车中心联合因果建模和因果感知策略对齐，实现端到端自动驾驶的闭环规划，在Bench2Drive和NAVSIM上取得领先性能。
ai.motivation: 现有端到端自动驾驶方法忽略自车规划中的因果相互依赖，导致交互关键场景下轨迹预测不一致且不可靠。
ai.method: 设计自车中心联合因果建模模块，在边缘预测分支上学习自车与交互相关智能体间的因果依赖；采用联合模式嵌入实现因果感知策略对齐，将随机自车策略与闭环反馈对齐。
ai.result: 在Bench2Drive上驾驶分数87.53、成功率71.81，在NAVSIM上PDMS达91.1。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过显式建模因果依赖提升端到端自动驾驶在复杂交互场景下的规划一致性和可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 将因果图引入自车与周围智能体的联合场景表示，并通过闭环反馈对齐策略，避免因果混淆。
idea_spark.risk: 因果结构假设可能不适用于所有场景，且闭环反馈计算可能引入额外延迟。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断角度重新审视端到端规划，启发将结构化因果先验融入隐式场景建模。

en.tldr: Proposes CaAD, a causality-aware end-to-end autonomous driving framework with ego-centric joint causal modeling and causality-aware policy alignment, achieving strong closed-loop planning performance on Bench2Drive and NAVSIM.
en.motivation: Existing end-to-end methods overlook causal inter-dependencies in ego planning, leading to inconsistent trajectory predictions in interaction-critical scenarios.
en.method: Designs an ego-centric joint-causal modeling module to learn causal dependencies between ego and interaction-relevant agents; employs joint-mode embeddings for causality-aware policy alignment with closed-loop feedback.
en.result: Achieves Driving Score 87.53 and Success Rate 71.81 on Bench2Drive, and PDMS 91.1 on NAVSIM.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how explicit causal dependency modeling improves planning consistency and reliability in complex interactive driving scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce causal graphs into joint scene representation of ego and surrounding agents, and align policy via closed-loop feedback to avoid causal confounding.
idea_spark.risk: Causal structure assumptions may not generalize to all scenarios, and closed-loop feedback computation may introduce latency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting end-to-end planning from a causal inference perspective inspires incorporating structured causal priors into implicit scene modeling.

papers.title: GenCape: Structure-Inductive Generative Modeling for Category-Agnostic Pose Estimation
papers.summary: Category-agnostic pose estimation (CAPE) aims to localize keypoints on query images from arbitrary categories, using only a few annotated support examples for guidance. Recent approaches either treat keypoints as isolated entities or rely on manually defined skeleton priors, which are costly to annotate and inherently inflexible across diverse categories. Such oversimplification limits the model's capacity to capture instance-wise structural cues critical for accurate pixel-level localization. To overcome these limitations, we propose GenCape, a Generative-based framework for CAPE that infers keypoint relationships solely from image-based support inputs, without additional textual descriptions or predefined skeletons. Our framework consists of two principal components: an iterative Structure-aware Variational Autoencoder (i-SVAE) and a Compositional Graph Transfer (CGT) module. The former infers soft, instance-specific adjacency matrices from support features through variational inference, embedded layer-wise into the Graph Transformer Decoder for progressive structural priors refinement. The latter adaptively aggregates multiple latent graphs into a query-aware structure via Bayesian fusion and attention-based reweighting, enhancing resilience to visual uncertainty and support-induced bias. This structure-aware design facilitates effective message propagation among keypoints and promotes semantic alignment across object categories with diverse keypoint topologies. Experimental results on the MP-100 dataset show that our method achieves substantial gains over graph-support baselines under both 1- and 5-shot settings, while maintaining competitive performance against text-support counterparts.

ai.tldr: 提出GenCape，一种基于生成式模型的类别无关姿态估计方法，通过结构归纳从少量支持样本中推断关键点关系，无需预定义骨架。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将关键点视为孤立实体或依赖人工定义的骨架先验，成本高且灵活性差，难以捕捉实例级结构信息。
ai.method: 包含迭代结构感知变分自编码器（i-SVAE）和组合图迁移（CGT）模块，前者从支持特征推断软邻接矩阵，后者通过贝叶斯融合和注意力重加权聚合多潜在图。
ai.result: 在MP-100数据集上，1-shot和5-shot设置下均优于基于图的基线方法，与基于文本的方法性能相当。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用生成式模型和图结构实现跨类别关键点定位，无需额外标注。

idea_spark.idea: 将i-SVAE的迭代细化机制与CGT的查询自适应聚合结合，实现动态结构先验学习。
idea_spark.risk: 对支持样本质量敏感，复杂拓扑下贝叶斯融合可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从变分推断和图注意力机制中汲取灵感，实现无监督结构发现。

zh.motivation: 现有方法将关键点视为孤立实体或依赖人工定义的骨架先验，成本高且灵活性差，难以捕捉实例级结构信息。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用生成式模型和图结构实现跨类别关键点定位，无需额外标注。

idea_spark.risk: 对支持样本质量敏感，复杂拓扑下贝叶斯融合可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从变分推断和图注意力机制中汲取灵感，实现无监督结构发现。

en.tldr: Proposes GenCape, a generative framework for category-agnostic pose estimation that infers keypoint relationships from few support samples without predefined skeletons.
en.motivation: Existing methods treat keypoints as isolated entities or rely on costly manual skeleton priors, limiting flexibility and structural cue capture.
en.method: Consists of an iterative Structure-aware VAE (i-SVAE) inferring soft adjacency matrices from support features, and a Compositional Graph Transfer (CGT) module aggregating multiple latent graphs via Bayesian fusion and attention reweighting.
en.result: Outperforms graph-support baselines on MP-100 under 1- and 5-shot settings, competitive with text-support methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how generative modeling and graph structures enable cross-category keypoint localization without extra annotations.

idea_spark.idea: Combine i-SVAE's iterative refinement with CGT's query-adaptive aggregation for dynamic structural prior learning.
idea_spark.risk: Sensitive to support sample quality; Bayesian fusion may introduce noise in complex topologies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from variational inference and graph attention mechanisms for unsupervised structure discovery.

papers.title: Drag within Prior Distribution: Text-Conditioned Point-Based Image Editing within Distribution Constraints
papers.summary: Diffusion-based point editing methods have gained significant traction in image editing tasks due to their ability to manipulate image semantics and fine details by applying localized perturbations on the manifold of noise latent. However, these approaches face several limitations. Traditional point-based editing relies on pairs of handle and target points to define motion trajectories, which can introduce ambiguity or unnecessary alterations. Furthermore, when the distance between the handle and target points is large, the accumulated perturbations often cause the noise latent deviation from inversion score trajectory, resulting in unnatural artifacts. To address these issues in global editing tasks, we introduce a CLIP-based model to evaluate and guide intermediate editing steps, ensuring that the generated results remain both semantically aligned. Additionally, we propose a prior-preservation loss that constrains the optimized latent code to stay within the sampling space of the diffusion prior, improving consistency with the original data distribution, to ensure the model generates images along a familiar score trajectory. For fine-grained tasks, we present a directionally-weighted point tracking mechanism that steers the editing process toward the target direction within similar feature regions. This improves both the tracking accuracy and generation quality, while also reducing the editing time.

ai.tldr: 提出一种文本条件点式图像编辑方法，通过CLIP模型和先验保持损失约束编辑过程，解决传统点编辑中的语义偏差和伪影问题。
ai.motivation: 传统点编辑依赖手柄-目标点对定义运动轨迹，当距离较大时累积扰动导致噪声潜变量偏离反演轨迹，产生不自然伪影。
ai.method: 引入CLIP模型评估中间编辑步骤的语义对齐性，提出先验保持损失约束潜变量在扩散先验采样空间内，并设计方向加权点跟踪机制。
ai.result: 在全局编辑中保持语义一致性，在细粒度编辑中提高跟踪精度和生成质量，同时减少编辑时间。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户通过文本和点交互实现更自然、语义一致的图像编辑，减少手动调整。

idea_spark.idea: 结合CLIP语义引导和方向加权跟踪，可扩展至视频帧序列编辑。
idea_spark.risk: CLIP模型可能引入文本-图像对齐偏差，影响编辑自由度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用分布约束防止潜变量漂移，启发其他生成任务中的稳定性改进。

zh.tldr: 提出一种文本条件点式图像编辑方法，通过CLIP模型和先验保持损失约束编辑过程，解决传统点编辑中的语义偏差和伪影问题。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户通过文本和点交互实现更自然、语义一致的图像编辑，减少手动调整。

idea_spark.idea: 结合CLIP语义引导和方向加权跟踪，可扩展至视频帧序列编辑。
idea_spark.risk: CLIP模型可能引入文本-图像对齐偏差，影响编辑自由度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用分布约束防止潜变量漂移，启发其他生成任务中的稳定性改进。

en.tldr: Proposes a text-conditioned point-based image editing method that uses a CLIP model and prior-preservation loss to constrain the editing process, addressing semantic misalignment and artifacts in traditional point editing.
en.motivation: Traditional point editing relies on handle-target point pairs to define motion trajectories; large distances cause accumulated perturbations that deviate noise latents from inversion score trajectories, leading to unnatural artifacts.
en.method: Introduces a CLIP-based model to evaluate intermediate editing steps for semantic alignment, a prior-preservation loss to keep latents within the diffusion prior sampling space, and a directionally-weighted point tracking mechanism.
en.result: Achieves semantic consistency in global editing, improves tracking accuracy and generation quality in fine-grained tasks, while reducing editing time.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to perform more natural and semantically consistent image editing via text and point interactions, reducing manual adjustments.

idea_spark.idea: Combining CLIP semantic guidance with directional weighted tracking could extend to video frame sequence editing.
idea_spark.risk: The CLIP model may introduce text-image alignment biases, limiting editing freedom.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using distribution constraints to prevent latent drift inspires stability improvements in other generative tasks.

papers.title: On the Size Complexity and Decidability of First-Order Progression
papers.summary: Progression, the task of updating a knowledge base to reflect action effects, generally requires second-order logic. Identifying first-order special cases, by restricting either the knowledge base or action effects, has long been a central topic in reasoning about actions. It is known that local-effect, normal, and acyclic actions, three increasingly expressive classes, admit first-order progression. However, a systematic analysis of the size of such progressions, crucial for practical applications, has been missing. In this paper, using the framework of Situation Calculus, we show that under reasonable assumptions, first-order progression for these action classes grows only polynomially. Moreover, we show that when the KB belongs to decidable fragments such as two-variable first-order logic or universal theories with constants, the progression remains within the same fragment, ensuring decidability and practical applicability.

ai.tldr: 本文在情景演算框架下，系统分析了局部效应、正规和循环三类动作的一阶进展的规模复杂度，证明其多项式增长，并确保在可判定片段内进展保持可判定性。
ai.motivation: 一阶进展的规模复杂度对实际应用至关重要，但此前缺乏系统分析。
ai.method: 在情景演算中，对三类动作类进行形式化分析，证明进展规模多项式增长，并验证在可判定片段（如两变量一阶逻辑）中进展保持可判定性。
ai.result: 在合理假设下，一阶进展规模多项式增长；当知识库属于可判定片段时，进展仍在该片段内，保证可判定性和实际可用性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解一阶进展在推理动作影响时的计算复杂度和可判定性边界，指导实际知识库更新系统的设计。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将进展限制在更丰富的可判定片段（如带等词的两变量逻辑）以扩展适用场景。
idea_spark.risk: 实际动作效果可能超出本文假设，导致进展规模超多项式增长或不可判定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于将动作类与逻辑片段结合以保持可判定性，可尝试类似方法处理其他推理任务（如规划验证）。

zh.tldr: 本文在情景演算框架下，系统分析了局部效应、正规和循环三类动作的一阶进展的规模复杂度，证明其多项式增长，并确保在可判定片段内进展保持可判定性。
zh.motivation: 一阶进展的规模复杂度对实际应用至关重要，但此前缺乏系统分析。
zh.method: 在情景演算中，对三类动作类进行形式化分析，证明进展规模多项式增长，并验证在可判定片段（如两变量一阶逻辑）中进展保持可判定性。
zh.result: 在合理假设下，一阶进展规模多项式增长；当知识库属于可判定片段时，进展仍在该片段内，保证可判定性和实际可用性。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解一阶进展在推理动作影响时的计算复杂度和可判定性边界，指导实际知识库更新系统的设计。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将进展限制在更丰富的可判定片段（如带等词的两变量逻辑）以扩展适用场景。
idea_spark.risk: 实际动作效果可能超出本文假设，导致进展规模超多项式增长或不可判定。

en.tldr: This paper systematically analyzes the size complexity of first-order progression for local-effect, normal, and acyclic actions in the Situation Calculus, proving polynomial growth and ensuring decidability within decidable fragments.
en.motivation: The size complexity of first-order progression is crucial for practical applications but lacked systematic analysis.
en.method: Formal analysis within the Situation Calculus for three action classes, proving polynomial growth of progression size and verifying that progression stays within decidable fragments (e.g., two-variable first-order logic).
en.result: Under reasonable assumptions, first-order progression size grows polynomially; when the KB belongs to decidable fragments, progression remains in the same fragment, ensuring decidability and practical applicability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the computational complexity and decidability boundaries of first-order progression in reasoning about action effects, guiding the design of practical knowledge base update systems.

idea_spark.idea: Explore restricting progression to richer decidable fragments (e.g., two-variable logic with equality) to extend applicability.
idea_spark.risk: Real-world action effects may exceed the paper's assumptions, leading to super-polynomial growth or undecidability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by combining action classes with logical fragments to maintain decidability, similar approaches could be applied to other reasoning tasks (e.g., planning verification).

papers.title: EvoGround: Self-Evolving Video Agents for Video Temporal Grounding
papers.summary: Video temporal grounding (VTG) takes an untrimmed video and a natural-language query as input and localizes the temporal moment that best matches the query. Existing methods rely on large, task-specific datasets requiring costly manual annotation. We introduce EvoGround, a framework of two coupled self-evolving agents, a proposer and a solver, that learn temporal grounding from raw videos without any human-labeled data. The proposer generates query--moment pairs from raw videos, while the solver learns to ground them and feeds back signals that improve the proposer in return. Through this self-reinforcing reinforcement-learning loop, the two agents are initialized from the same backbone and mutually improve across iterations. Trained on 2.5K unlabeled videos, EvoGround matches or surpasses fully supervised models across multiple VTG benchmarks, while emerging as a state-of-the-art fine-grained video captioner without manual labels.

ai.tldr: 提出EvoGround框架，通过两个自演化智能体（提议者和求解者）在无标注视频上学习时间定位，性能媲美全监督方法。
ai.motivation: 现有视频时间定位方法依赖大量人工标注数据，成本高昂。
ai.method: 构建提议者-求解者双智能体系统，通过强化学习循环相互改进，从原始视频中自动生成查询-时刻对并学习定位。
ai.result: 在2.5K无标注视频上训练，在多个基准上匹配或超越全监督模型，并成为无需标注的细粒度视频描述器。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需人工标注即可进行视频时间定位和细粒度描述的方法，降低数据成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将提议者与求解者的反馈循环扩展到多模态场景，例如结合音频或文本线索。
idea_spark.risk: 自演化循环可能收敛到次优解，需设计正则化或多样性奖励。

en.tldr: EvoGround uses two self-evolving agents (proposer and solver) to learn video temporal grounding from raw videos without human labels, matching or surpassing fully supervised methods.
en.motivation: Existing video temporal grounding methods rely on large, costly manually annotated datasets.
en.method: A proposer-solver dual-agent framework with a reinforcement learning loop that generates query-moment pairs from raw videos and iteratively improves both agents.
en.result: Trained on 2.5K unlabeled videos, it matches or exceeds fully supervised models on multiple benchmarks and emerges as a state-of-the-art fine-grained video captioner without manual labels.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method for video temporal grounding and fine-grained captioning without human annotation, reducing data cost.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the proposer-solver feedback loop to multimodal settings, e.g., incorporating audio or text cues.
idea_spark.risk: The self-reinforcing loop may converge to suboptimal solutions; regularization or diversity rewards may be needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by self-play in reinforcement learning, using mutual improvement between agents to replace human annotation.

papers.title: Respecting Self-Uncertainty in On-Policy Self-Distillation for Efficient LLM Reasoning
papers.summary: On-policy self-distillation trains a reasoning model on its own rollouts while a teacher, often the same model conditioned on privileged context, provides dense token-level supervision. Existing objectives typically weight the teacher's token-level signal uniformly across a chain-of-thought sequence, despite substantial variation in the entropy of the teacher's predictive distribution. We propose EGRSD (Entropy-Guided Reinforced Self-Distillation), which unifies token-level updates through three signals: a reward-grounded direction, a teacher-student likelihood-ratio magnitude, and the proposed teacher-entropy confidence gate that down-weights high-entropy token positions while maintaining a nonzero lower bound on every token weight. We further introduce CL-EGRSD, a causal-lookahead variant that distinguishes sustained high-entropy spans from transient high-entropy positions whose following context rapidly becomes low entropy. Experiments with Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B in thinking mode show that EGRSD and CL-EGRSD advance the accuracy-length frontier among the compared trainable methods.

ai.tldr: 提出熵引导强化自蒸馏方法，通过教师熵置信门控降低高熵token权重，提升LLM推理效率。
ai.motivation: 现有自蒸馏方法对教师token级信号统一加权，忽略了教师预测分布熵的显著变化。
ai.method: EGRSD融合奖励方向、似然比幅度和教师熵置信门控；CL-EGRSD区分持续高熵与瞬时高熵位置。
ai.result: 在Qwen3-4B/8B上，EGRSD和CL-EGRSD在准确率-长度前沿上优于对比方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用不确定性信息改进自蒸馏，提升推理模型效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将教师熵置信门控与因果前瞻结合，动态调整token权重。
idea_spark.risk: 高熵位置权重过低可能丢失关键推理步骤。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从教师不确定性中提取信号，而非仅依赖硬标签或固定权重。

en.tldr: Proposes entropy-guided reinforced self-distillation that down-weights high-entropy token positions via a teacher-entropy confidence gate, improving LLM reasoning efficiency.
en.motivation: Existing self-distillation methods weight teacher token-level signals uniformly, ignoring substantial variation in teacher predictive entropy.
en.method: EGRSD unifies reward-grounded direction, likelihood-ratio magnitude, and teacher-entropy confidence gate; CL-EGRSD distinguishes sustained vs. transient high-entropy spans.
en.result: On Qwen3-4B/8B, EGRSD and CL-EGRSD advance the accuracy-length frontier among compared trainable methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage uncertainty information to improve self-distillation for efficient reasoning models.

idea_spark.idea: Combine teacher-entropy confidence gating with causal lookahead to dynamically adjust token weights.
idea_spark.risk: Overly down-weighting high-entropy positions may discard critical reasoning steps.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extract signals from teacher uncertainty rather than relying solely on hard labels or fixed weights.

papers.title: Large Language Models for Agentic NetOps and AIOps: Architectures, Evaluation, and Safety
papers.summary: Large language models are increasingly being used to support network operations (NetOps) and artificial intelligence for IT operations (AIOps), including incident investigation, root-cause analysis, configuration synthesis, and limited self-healing. In both NetOps and AIOps, this shift is changing how tasks are managed. Agent-based operations work as workflows, from gathering evidence to taking action, following permissions, policies, and checks, and providing rollback options when necessary. This is crucial because operational decisions can have instant impacts. To make the argument concrete, we organise the relevant literature around the hierarchy of autonomy, tool scope, evidence traces, and assurance contracts. These contracts define what an agent may observe, propose, and execute. They also define the checks that must pass before any action is allowed. A consistent pattern appears across work on telemetry query recommendation, diagnosis, root-cause analysis, configuration synthesis, change planning, and limited self-healing. Operational reliability does not come chiefly from the model itself. It depends on the machinery around the model. We also argue that evaluation should go beyond static question answering. Agentic NetOps and AIOps systems require workflow-centred evaluation, including trace quality, bounded tool use, safe proposal generation, replay in sandboxed environments, and canary trials with rollback-aware scoring. Without these measures, a system may appear robust yet remain too fragile. Finally, we examine security, privacy, and governance risks that become acute when agents sit close to operational control surfaces. Taken together, the survey concludes that progress in intelligent NetOps and AIOps will depend on treating autonomy as a constrained operational control problem, whose outputs must be reliable, auditable, and securely deployable.

ai.tldr: 综述了大语言模型在智能网络运维和AIOps中的应用，强调自主性需受约束、评估需面向工作流，并讨论了安全与治理风险。
ai.motivation: 网络运维和AIOps中操作决策影响即时，现有模型缺乏可靠性和可审计性，需要系统化的自主性控制与评估框架。
ai.method: 围绕自主性层级、工具范围、证据轨迹和保证合同组织文献，提出工作流中心评估方法，包括痕迹质量、受限工具使用、安全提案生成、沙盒回放和金丝雀试验。
ai.result: 操作可靠性主要来自模型外围机制而非模型本身；评估需超越静态问答；安全风险在靠近控制面时加剧。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和工程师理解LLM在NetOps/AIOps中的架构选择、评估指标和安全部署要点。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个基于保证合同的自主运维框架，将操作权限、观察范围和回滚策略显式编码为可审计的合约。
idea_spark.risk: 合约可能过于严格限制模型灵活性，或合约本身存在漏洞导致安全绕过。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从软件工程中的契约式设计（Design by Contract）和混沌工程中获得启发，将运维自主性视为受控实验。

en.tldr: A survey on using large language models for agentic NetOps and AIOps, emphasizing constrained autonomy, workflow-centered evaluation, and safety risks.
en.motivation: Operational decisions in NetOps and AIOps have immediate impact, requiring reliable and auditable systems beyond static QA.
en.method: Organizes literature around hierarchy of autonomy, tool scope, evidence traces, and assurance contracts; proposes workflow-centered evaluation including trace quality, bounded tool use, safe proposal generation, sandbox replay, and canary trials with rollback-aware scoring.

en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and engineers understand architecture choices, evaluation metrics, and safe deployment for LLMs in NetOps/AIOps.

idea_spark.idea: Design an autonomous operations framework based on assurance contracts that explicitly encode operational permissions, observation scope, and rollback strategies as auditable contracts.
idea_spark.risk: Contracts may overly restrict model flexibility, or contract vulnerabilities could lead to security bypasses.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Design by Contract in software engineering and chaos engineering, treating operational autonomy as controlled experiments.

papers.title: Pyramid Self-contrastive Learning Framework for Test-time Ultrasound Image Denoising
papers.summary: The inherent electronic and speckle noise complicates clinical interpretation of ultrasound images. Conventional denoising methods rely on explicit noise assumptions whose validity diminishes under composite noise conditions. Learning-based methods require massive labeled data and model parameters. These pre-defined and pre-trained manners entail an inevitable domain shift in complex in vivo environments, so they are limited to a specific noise type and often blur structural details. In this study, we propose a pure test-time training framework for one-shot ultrasound image denoising and apply it to synthetic aperture ultrasound (SAU), which synthesizes transmit focus from sub-aperture transmissions. Our Aperture-to-Aperture (A2A) framework disentangles anatomical similarity and noise randomness from shuffled sub-apertures through self-contrastive learning in pyramid latent spaces. The clean image is then decoded from the anatomy space, while discarding the noise space. A2A is trained at test time on one noisy sample of SAU signals, so it fundamentally eliminates the domain shift and pretraining costs. Simulation experiments, including electronic noise levels of 0 to 30 dB and different inclusion geometries, demonstrated an improvement of 69.3% SNR and 34.4% CNR by A2A. The in vivo results showed 84.8% SNR and 25.7% CNR gains using only two aperture data of the heart in six echocardiographic views, liver, and kidney. A2A delivers clear images/signals across diverse imaging targets and configurations, paving the way for more reliable anatomical visualization and functional assessment by ultrasound.

ai.tldr: 提出一种纯测试时训练框架A2A，通过自对比学习在金字塔潜空间中分离解剖相似性与噪声随机性，实现单样本超声图像去噪，消除域偏移并提升信噪比。
ai.motivation: 传统超声去噪方法依赖显式噪声假设或大量标注数据，在复杂体内环境中存在域偏移，且易模糊结构细节。
ai.method: 利用子孔径数据构建Aperture-to-Aperture框架，在金字塔潜空间中进行自对比学习，将解剖特征与噪声解耦，仅从解剖空间解码干净图像。
ai.result: 仿真实验SNR提升69.3%，CNR提升34.4%；体内实验（心脏、肝脏、肾脏）SNR提升84.8%，CNR提升25.7%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解一种无需预训练、适应性强、能保留结构细节的超声图像去噪新方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将金字塔潜空间解耦策略应用于视频去噪或多模态图像融合。
idea_spark.risk: 测试时训练的计算开销可能限制实时应用；对子孔径数据的依赖可能不适用于所有超声系统。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用数据自身结构（如子孔径）进行自监督学习，避免域偏移，启发无监督域适应新思路。

idea_spark.risk: 测试时训练的计算开销可能限制实时应用；对子孔径数据的依赖可能不适用于所有超声系统。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用数据自身结构（如子孔径）进行自监督学习，避免域偏移，启发无监督域适应新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes a pure test-time training framework A2A that disentangles anatomy and noise via self-contrastive learning in pyramid latent spaces for one-shot ultrasound denoising, eliminating domain shift and improving SNR.
en.motivation: Conventional denoising methods rely on explicit noise assumptions or large labeled data, causing domain shift in complex in vivo environments and blurring structural details.
en.method: Uses sub-aperture data to build an Aperture-to-Aperture framework with self-contrastive learning in pyramid latent spaces, decoupling anatomical features from noise and decoding clean images from the anatomy space.
en.result: Simulation: 69.3% SNR and 34.4% CNR improvement; in vivo (heart, liver, kidney): 84.8% SNR and 25.7% CNR gains.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a novel ultrasound denoising method that requires no pre-training, adapts well, and preserves structural details.

idea_spark.idea: Apply pyramid latent space decoupling to video denoising or multi-modal image fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Test-time training computational cost may limit real-time applications; dependence on sub-aperture data may not suit all ultrasound systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging data's own structure (e.g., sub-apertures) for self-supervised learning avoids domain shift, inspiring new unsupervised domain adaptation approaches.

papers.title: SimART: A Unified and Open Real-world Multimodal Simulation Platform for 6G Integrated Sensing and Communication
papers.summary: Research on sixth-generation (6G) integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) increasingly depends on multimodal datasets. These datasets need to jointly characterize wireless propagation, onboard sensing, and platform mobility. Existing tools cover only part of these aspects. Robotics simulators model physics and perception but not site-specific channels, while ray tracing and link level tools lack vehicle dynamics and onboard sensors. Combining them manually leads to workflows that are fragile and hard to reproduce. Rather than introducing another standalone simulator, this article presents SimART. It integrates mature robotics, ray tracing, and wireless evaluation engines into a single reproducible pipeline. The key idea is a robot operating system (ROS) backbone that both synchronizes and organizes all multimodal streams. A shared clock, a common coordinate frame, and timestamped messages keep the streams aligned in time and space, and a single rosbag recording captures the full session into one reproducible file. This design decouples the sensing front end from the wireless back end, so that any ROS-compatible simulator can be plugged in while reusing the same back end across aerial, ground, indoor, and maritime ISAC settings. On top of this backbone, SimART contributes a scene construction pipeline that converts both OpenStreetMap extracts and user-defined layouts into spatially aligned visual and electromagnetic assets, and a channel knowledge map (CKM) generator that aggregates ray tracing and system level outputs into spatial priors for ISAC algorithms. A case study on vision and position aided beam prediction demonstrates the utility of the platform. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/guchuanv-alt/SimART.

ai.tldr: SimART是一个统一且开放的多模态仿真平台，用于6G集成感知与通信研究，通过ROS骨干同步无线传播、感知和移动性数据。
ai.motivation: 现有工具无法同时覆盖无线传播、感知和移动性，手动组合脆弱且难以复现。
ai.method: 基于ROS骨干同步多模态流，集成机器人、射线追踪和无线评估引擎，构建场景管道和信道知识图生成器。
ai.result: 案例研究显示平台在视觉和位置辅助波束预测中的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员在统一可复现的管道中测试ISAC算法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用信道知识图作为空间先验提升感知辅助通信性能。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖ROS生态，可能限制非ROS兼容工具的集成。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将机器人仿真与无线通信仿真深度融合，实现端到端可复现。

en.tldr: SimART is a unified and open multimodal simulation platform for 6G integrated sensing and communication, using a ROS backbone to synchronize wireless propagation, sensing, and mobility data.
en.motivation: Existing tools cover only part of the aspects; manual combination is fragile and hard to reproduce.
en.method: Integrates robotics, ray tracing, and wireless evaluation engines via a ROS backbone, with scene construction and channel knowledge map generation.
en.result: A case study demonstrates utility in vision and position aided beam prediction.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers test ISAC algorithms in a reproducible pipeline.

idea_spark.idea: Use channel knowledge maps as spatial priors to enhance sensing-assisted communication.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on ROS may limit integration with non-ROS-compatible tools.
idea_spark.inspiration: Deep integration of robotics and wireless communication simulation for end-to-end reproducibility.

papers.title: Weakly Supervised Segmentation as Semantic-Based Regularization
papers.summary: Weakly supervised semantic segmentation (WSSS) trains dense pixel-level segmentation models from partial or coarse annotations such as bounding boxes, scribbles, or image-level tags. While recent work leverages foundation models such as the Segment Anything Model (SAM) to generate pseudo-labels, these approaches typically depend on heuristic prompt choices and offer limited ways to incorporate prior knowledge or heterogeneous labels. We address this gap by taking a neurosymbolic perspective: integrating differentiable fuzzy logic with deep segmentation models. Weak annotations and domain-specific priors are unified as continuous logical constraints that fine-tune SAM under weak supervision. The refined foundation model then produces improved pseudo-labels, from which we train a second-stage prompt-free segmentation model. Experiments on Pascal VOC 2012 and the REFUGE2 optic disc/cup segmentation dataset show that our logic-guided fine-tuning yields higher-quality pseudo-labels, leading to state-of-the-art segmentation accuracy that often exceeds densely supervised baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出一种神经符号方法，将可微模糊逻辑与SAM结合，在弱监督下通过逻辑约束微调SAM生成高质量伪标签，进而训练无提示分割模型，在Pascal VOC 2012和REFUGE2上达到SOTA。
ai.motivation: 现有弱监督分割依赖启发式提示，难以融入先验知识和异构标签。
ai.method: 将弱标注和领域先验统一为连续逻辑约束，微调SAM；再用改进的伪标签训练第二阶段无提示分割模型。
ai.result: 逻辑引导微调生成更高质量伪标签，分割精度超越全监督基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种将领域知识融入弱监督分割的通用框架，减少对密集标注的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 将模糊逻辑规则（如空间关系、形状先验）编码为可微损失，引导基础模型适应特定领域。
idea_spark.risk: 逻辑规则设计依赖专家知识，复杂场景下规则可能不完整或冲突。
idea_spark.inspiration: 神经符号结合为弱监督学习提供新范式，未来可探索自动规则学习或与大型语言模型交互生成规则。

idea_spark.inspiration: 神经符号结合为弱监督学习提供新范式，未来可探索自动规则学习或与大型语言模型交互生成规则。

en.tldr: Proposes a neurosymbolic approach integrating differentiable fuzzy logic with SAM, fine-tuning it under weak supervision via logical constraints to produce high-quality pseudo-labels, then training a prompt-free segmentation model, achieving SOTA on Pascal VOC 2012 and REFUGE2.
en.motivation: Existing WSSS methods rely on heuristic prompts and offer limited ways to incorporate prior knowledge or heterogeneous labels.
en.method: Unify weak annotations and domain priors as continuous logical constraints to fine-tune SAM; then use refined pseudo-labels to train a second-stage prompt-free segmentation model.
en.result: Logic-guided fine-tuning yields higher-quality pseudo-labels, leading to segmentation accuracy that often exceeds densely supervised baselines.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general framework to incorporate domain knowledge into weakly supervised segmentation, reducing reliance on dense annotations.

idea_spark.idea: Encode fuzzy logic rules (e.g., spatial relations, shape priors) as differentiable losses to guide foundation model adaptation to specific domains.
idea_spark.risk: Logic rule design relies on expert knowledge; rules may be incomplete or conflicting in complex scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Neurosymbolic combination offers a new paradigm for weakly supervised learning; future work could explore automatic rule learning or interaction with large language models to generate rules.

papers.title: AssemblyBench: Physics-Aware Assembly of Complex Industrial Objects
papers.summary: Assembling objects from parts requires understanding multimodal instructions, linking them to 3D components, and predicting physically plausible 6-DoF motions for each assembly step. Existing datasets focus on simplified scenarios, overlooking shape complexities and assembly trajectories in industrial assemblies. We introduce AssemblyBench, a synthetic dataset of 2,789 industrial objects with multimodal instruction manuals, corresponding 3D part models, and part assembly trajectories. We also propose a transformer-based model, AssemblyDyno, which uses the instructional manual and the 3D shape of each part to jointly predict assembly order and part assembly trajectories. AssemblyDyno outperforms prior works in both assembly pose estimation and trajectory feasibility, where the latter is evaluated by our physics-based simulations.

ai.tldr: 提出AssemblyBench数据集和AssemblyDyno模型，用于复杂工业物体的物理感知装配。
ai.motivation: 现有装配数据集过于简化，忽略了工业装配中的形状复杂性和轨迹。
ai.method: 构建包含2789个工业物体及多模态说明手册的数据集，并设计基于Transformer的模型联合预测装配顺序和部件轨迹。
ai.result: 在装配姿态估计和轨迹可行性上优于先前工作，后者通过物理仿真评估。
ai.help_to_user: 为工业装配自动化提供基准数据和模型参考。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理仿真反馈在线优化装配轨迹。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实场景的域差异可能影响泛化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用物理仿真作为训练信号，提升模型对物理约束的感知。

en.method: Constructs a dataset of 2,789 industrial objects with multimodal manuals, and proposes a transformer model to jointly predict assembly order and part trajectories.
en.result: Outperforms prior work in both pose estimation and trajectory feasibility, the latter evaluated via physics simulation.
en.help_to_user: Provides benchmark data and model reference for industrial assembly automation.

idea_spark.risk: Domain gap between synthetic and real data may hinder generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use physics simulation as training signal to enhance physical constraint awareness.

papers.title: SECOND-Grasp: Semantic Contact-guided Dexterous Grasping
papers.summary: Achieving reliable robotic manipulation, such as dexterous grasping, requires a synergy between physically stable interactions and semantic task guidance, yet these objectives are often treated as separate, disjoint goals. In this paper, we investigate how to integrate dexterous grasping techniques, i.e., physically stable grasps for object lifting and language-guided grasp generation, to achieve both physical stability and semantic understanding. To this end, we propose SECOND-Grasp (SEmantic CONtact-guided Dexterous Grasping), a unified framework that enables robotic hands to dynamically adjust grasping strategies based on semantic reasoning while ensuring physical feasibility. We begin by obtaining coarse contact proposals through vision-language reasoning to infer where contacts should occur based on object properties, followed by segmentation to localize these regions across views. To further ensure consistency across multiple viewpoints, we introduce Semantic-Geometric Consistency Refinement (SGCR), which refines initial contact predictions by enforcing semantic consistency across views and removing geometrically invalid regions, yielding reliable 3D contact maps. Then, we derive a feasible hand pose for each contact map via inverse kinematics, generating a supervision signal for policy learning. Our approach, trained on DexGraspNet, consistently outperforms baselines in lifting success rate on both seen and unseen categories, achieving 98.2% and 97.7%, respectively, while also improving intent-aware grasping by 12.8% and 26.2%. We further show promising results on additional datasets and robotic hands, including Shadow Hand and Allegro Hand.

ai.tldr: 提出SECOND-Grasp框架，融合视觉语言推理与物理稳定性，实现语义引导的灵巧抓取。
ai.motivation: 现有灵巧抓取方法常将物理稳定性和语义任务指导分离，缺乏统一框架。
ai.method: 通过视觉语言推理生成粗略接触提议，经语义几何一致性精炼得到可靠3D接触图，再通过逆运动学推导手部姿态并训练策略。
ai.result: 在DexGraspNet上，已知和未知类别提升成功率分别达98.2%和97.7%，意图感知抓取提升12.8%和26.2%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将语义理解融入灵巧抓取，提升机器人操作的任务适应性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用大语言模型直接生成接触约束，替代逆运动学求解。
idea_spark.risk: 视觉语言推理可能产生不准确的接触提议，影响物理可行性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将语义推理与几何约束结合，启发更通用的机器人操作框架。

en.tldr: Proposes SECOND-Grasp, a unified framework integrating vision-language reasoning and physical stability for semantic-guided dexterous grasping.
en.motivation: Existing dexterous grasping methods often treat physical stability and semantic task guidance as separate objectives, lacking a unified approach.
en.method: Generates coarse contact proposals via vision-language reasoning, refines them with semantic-geometric consistency to obtain reliable 3D contact maps, then derives hand poses via inverse kinematics for policy training.
en.result: Achieves 98.2% and 97.7% lifting success on seen and unseen categories on DexGraspNet, and improves intent-aware grasping by 12.8% and 26.2%.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to integrate semantic understanding into dexterous grasping for improved task adaptability in robotic manipulation.

idea_spark.idea: Use large language models to directly generate contact constraints, bypassing inverse kinematics.
idea_spark.risk: Vision-language reasoning may produce inaccurate contact proposals, compromising physical feasibility.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining semantic reasoning with geometric constraints inspires more general robotic manipulation frameworks.

papers.title: STAR: Semantic-Temporal Adaptive Representation Learning for Few-Shot Action Recognition
papers.summary: Few-shot action recognition (FSAR) requires models to generalize to novel action categories from only a handful of annotated samples. Despite progress with vision-language models, existing approaches still suffer from semantic-temporal misalignment, where static textual prompts fail to capture decisive visual cues that appear sparsely across sequences, and from inadequate modeling of multi-scale temporal dynamics, as short-term discriminative cues and long-range dependencies are often either oversmoothed or fragmented. To address these challenges, we propose Semantic Temporal Adaptive Representation Learning (STAR), a unified framework, consisting of a semantic-alignment component and a temporal-aware component, effectively bridging the semantic and temporal gaps and transferring the sequence modeling capability of Mamba into the FSAR. The semantic alignment module introduces a Temporal Semantic Attention (TSA) mechanism, which performs frame-level cross-modal alignment with textual cues, ensuring fine-grained semantic-temporal consistency. The temporal-aware module incorporates a Semantic Temporal Prototype Refiner (STPR) that integrates semantic-guided Mamba blocks with multi-frequency temporal sampling and bidirectional state-space refinement, yielding semantically aligned prototypes with enhanced discriminative fidelity and temporal consistency. Furthermore, temporally dependent class descriptors derived from large language models (LLMs) provide long-range semantic guidance. Extensive experiments on five FSAR benchmarks demonstrate the consistent superiority of STAR over state-of-the-art methods. For instance, STAR achieves up to 8.1% and 6.7% gains on the SSv2-Full and SSv2-Small datasets under the 1-shot setting, and 7.3% on HMDB51, validating its effectiveness under limited supervision. The code is available at https://github.com/HongliLiu1/STAR-main.

ai.tldr: 提出STAR框架，通过语义-时间自适应表示学习解决少样本动作识别中的语义-时间错配和多尺度时间动态建模问题。
ai.motivation: 现有方法存在静态文本提示无法捕捉序列中稀疏关键视觉线索的语义-时间错配，以及短期判别线索与长期依赖建模不足的问题。
ai.method: 包含语义对齐模块（Temporal Semantic Attention实现帧级跨模态对齐）和时间感知模块（Semantic Temporal Prototype Refiner结合语义引导Mamba块、多频时间采样和双向状态空间细化），并利用LLM生成时间依赖的类别描述提供长期语义指导。
ai.result: 在五个少样本动作识别基准上超越现有方法，例如在SSv2-Full和SSv2-Small的1-shot设置下分别提升8.1%和6.7%，在HMDB51上提升7.3%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种统一的少样本动作识别框架，可有效利用视觉-语言模型和序列建模能力，适用于标注样本稀缺的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将Mamba的状态空间模型与语义引导结合，用于细粒度原型细化，可能启发更高效的时序特征学习。
idea_spark.risk: 对LLM生成的类别描述质量敏感，若描述不准确可能引入噪声；Mamba模块的计算开销需评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM生成时间依赖描述来提供长期语义指导，可启发其他少样本学习任务中利用外部知识增强表示的方法。

zh.motivation: 现有方法存在静态文本提示无法捕捉序列中稀疏关键视觉线索的语义-时间错配，以及短期判别线索与长期依赖建模不足的问题。
zh.method: 包含语义对齐模块（Temporal Semantic Attention实现帧级跨模态对齐）和时间感知模块（Semantic Temporal Prototype Refiner结合语义引导Mamba块、多频时间采样和双向状态空间细化），并利用LLM生成时间依赖的类别描述提供长期语义指导。
zh.result: 在五个少样本动作识别基准上超越现有方法，例如在SSv2-Full和SSv2-Small的1-shot设置下分别提升8.1%和6.7%，在HMDB51上提升7.3%。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种统一的少样本动作识别框架，可有效利用视觉-语言模型和序列建模能力，适用于标注样本稀缺的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将Mamba的状态空间模型与语义引导结合，用于细粒度原型细化，可能启发更高效的时序特征学习。
idea_spark.risk: 对LLM生成的类别描述质量敏感，若描述不准确可能引入噪声；Mamba模块的计算开销需评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM生成时间依赖描述来提供长期语义指导，可启发其他少样本学习任务中利用外部知识增强表示的方法。

en.tldr: Proposes STAR, a unified framework for few-shot action recognition that addresses semantic-temporal misalignment and multi-scale temporal dynamics via semantic alignment and temporal-aware components.
en.motivation: Existing methods suffer from semantic-temporal misalignment where static text prompts miss sparse key visual cues, and inadequate modeling of short-term discriminative cues and long-range dependencies.
en.method: Includes a semantic alignment module (Temporal Semantic Attention for frame-level cross-modal alignment) and a temporal-aware module (Semantic Temporal Prototype Refiner with semantic-guided Mamba blocks, multi-frequency temporal sampling, and bidirectional state-space refinement), plus LLM-generated temporally dependent class descriptors for long-range guidance.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art on five FSAR benchmarks, e.g., 8.1% and 6.7% gains on SSv2-Full and SSv2-Small under 1-shot, and 7.3% on HMDB51.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified framework for few-shot action recognition that effectively leverages vision-language models and sequence modeling, suitable for scenarios with limited annotations.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Mamba's state-space model with semantic guidance for fine-grained prototype refinement may inspire more efficient temporal feature learning.
idea_spark.risk: Sensitive to the quality of LLM-generated class descriptors; inaccurate descriptions may introduce noise. Computational cost of Mamba modules needs evaluation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using LLMs to generate temporally dependent descriptors for long-range semantic guidance can inspire methods that leverage external knowledge in other few-shot learning tasks.

keywords_raw: large language models llms for class descriptors

papers.title: Edit-level Majority Voting Mitigates Over-Correction in LLM-based Grammatical Error Correction
papers.summary: Grammatical error correction using large language models often suffers from the over-correction issue. To mitigate this, we propose a training-free inference method that performs edit-level majority voting over multiple candidates generated by a single model, without requiring model modifications or additional training. Across nine benchmarks covering English, Czech, German, Ukrainian, Korean, Hindi, and Romanian, the proposed method outperforms both greedy and MBR decoding in most cases. Moreover, it yields stable correction quality regardless of the instruction prompts used. We release two repository supporting GEC datasets loading and LLM inference.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于编辑级多数投票的训练无关推理方法，有效缓解大语言模型在语法纠错中的过度纠正问题。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型在语法纠错中常过度纠正，现有方法需修改模型或额外训练，缺乏轻量级解决方案。
ai.method: 对单个模型生成的多个候选结果进行编辑级多数投票，无需训练或模型改动。
ai.result: 在英语、捷克语等7种语言的9个基准上，多数情况下优于贪婪解码和MBR解码，且对指令提示不敏感。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的推理增强方法，可提升语法纠错系统的准确性和稳定性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将编辑级投票与置信度校准结合，或用于多模型集成场景。
idea_spark.risk: 对长文本或高噪声输入，多数投票可能引入偏差，需验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从集成学习角度，将解码过程视为弱分类器组合，启发更高效的投票机制设计。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于编辑级多数投票的训练无关推理方法，有效缓解大语言模型在语法纠错中的过度纠正问题。
zh.motivation: 大语言模型在语法纠错中常过度纠正，现有方法需修改模型或额外训练，缺乏轻量级解决方案。
zh.method: 对单个模型生成的多个候选结果进行编辑级多数投票，无需训练或模型改动。
zh.result: 在英语、捷克语等7种语言的9个基准上，多数情况下优于贪婪解码和MBR解码，且对指令提示不敏感。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的推理增强方法，可提升语法纠错系统的准确性和稳定性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将编辑级投票与置信度校准结合，或用于多模型集成场景。
idea_spark.risk: 对长文本或高噪声输入，多数投票可能引入偏差，需验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从集成学习角度，将解码过程视为弱分类器组合，启发更高效的投票机制设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a training-free inference method using edit-level majority voting to mitigate over-correction in LLM-based grammatical error correction.
en.motivation: LLMs often over-correct in GEC; existing solutions require model modifications or extra training, lacking a lightweight approach.
en.method: Performs edit-level majority voting over multiple candidates from a single model, without training or model changes.
en.result: Outperforms greedy and MBR decoding on 9 benchmarks across 7 languages, with stable quality across instruction prompts.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play inference enhancement that improves accuracy and stability of GEC systems.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining edit-level voting with confidence calibration or applying it in multi-model ensemble settings.
idea_spark.risk: For long texts or high-noise inputs, majority voting may introduce bias; robustness needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: From ensemble learning perspective, treating decoding as weak classifier combination inspires more efficient voting mechanisms.

papers.title: Fine-tuning with Hierarchical Prompting for Robust Propaganda Classification Across Annotation Schemas
papers.summary: Propaganda detection in social media is challenging due to noisy, short texts and low annotation agreements. We introduce a new intent-focused taxonomy of propaganda techniques and compare it against an established, higher-agreement schema. Along three dimensions (model portfolio, schema effects, and prompting strategy) we evaluate the taxonomies as a classification task with the help of four language models (GPT-4.1-nano, Phi-4 14B, Qwen2.5-14B, Qwen3-14B). Our results show that fine-tuning is essential, since it transforms weak zero-shot baselines into competitive systems and reveals methodological differences that are hidden using base models. Across schemas, the Qwen models achieve the strongest overall performance, and Phi-4 14B consistently outperforms GPT-4.1-nano. Our hierarchical prompting method (HiPP), which predicts fine-grained techniques before aggregating them, is especially beneficial after fine-tuning and on the more ambiguous, low-agreement taxonomy, while remaining competitive on the simpler schema. The HQP dataset, annotated with the new intent-based labels, provides a richer lens on propaganda's strategic goals and a challenging benchmark for future work on robust, real-world detection.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于层次提示的微调方法，用于跨不同标注模式的鲁棒性宣传分类。
ai.motivation: 社交媒体中的宣传检测面临噪声大、文本短、标注一致性低等挑战，现有方法难以鲁棒地处理不同标注模式。
ai.method: 引入意图导向的宣传技术分类体系，并对比已有高一致性模式；沿模型组合、模式效应和提示策略三个维度，使用四种语言模型评估；提出层次提示方法（HiPP），先预测细粒度技术再聚合。
ai.result: 微调至关重要，能将弱零样本基线提升为竞争系统；Qwen模型整体最强，Phi-4 14B持续优于GPT-4.1-nano；HiPP在微调后和低一致性模式上尤其有效。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过层次提示和微调提升宣传分类的鲁棒性，并提供了新的数据集和基准。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将HiPP应用于多标签分类或跨领域迁移学习，以增强模型对噪声数据的适应性。
idea_spark.risk: 层次提示可能增加计算开销，且在简单模式上优势不明显。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类逐层推理的启发，将复杂分类分解为子任务再整合。

zh.motivation: 社交媒体中的宣传检测面临噪声大、文本短、标注一致性低等挑战，现有方法难以鲁棒地处理不同标注模式。

en.tldr: Proposes fine-tuning with hierarchical prompting for robust propaganda classification across different annotation schemas.
en.motivation: Propaganda detection in social media suffers from noisy short texts and low annotation agreement, making it challenging to handle diverse annotation schemas robustly.
en.method: Introduces an intent-focused taxonomy of propaganda techniques and compares it with an established schema; evaluates four language models along model portfolio, schema effects, and prompting strategy; proposes Hierarchical Prompting (HiPP) that predicts fine-grained techniques before aggregating them.
en.result: Fine-tuning is essential, transforming weak zero-shot baselines into competitive systems; Qwen models achieve strongest overall performance, Phi-4 14B consistently outperforms GPT-4.1-nano; HiPP is especially beneficial after fine-tuning and on the low-agreement taxonomy.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how hierarchical prompting and fine-tuning improve robustness in propaganda classification, and provides a new dataset and benchmark.

idea_spark.idea: Explore applying HiPP to multi-label classification or cross-domain transfer learning to enhance model adaptability to noisy data.
idea_spark.risk: Hierarchical prompting may increase computational cost and show limited advantage on simpler schemas.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human hierarchical reasoning, decomposing complex classification into subtasks before integration.

papers.title: EgoForce: Robust Online Egocentric Motion Reconstruction via Diffusion Forcing
papers.summary: With recent advances in embodied agents and AR devices, egocentric observations are readily available as input for real-world interactive online applications. However, egocentric viewpoints can only sporadically observe hands, in addition to the estimated head trajectory. We propose EgoForce, an online framework for reconstructing long-term full-body motion from noisy egocentric input. While existing generative frameworks can robustly handle noisy and sparse measurements, they assume a fixed-length observation window is available and are thus not suitable for real-time applications. Faster inference often relies on autoregressive prediction, sacrificing robustness. In contrast, we adopt a diffusion-based method with a temporally asymmetric noise schedule inspired by Diffusion Forcing. Specifically, our approach models temporally evolving uncertainty and incrementally denoises states as new streaming observations arrive. Combined with a noise-robust imputation strategy, EgoForce progressively generates stable and coherent full-body motion under strict causal constraints. Experiments demonstrate that our online framework outperforms existing online and offline methods, enabling long-horizon, full-body motion reconstruction in challenging egocentric scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出EgoForce，一种基于扩散强迫的在线框架，从嘈杂的自我中心输入中重建长期全身运动。
ai.motivation: 现有生成方法假设固定长度观测窗口，不适合实时应用；自回归预测虽快但鲁棒性差。
ai.method: 采用时间不对称噪声调度的扩散方法，随新流观测逐步去噪，结合噪声鲁棒插值策略，在严格因果约束下生成稳定连贯的全身运动。
ai.result: 在挑战性自我中心场景中，在线框架优于现有在线和离线方法，实现长时程全身运动重建。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用扩散模型实现实时、鲁棒的全身运动重建，适用于AR/VR和具身智能体。

idea_spark.idea: 将扩散强迫与因果注意力结合，进一步处理部分遮挡或缺失的观测。
idea_spark.risk: 实时性可能受扩散步数限制，需平衡质量与延迟。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受扩散强迫启发，利用时间不确定性建模实现因果在线去噪。

en.tldr: Proposes EgoForce, an online framework based on diffusion forcing for reconstructing long-term full-body motion from noisy egocentric input.
en.motivation: Existing generative methods assume fixed-length observation windows, unsuitable for real-time applications; autoregressive prediction is fast but lacks robustness.
en.method: Adopts a diffusion method with temporally asymmetric noise schedule, incrementally denoising states as streaming observations arrive, combined with a noise-robust imputation strategy under strict causal constraints.
en.result: Outperforms existing online and offline methods in challenging egocentric scenarios, enabling long-horizon full-body motion reconstruction.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to leverage diffusion models for real-time, robust full-body motion reconstruction, applicable to AR/VR and embodied agents.

idea_spark.idea: Combine diffusion forcing with causal attention to handle partially occluded or missing observations.
idea_spark.risk: Real-time performance may be limited by the number of diffusion steps, requiring a trade-off between quality and latency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by diffusion forcing, using temporal uncertainty modeling for causal online denoising.

papers.title: DirectTryOn: One-Step Virtual Try-On via Straightened Conditional Transport
papers.summary: Recent diffusion- and flow-based VTON methods achieve strong results with pretrained generative models, but their reliance on multi-step sampling incurs high inference cost, while existing acceleration methods largely overlook the intrinsic structure of the try-on task. In this paper, we highlight a key observation: VTON outputs are highly constrained by the conditional inputs, suggesting that the conditional sampling trajectory can be much straighter than that in general image generation, making one-step generation a natural solution. However, limited task-specific data makes training from scratch impractical, forcing existing methods to fine-tune pretrained models whose objectives do not encourage such straight conditional trajectories. Thus, the deviation from an ideal straight path mainly comes from the mismatch between pretrained base models and the conditional nature of try-on generation, rather than from the task itself. Motivated by this insight, we encourage straighter VTON sampling trajectories through three targeted modifications: pure conditional transport, a garment preservation loss, and a self consistency loss. We further introduce a one-step distillation stage. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with one-step sampling, establishing a new standard for efficient and high-quality VTON.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于直线化条件传输的单步虚拟试穿方法，通过三项针对性修改实现高质量单步采样。
ai.motivation: 现有扩散和流模型虚拟试穿方法依赖多步采样，推理成本高，且加速方法未利用试穿任务的条件约束特性。
ai.method: 通过纯条件传输、衣物保留损失和自一致性损失鼓励直线化采样轨迹，并引入单步蒸馏阶段。
ai.result: 在单步采样下达到最先进性能，建立高效高质量虚拟试穿新标准。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速生成逼真的虚拟试穿图像，降低计算资源需求。

idea_spark.idea: 利用任务固有的条件约束特性，设计定制化损失函数和蒸馏策略，实现单步生成。
idea_spark.risk: 可能对复杂场景或罕见衣物类型泛化不足，需更多数据或正则化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从任务结构出发优化生成轨迹，而非通用加速方法，启发领域特定高效生成模型设计。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于直线化条件传输的单步虚拟试穿方法，通过三项针对性修改实现高质量单步采样。

en.tldr: Proposes a one-step virtual try-on method via straightened conditional transport, achieving high quality with three targeted modifications.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion and flow-based VTON methods rely on multi-step sampling with high inference cost, and acceleration methods overlook the conditional nature of the task.
en.method: Encourages straighter sampling trajectories via pure conditional transport, garment preservation loss, and self-consistency loss, plus a one-step distillation stage.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance with one-step sampling, setting a new standard for efficient and high-quality VTON.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast generation of realistic virtual try-on images with reduced computational cost.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage task-specific conditional constraints to design customized losses and distillation for one-step generation.
idea_spark.risk: May generalize poorly to complex scenes or rare garment types, requiring more data or regularization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Optimizing generation trajectories from task structure rather than generic acceleration inspires domain-specific efficient generative models.

papers.title: PoisonCap: Efficient Hierarchical Temporal Safety for CHERI
papers.summary: In this paper, we present PoisonCap: scalable temporal safety with strict use-after-free protection and initialisation safety for CHERI systems. Efficient memory safety is an increasing priority for programming languages, operating systems, and hardware designs, and CHERI is a leading hardware/software system that provides native spatial safety and a foundation for temporal memory safety. Cornucopia Reloaded, the current state-of-the-art CHERI temporal safety solution, provides use-after-reallocation safety instead of stronger use-after-free safety, and is not able to enforce initialisation safety. We show that a new 'poison' capability format can be used to enforce strict use-after-free and initialisation safety, and also to communicate memory state to the microarchitecture for efficient cache management of quarantined memory. We enable elegant delegation of memory poisoning privilege using capability bounds to allow nested allocators to enforce safety on their consumers without disturbing upstream allocators. PoisonCap can replace the Cornucopia shadow bitmap, and also automatically zeros memory on reallocation, or optionally traps on read-before-write to enforce initialisation safety. As a result, it incurs no fundamental overhead relative to a Cornucopia baseline that zeros before reallocation, strengthening CHERI temporal safety without performance overhead.

ai.tldr: 提出PoisonCap，一种针对CHERI系统的高效分层时间安全机制，提供严格的释放后使用保护和初始化安全。
ai.motivation: 现有CHERI时间安全方案Cornucopia Reloaded仅提供重分配后使用安全，无法实现更强的释放后使用安全和初始化安全。
ai.method: 设计新的“毒化”能力格式，通过能力边界优雅地委派内存毒化权限，支持嵌套分配器在不干扰上游分配器的情况下强制执行安全策略。
ai.result: PoisonCap在零化重分配基线基础上无额外性能开销，同时增强了CHERI时间安全性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在CHERI架构中实现更严格的内存时间安全，并了解其性能优势。

idea_spark.idea: 利用能力边界实现分层安全委派，使不同层级的分配器能独立管理内存安全。
idea_spark.risk: 毒化能力格式可能增加硬件复杂度，且需确保委派机制本身不被绕过。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从操作系统权限委派和硬件缓存管理中获得灵感，将内存状态信息传递给微架构。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从操作系统权限委派和硬件缓存管理中获得灵感，将内存状态信息传递给微架构。

en.tldr: PoisonCap provides efficient hierarchical temporal safety for CHERI systems, enforcing strict use-after-free and initialization safety.
en.motivation: Current CHERI temporal safety solution (Cornucopia Reloaded) only offers use-after-reallocation safety and cannot enforce initialization safety.
en.method: A novel poison capability format is designed, enabling delegation of memory poisoning privilege via capability bounds, allowing nested allocators to enforce safety without disturbing upstream allocators.
en.result: PoisonCap incurs no fundamental overhead compared to a Cornucopia baseline that zeros before reallocation, while strengthening CHERI temporal safety.
en.help_to_user: Understand how to achieve stricter temporal memory safety on CHERI with no performance penalty.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage capability bounds for hierarchical safety delegation, enabling independent memory safety management across allocator layers.
idea_spark.risk: The poison capability format may increase hardware complexity, and the delegation mechanism must be robust against bypass attacks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by OS privilege delegation and hardware cache management, communicating memory state to the microarchitecture.

papers.title: Unitary invariance of Connes spectral distances of quantum states
papers.summary: In this paper, we study the properties of Connes spectral distances between quantum states under unitary transformations. We mainly focus on spectral triples with matrix algebras acting on finite dimensional Hilbert spaces via some linear representations. We derive some elementary properties of the Connes spectral distances and optimal elements. We prove that there are some finite spectral triples in which the Lipschitz seminorms are equal to the operator norms. We also explicitly construct some spectral triples in which the Connes spectral distances between quantum states are exactly the quantum trace distances. These results and concrete examples are significant for studies of geometric structures of finite spectral triples and mathematical relations of qubits and other quantum states in the framework of noncommutative geometry.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了量子态在酉变换下Connes谱距离的性质，证明了在某些有限谱三元组中Lipschitz半范数等于算子范数，并构造了谱距离等于量子迹距离的例子。
ai.motivation: 探索非交换几何框架下量子态几何结构，特别是酉变换对Connes谱距离的影响。
ai.method: 基于有限维Hilbert空间上矩阵代数的谱三元组，推导谱距离和最优元的基本性质，并构造具体例子。
ai.result: 发现某些谱三元组中Lipschitz半范数与算子范数相等，且谱距离可精确等于量子迹距离。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解非交换几何中量子态距离的酉不变性，以及谱三元组与量子信息度量的联系。

idea_spark.idea: 利用谱三元组构造量子态距离的几何解释，可能用于量子纠错码或量子态区分。
idea_spark.risk: 有限维谱三元组的构造可能无法直接推广到无限维系统。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将非交换几何的谱距离与量子迹距离的等价性，启发设计新的量子态距离度量。
